q: Who was the first Indian to win the World Amateur Billiards title?
subject: currentaff
exp: Wilson Lionel Garton Jones was a professional billiards player from India. He made three unsuccessful attempts at the world title, starting in 1951 in London, followed by the world championships in Calcutta in 1952 and Sydney in 1954. However, in 1958, when the world championship returned to Calcutta, Jones found his form. At the Great Eastern Hotel in Calcutta, he achieved a major upset in billiards by becoming the first Asian to win the championship. Jones holds the distinction of being the first Indian to become a World Champion in any sport. Hence, the correct answer is option C.

q: Consider the following statements regarding the AI Supercomputer 'AIRAWAT' and its significance in the context of India's AI endeavors: 1. The AI Research Analytics and Knowledge Dissemination Platform (AIRAWAT) is a Proof of Concept (PoC) with a peak compute capacity of 410 AI Petaflops Mixed Precision, integrated with PARAM Siddhi-AI, contributing to India's ambitious National Program on AI (NPAI). 3. MeitY has outlined plans to scale AIRAWAT to a compute capacity of 1,000 AI Petaflops Mixed Precision, aligning with Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi's vision of leveraging technology and AI for the welfare of citizens and socio- economic growth. Select the correct statement(s) from the options below:
subject: currentaff
exp: 1. Statement 1: AIRAWAT's ranking of 75th in the Top 500 Global Supercomputing List does position India as a notable player in AI supercomputing. This recognition highlights India's progress and capabilities in the field of high-performance computing and AI. 2. Statement 2: The AI Research Analytics and Knowledge Dissemination Platform (AIRAWAT) is indeed a Proof of Concept (PoC) with a peak compute capacity of 410 AI Petaflops Mixed Precision. This platform, integrated with PARAM Siddhi-AI, contributes significantly to India's National Program on AI (NPAI), showcasing the country's commitment to advancing AI research and development. 3. Statement 3: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has plans to scale AIRAWAT to a compute capacity of 1,000 AI Petaflops Mixed Precision. This aligns with Prime Minister Narendra Modi's vision of leveraging technology and AI for the welfare of citizens and socio-economic growth, reflecting a strategic national effort to harness the power of AI for broader development goals. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: Which of the following is the name of the woman robot astronaut that will fly into space ahead of ISRO’s “Gaganyaan” mission?

Vyommitra is a female robot designed for space travel by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO). Her name comes from the Sanskrit words Vyōma meaning "space" and Mitra meaning "friend". What will Vyommitra do? Vyommitra will accompany Indian astronauts on space missions and participate in uncrewed experimental missions before the crewed flights. She will also test spacecraft safety. When will Vyommitra fly? Vyommitra will take her first flight on the Gaganyaan G-2 unmanned mission in | | Defence 2025. Where was Vyommitra designed? Vyommitra was designed at the Vikram Sarabhai Space Center in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala. What is Gaganyaan? Gaganyaan is India's first human manned space flight, carrying Indian astronauts into space. Hence, the correct answer is option (C).

q: In which state was the Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) first launched?
subject: currentaff
exp: Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) is a Government sponsored scheme to provide highly subsidised food to millions of the poorest families. It was launched by the government on 25 December 2000 and first implemented in Rajasthan. About Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY): The Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) is a government program that provides subsidized food to the poorest families in India. It was launched in 2000 to help ensure food security. How does it work? The AAY scheme is part of the Targeted Public Distribution System (TPDS). Eligible families receive a unique Antyodaya Ration Card. The card allows them to buy food at subsidized rates from fair price shops. The AAY scheme provides 35 kg of food grains per month to each eligible family. This includes rice, wheat, and coarse grains. Who is eligible? The AAY scheme is for the poorest families in the country, as identified by the Below Poverty Line (BPL). How is it implemented? The Food Corporation of India supplies the food. The state government is responsible for transportation costs and other incidental charges. What are the benefits? The AAY scheme has helped reduce hunger and malnutrition. It has also helped improve the food security of the country. Hence, the correct answer is option (A).

q: Chinook is which of the following types of aircraft?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Chinook is a heavy-lift helicopter. It's used for transporting troops, cargo, and equipment, as well as for search and rescue, disaster relief, and more. How it's used: The U.S. Army uses the Chinook as its primary heavy troop and supply transport aircraft. The Chinook is used for medical evacuation, search and rescue, parachute drops, disaster relief, and aircraft recovery. The Indian Air Force uses the Chinook as a helicopter. How it's designed: The Chinook is a tandem-rotor helicopter with twin engines. It has a digital cockpit management system. It has a triple hook system for stability and carrying multiple external loads. It has a monolithic airframe with vibration reduction. History: The Chinook was developed by the American aerospace company Boeing | | Defence Vertol in the 1960s. It's now manufactured by Boeing Defense, Space & Security. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Which of the following tanks was not developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)?

Bhishma (T-90) – Not developed by DRDO; it is a Russian-origin T-90 tank that was imported and later assembled in India by the Ordnance Factory Board (OFB). Ajeya (T-72) – A modified version of the Soviet T-72 tank, assembled in India, but the modifications were not developed by DRDO. Zorawar – A light tank being developed by DRDO for high-altitude warfare, particularly for deployment in Ladakh. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Where was India's first Grain ATM inaugurated?

q: Which of the following ministries introduced the 'Rashtraparv' website?

Good Governance Day is observed every year since 2014 on the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee. The Rashtraparv platform provides users with information about national events organized by the Ministry of Defence, such as Republic Day, Beating Retreat Ceremony, and Independence Day. q: What is the theme for World Ozone Day 2024?
o: Caring for All Life Under the Sun
subject: currentaff
exp: The theme for World Ozone Day 2024 is "Montreal Protocol: Advancing Climate Action," highlighting the Protocol's vital role in both safeguarding the ozone layer and addressing climate change. The Montreal Protocol was signed in 1987 to phase out ozone-depleting substances (ODS) such as CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) and halons. These substances not only damage the ozone layer but are also potent greenhouse gases. By controlling them, the protocol indirectly mitigates global warming. The Kigali Amendment (2016) expanded the protocol’s scope to include the reduction of hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), which are major contributors to climate change. Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Which of the following is not an indigenous defense system or equipment of India?

Although some components are assembled or produced in India under license, it is not originally developed by India. Arjun: This is a fully indigenous main battle tank (MBT) developed by the DRDO. It is designed and manufactured in India. Zorawar: This is an indigenous light tank being developed by DRDO and Larsen & Toubro (L&T). It is designed for high-altitude warfare, particularly for deployment in Ladakh and Arunachal Pradesh. Zorawar is still under development but is fully indigenous in design and production. Hence the correct answer is option B.

subject: currentaff
exp: “End Polio: Every Child, Every Vaccine, Everywhere” — this theme emphasizes the urgent need to ensure that every child in every part of the world receives the polio vaccine so that the disease can be eradicated completely. Hence, the correct answer is option (A).

q: With reference to Aero India, Asia’s Largest Air Show, consider the following statements. 1. Aero India is an annual air show and aviation exhibition held in Bengaluru and is organised by the Defence Exhibition Organisation under the Department of Defence Production, Ministry of Defence. 2. Aero India 2025, the 15th edition of the event, had a broad theme, “The Runway to a Billion Opportunities”. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Statement 1: Incorrect Aero India is not an annual event. It is a biennial (held every two years) air show conducted in Bengaluru, Karnataka. It is organised by the Defence Exhibition Organisation (DEO) under the Department of Defence Production, Ministry of Defence, but the word annual makes the statement incorrect. Statement 2: Correct Aero India 2025 was the 15th edition of the event, and its theme was “The Runway to a Billion Opportunities.” Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: The 22nd ASEAN–India Summit held in 2025 declared the year 2026 as which of the following?
o: The ASEAN-India Year of Economic Collaboration
The ASEAN-India Year of Digital Connectivity
The ASEAN-India Year of Cultural Engagement
The ASEAN-India Year of Maritime Cooperation
subject: currentaff
exp: The 22nd ASEAN–India Summit held in 2025 declared the year 2026 as the ASEAN-India Year of Maritime Cooperation. This declaration was made to deepen collaboration between India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) in areas such as maritime security, trade, humanitarian assistance, disaster relief, and the blue economy, reflecting a stronger strategic partnership focused on maritime domains and regional stability. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: What percentage of posts in the Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA) must be reserved for women according to the recent Supreme Court directive?
subject: currentaff
exp: According to the recent Supreme Court directive, the percentage of posts in the Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA) that must be reserved for women is 33%. The Supreme Court mandated that at least one-third of the posts in the executive committee of the SCBA should be reserved for women, which translates to a minimum of 33%. Hence, the correct answer is option (C).

q: Which of the following countries is the manufacturer of Pilatus PC-7 aircraft?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Pilatus PC-7 Turbo Trainer is a low- wing tandem-seat training aircraft, designed and manufactured in Switzerland by Pilatus Aircraft. The aircraft is capable of all basic training functions, including aerobatics, instrument, tactical, and night flying. Hence, the correct answer is option (A).

q: Who won the United World Wrestling rising star of the Year 2023?
subject: currentaff
exp: The 19-year-old Antim Panghal from Haryana becomes the UWW Women's Rising Stars of the Year 2023 for her exceptional performances, including her two women's World Under-20 Championship titles (2022 and 2023) in the 53kg category. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: Who won the gold medal in the World Athletics Championships 2023?
subject: currentaff
exp: Neeraj Chopra won the gold medal in the men's javelin throw event at the 2023 World Athletics Championships with a throw of 88.17 meters. He became the first Indian athlete to win a gold medal at the World Championships in javelin throw. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Which fighter jet of the Indian Air Force successfully test-fired the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Sukhoi-30 MKI has been equipped to launch the BrahMos missile, specifically the air-launched variant known as BrahMos-A, allowing for long- range precision strikes against land and sea targets. The BrahMos missile, noted for its supersonic speed and precision, was fired from a Sukhoi fighter jet, demonstrating the IAF's capacity to deploy long-range BrahMos cruise missiles. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: National Space Day is celebrated on which of the following date?
subject: currentaff
exp: On 23 August 2023, the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) reached a significant milestone by successfully landing Chandrayaan-3's lander and rover on the Moon. Recognizing this achievement, Prime Minister Narendra Modi proclaimed August 23 as "National Space Day" in India. Hence, the correct answer is option (C).

q: Which international alliance is heavily involved in the Orion 23 military exercise alongside the Indian Air Force?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Indian Air Force's Rafale fighter jets will be participating in a military exercise in France called Orion 23, which involves thousands of NATO troops. The exercise aims to enhance military cooperation and interoperability among participating nations. The Golden Arrows squadron, part of the Western Air Command, is likely to provide the aircraft for the exercise. The exercise includes multiple dimensions, such as ground troops, warships, aircraft carriers, and combat aircraft. Hence, the correct answer is option (A).

q: Consider the following statements with reference to INS Jatayu: 1. INS Jatayu is the new naval base established by the Indian Navy in the Lakshadweep Islands. 2. It will serve as a naval detachment there, enhancing the Indian Navy's operational reach in the region. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Defence and Security Explanation: Naval Detachment Minicoy will be commissioned as INS Jatayu, an upgraded naval base by the Indian Navy to resolve to incrementally augment security infrastructure at the strategic Lakshadweep Islands. INS Jatayu will effectively be the country’s second naval base in Lakshadweep. India has had a naval detachment in Minicoy, the southernmost atoll of the Lakshwadeep archipelago, since the 1980s, The Navy’s first base on the islands, INS Dweeprakshak in Kavaratti, was commissioned in 2012. INS Jatayu is being commissioned days after the inauguration of an airstrip and a jetty that India has built on Agaléga, Mauritius. It will enhance the operational reach of the Navy in the Indian Ocean, a vital strategic imperative as China seeks to increase its footprint in the region. Hence, the correct answer is (C).

q: What is the theme for World Environment Day 2024?
o: Land Restoration, Desertification, and Drought Resilience
subject: currentaff
exp: The theme for World Environment Day 2024 is "Land restoration, desertification, and drought resilience". The slogan for 2024 is "Our land. Our future. World Environment Day is celebrated annually on June 5th, after it was established at the Stockholm Conference on the Human Environment in 1972. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: Which Indian brand sponsored the Netherlands men's cricket team for the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2024?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Indian brand that sponsored the Netherlands men's cricket team for the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2024 is Amul. Amul has a history of sponsoring cricket teams during ICC tournaments, including previous collaborations with the Netherlands national side. Hence, the correct answer is option (A).

q: In the best Hindi film category which film won the 66th national award in 2020?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Hindi film industry dominated the 66th National Film Awards, which were announced in New Delhi. AndhaDhun was named the best Hindi film of the year at the National Film Awards with Ayushmann Khurrana and Vicky Kaushal (Uri) sharing the best actor award. Keerthy Suresh won the best actress award (Mahanati). Hence, the correct answer is Option B i.e Andhadun.

q: The range of Agni - VI missile is ________.
subject: currentaff
exp: Agni-VI is an MIRV-capable intercontinental ballistic missile under development by the Defence Research and Development Organisation. Agni-VI will be a four-stage intercontinental ballistic missile, which is in the hardware development phase, after its design phase was completed. Agni-VI missile is likely to carry up to 10 MIRV warheads and will have a strike range of 11,000- 12,000 km. A senior DRDO scientist said that the new generation Agni-VI missile will be sleeker, easily transportable and would be readily deployed. It will have the capability to be launched from submarine and from land-based launchers. Hence, the correct answer is Option B i.e 11000 km - 12000 km.

q: The 7th edition of the bilateral air exercise between India and France concluded at Air Force Station, Jodhpur on 12th November 2022. What was the name of the exercise?
subject: currentaff
exp: The seventh edition of the bilateral air exercise between the Indian Air Force (IAF) and the French Air and Space Force (FASF), 'Exercise Garuda-VII' concluded at Air Force Station, Jodhpur on 12 November 2022. The FASF participated in the exercise with Rafale fighter aircraft and A-330 Multi Role Tanker Transport (MRTT) aircraft, while the IAF contingent comprised Su-30 MKI, Rafale, LCA 'Tejas' and Jaguar fighter aircraft. This fighter element was complemented by the IAF's Flight Refuelling Aircraft, AWACS and AEW&C, as well as Mi-17 helicopters and the newly inducted LCH 'Prachanda'. Exercise Garuda-VII provided the two Air Forces with the opportunity for professional interaction and sharing of operational knowledge and experience. With meticulous planning and execution of various phases of the exercise, personnel of the IAF and the FASF were exposed to realistic air combat simulations and the associated Combat Support Operations. Hence, the correct answer is Option A i.e Garuda.

q: In March 2023, Who is the Home Minister of India?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Minister of Home Affairs is the head of the Ministry of Home Affairs of the Government of India. One of the senior-most officers in the Union Cabinet, the chief responsibility of the home minister is the maintenance of the internal security of India; the country's large police force comes under its jurisdiction. Ever since the time of independent India's first Home Minister, Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the office has been seen as second in seniority only to the Prime Minister in the union cabinet. The current minister is Amit Shah of the Bharatiya Janata Party who has been in office since 31 May 2019. Hence, the correct answer is Option B i.e Amit Shah.

q: The joint naval exercise Varuna is between India and which of the following nations?
subject: currentaff
exp: The 21st Edition of the Bilateral Naval Exercise between India and France – Exercise Varuna commenced on the Western Seaboard. While the bilateral exercise between the two navies were initiated in 1993, it was christened as ‘VARUNA’ in 2001 and has become a hallmark of India – France strategic bilateral relationship. This edition will witness participation of indigenous guided missile stealth destroyer INS Chennai, guided missile frigate INS Teg, maritime patrol aircraft P- 8I and Dornier, integral helicopters and MiG29K fighter aircraft. The French Navy will be represented by the aircraft carrier Charles De Gaulle, frigates FS Forbin and Provence, support vessel FS Marne and maritime patrol aircraft Atlantique. It will witness advanced air defence exercises, tactical manoeuvres, surface firings, underway replenishment and other maritime operations. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e France.

q: Which of the following won the Vijay Hazare Trophy at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, Gujarat?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Vijay Hazare Trophy is an annual limited overs domestic cricket competition organised by the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI). It involves the state and union territory teams which take part in the Ranji Trophy. The tournament had been played at zonal level only until 2002–03 when it was expanded to become a national competition. The most successful team since expansion is Tamil Nadu who have won the tournament five times. Saurashtra beat Maharashtra by 5 wickets in the final to win the Vijay Hazare Trophy at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, Gujarat in 2023. Hence, the correct answer is Option A i.e Saurashtra.

q: In March 2023, Who is the Chief Minister of Gujarat?
subject: currentaff
exp: The current chief minister is Bhupendrabhai Patel of the BJP. He was elected for the post following the resignation of then incumbent Vijay Rupani, who was in the office since 7 August 2016. Narendra Modi holds the record for the longest-serving Chief Minister of Gujarat, with a remarkable tenure of twelve and a half years from 7th October 2001 to 22nd May 2014. Suresh Mehta served the shortest term as Chief Minister, lasting for only 334 days from 21st October 1995 to 19th September 1996. Anandiben Patel made history as the first woman Chief Minister of Gujarat, breaking gender barriers in politics. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e Bhupendra Patel.

q: Statue of Unity is located in which state?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Statue of Unity is the world's tallest statue, with a height of 182 metres ,located near Kevadia in the state of Gujarat, India. It depicts Indian statesman and independence activist Vallabhbhai Patel (1875–1950), who was the first deputy prime minister and home minister of independent India and an adherent of Mahatma Gandhi. Patel is highly respected for playing a significant role in the political integration of India. The statue is located in Gujarat on the Narmada River in the Kevadiya colony, facing the Sardar Sarovar Dam 100 kilometres (62 mi) southeast of the city of Vadodara. Hence, the correct answer is Option A i.e Gujarat.

q: Which of the following countries will host the 2023 women's T-20 Cricket World Cup?
subject: currentaff
exp: The 2023 ICC Women's T20 World Cup is scheduled to be the eighth edition of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup tournament. It is scheduled to be held in South Africa. The 2023 ICC Men's Cricket World cup will be the 13th edition of the men's Cricket World Cup, scheduled to be hosted by India during October and November 2023. This will be the first time the competition is held completely in India. Three previous editions were partially hosted there - 1987, 1996, and 2011. Chairman of ICC: Greg Barclay. CEO of ICC: Geoff Allardice Headquarters of ICC: Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e South Africa.

q: Which of the following country was the runner up of FIFA World Cup 2022?
subject: currentaff
exp: The correct answer is Option A i.e. France. Key Points Argentina won its third FIFA World Cup after defeating France in the final match in 2022. The 2022 World Cup was the first to take place in the Middle East after the FIFA awarded Qatar the right to host in 2010. Only eight countries had won any of the 21 previous World Cup tournaments. Italy and Germany have won FIFA World Cup four times, and Brazil has won it five times.

q: In which State is the Similipal Tiger Reserve Situated?
subject: currentaff
exp: Simlipal National Park is a national park and tiger reserve in the Mayurbhanj district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is part of the Mayurbhanj Elephant Reserve, which includes three protected areas, Similipal Tiger Reserve, Hadgarh Wildlife Sanctuary and Kuldiha Wildlife Sanctuary. Simlipal National Park derives its name from the abundance of red silk cotton trees growing in the area. The park is home to Bengal tiger, Asian elephant, gaur, and chausingha. This protected area is part of the UNESCO World Network of Biosphere Reserves since 2009. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e Odisha.

q: What was the name of the rover of Chandrayaan - 2?
subject: currentaff
exp: Chandrayaan-2 mission is a highly complex mission, which represents a significant technological leap compared to the previous missions of ISRO, which brought together an Orbiter, Lander and Rover with the goal of exploring south pole of the Moon. The Lander of Chandrayaan-2 was named Vikram after Dr Vikram A Sarabhai, the Father of the Indian Space Programme. It was designed to function for one lunar day, which is equivalent to about 14 Earth days. Chandrayaan-2’s Rover was a 6-wheeled robotic vehicle named as Pragyan, which translates to 'wisdom' in Sanskrit. Hence, the correct answer is Option A i.e Pragyan.

q: With which country does India conduct "Harimau Shakti" exercise?
subject: currentaff
exp: HARIMAU SHAKTI is an annual training event between Indian and Malaysian armies which has been conducted since 2012. The Malaysian Army contingent comprises troops from 5th Royal Battalion of Malaysian Army. The Indian contingent is being represented by a Battalion of THE RAJPUT REGIMENT. It is aimed at enhancing military capability for conduct of Multi Domain Operations in a sub conventional scenario. During the exercise, both contingents will establish a Joint Command Post & establish an integrated surveillance grid along with a Joint Surveillance Centre. The Exercise will also witness employment of Drones/UAVs & Helicopters. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e Malaysia.

q: Recently, who has been appointed by the Government as Chief of Defence Staff (CDS)?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Government of India has appointed Lt General Anil Chauhan (Retired) as the next Chief of Defence Staff (CDS). He will also function as a Secretary to the Department of Military Affairs. The CDS was created in 2019 for a dual role, as the most senior uniformed officer in rank (First among equals) and as a Secretary of the Department of Military Affairs. Since India’s first CDS General Bipin Rawat had died, the post has been vacant. The government amended the service rules of the armed forces to fill the vacancy. Hence, the correct asnwer is Option D i.e Anil Chauhan.

q: Which scheme has been approved by central government for recruitment of youth in armed forces?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Union Cabinet today approved an attractive recruitment scheme for Indian youth to serve in the Armed Forces. The scheme is called AGNIPATH and the youth selected under this scheme will be known as Agniveers. AGNIPATH allows patriotic and motivated youth to serve in the Armed Forces for a period of four years. The AGNIPATH scheme has been designed to enable a youthful profile of the Armed Forces. It will provide an opportunity to the youth who may be keen to don the uniform by attracting young talent from the society who are more in tune with contemporary technological trends and plough back skilled, disciplined and motivated manpower into the society. As for the Armed Forces, it will enhance the youthful profile of the Armed Forces and provide a fresh lease of ‘Josh’ and ‘Jazba’ whilst at the same time bring about a transformational shift towards a more tech savvy Armed Forces – which is indeed the need of the hour. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e AGNIPATH.

q: Who took oath as the 22 nd Governor of Maharashtra on 18 February
subject: currentaff
exp: Ramesh Bais took oath as the 22nd Governor of Maharashtra, replacing Bhagat Singh Koshyari. Bais was administered the oath of office and secrecy by Acting Chief Justice of the Bombay High Court S V Gangapurwala at the Raj Bhavan. Bais took oath in Marathi. The Governor is the Executive Head of the State within the meaning of Article 153 and 154 of the Constitution of India. He cannot be a member of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha. If he has been a member of either of the house, he should vacate the seat on his first day as Governor in the office. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e Ramesh Bias.

q: The Sethusamudram Project is a proposed shipping canal project that would connect the __________.
o: Andaman Sea and the Malacca Strait
Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea
Gulf of Mannar and the Palk Strait
subject: currentaff
exp: Andaman Sea and the Malacca Strait: The Andaman Sea is located to the east of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, while the Malacca Strait is a narrow stretch of water between the Malay Peninsula and the Indonesian island of Sumatra. Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea:The Bay of Bengal is located to the northeast of the Indian subcontinent, while the Arabian Sea is to the southwest. These two water bodies are already connected via the Indian mainland, and there is no proposal to connect them through a canal as part of the Sethusamudram Project. Indian mainland and Sri Lanka: This option refers to the Sethusamudram Project itself. It proposes to connect the Indian mainland's eastern coast (Gulf of Mannar) with the northern coast of Sri Lanka (Palk Strait) through a shipping canal. Gulf of Mannar and the Palk Strait: This is the correct answer for the Sethusamudram Project. The Gulf of Mannar is located between the southeastern tip of India (Tamil Nadu) and the western coast of Sri Lanka. The Palk Strait lies between the Indian mainland and the northern coast of Sri Lanka. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e. Gulf of Mannar and the Palk Strait.

q: Which Indian state hosted the FIH Men's Hockey World Cup 2023?
subject: currentaff
exp: Odisha, a state in eastern India, hosted the FIH Men's Hockey World Cup in 2023. This state has been actively promoting hockey and has become a hub for international hockey events, hosting major tournaments in recent years. The matches were played at two venues in Odisha: Kalinga Stadium, Bhubaneswar: This stadium is known for its passionate hockey fans and has hosted several international hockey events in the past, including the FIH Men's Hockey World Cup 2018. Biju Patnaik Hockey Stadium, Rourkela: This venue also hosted matches during the tournament. Hence, the correct asnwer is Option D i.e. Odisha.

q: Which of the following folk dance forms is associated with Assam?
subject: currentaff
exp: Nautanki: Nautanki is a folk theatre form popular in North India, particularly in Uttar Pradesh. Kathakali: Kathakali is a classical dance- drama form from Kerala, known for its elaborate costumes, makeup, and expressive movements. Bihu: Bihu is a traditional folk dance and festival of Assam, celebrated with great fervor and enthusiasm. It is associated with the agricultural cycle and marks different phases of the Assamese calendar. Bihu dance is characterized by energetic movements, rhythmic swaying, and the use of traditional Assamese attire. Garba: Garba is a folk dance from Gujarat, traditionally performed during Navratri, a Hindu festival celebrating Goddess Durga. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e. Bihu.

q: With which country India conducted a bilateral air exercise, "Veer Guardian 23", from January 16 to 26,2023 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: The inaugural edition of the bilateral air exercise 'Veer Guardian 2023' between the Indian Air Force (IAF) and Japan Air Self Defence Force (JASDF) concluded in Japan, on 26 January 2023. The JASDF participated in the exercise with its F-2 and F-15 aircraft, while the IAF contingent participated with the Su-30 MKI aircraft. The IAF fighter contingent was complemented by one IL-78 Flight Refueling Aircraft and two C-17 Globemaster strategic airlift transport aircraft. During the joint training spanning 16 days, the two Air Forces engaged in complex and comprehensive aerial manoeuvres in multiple simulated operational scenarios. Hence, the correct asnwer is Option B i.e. Japan.

q: "UJALA", the National LED programme has been recently lauched in which city of Madhya Pradesh?
subject: currentaff
exp: Unnat Jyoti by Affordable LEDs for All (UJALA) was a project to distribute efficient LED lighting, launched by Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh. The project is spearheaded by the Energy Efficiency Services Limited. In non-subsidized LED lamp distribution projects, this program is considered the world's largest. UJALA will not only help reduce consumers their electricity bills but also contribute to the energy security of India. Hence, the correct answer is Option A i.e. Bhopal.

q: Which state government has recently declared 10% reservation quota for Economically Backward Classes (EBCs) in general category?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Gujarat government announced a new 10% quota in jobs and college admissions for the economically backward among upper castes, in an attempt to defuse the agitation by the Patidar community. Families having an annual income of ₹ 6 lakh or below will be eligible to avail the reservation. That means a family having an income up to ₹ 50,000 per month can take the benefit of the scheme. In 1992, the Supreme Court set a ceiling of 50% for all types of reservation while ruling that the Mandal Commission’s recommendation giving reservations to OBCs was constitutionally valid. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e. Gujarat.

q: Bhitarkanika Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which state of India?
subject: currentaff
exp: Bhitarkanika National Park is a 145 km2 large national park in northeast Kendrapara district in Odisha in eastern India. It was designated on 16 September 1998 and obtained the status of a Ramsar site on 19 August 2002. The area is also been designated as the second Ramsar site of the State after the Chilika Lake. It is surrounded by Bhitarkanika Wildlife Sanctuary, which spread over 672 km2. Gahirmatha Beach and Marine Sanctuary are to the east, separating the swamp region and mangroves from the Bay of Bengal. The national park and wildlife sanctuary is inundated by the rivers Brahmani, Baitarani, Dhamra, and Pathsala. It hosts many mangrove species, and is the second largest mangrove ecosystem in India. The national park is home to Saltwater crocodile (Crocodylus porosus), Indian python, king cobra, black ibis, darters and many other species of flora and fauna. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e. Odisha.

q: Pradhan Mantri Jan-Dhan Yojana is related to ________.
subject: currentaff
exp: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY), the biggest financial inclusion initiative in the world, was announced by the Hon'ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi on 15th August 2014 from the ramparts of the Red Fort and launched by him on 28th August 2014 across the country. While launching the Yojana, the Prime Minister had described the occasion as a festival to celebrate the liberation of the poor from a vicious cycle. PMJDY is National Mission for Financial Inclusion to ensure access to financial services, namely, Banking/Savings & Deposit Accounts, Remittance, Credit, Insurance and Pension in an affordable manner. Guinness Book of World Records- External website that opens in a new window also recognised the achievements made under PMJDY. It gave the certificate stating that the "Most bank accounts opened in one week as part of the Financial Inclusion Campaign is 18,096,130 and was achieved by the Department of Financial Services, Government of India from 23rd to 29th August, 2014." Hence, the correct answer is Option B.

q: Which of the following was awarded with the Best Global Music Performance in Grammy Awards 2024?
subject: currentaff
exp: During the premiere ceremony of the 66th Annual Grammy Awards in Los Angeles, California, US, Zakir Hussain secured the Best Global Music Performance Grammy for his contribution to “Pashto”. Zakir Hussain Allarakha Qureshi (9 March 1951 – 15 December 2024) was an Indian tabla player, composer, percussionist, music producer, and film actor. Widely regarded as the greatest tabla player of his generation and one of the greatest percussionists of all time, he was known for bringing Indian classical music to a global audience. He was the eldest son of the tabla player Alla Rakha, and won four Grammy Awards. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e. Zakir Hussain.

q: Who has been crowned Miss International India 2025?
subject: currentaff
exp: Roosh Sindhu, a talented and ambitious young woman from Gujarat, India, has been crowned Miss International India 2025.

q: Which country's president was the Chief Guest at India's Republic Day Parade 2025 celebrations?
subject: currentaff
exp: India celebrated its 76th Republic Day with a colorful parade in New Delhi, showcasing military strength, with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto as the guest of honor. Indonesia was also India's chief guest on its first Republic Day in 1950. India and Indonesia signed pacts on maritime security, health, traditional medicine, and are working on a defense cooperation framework. This includes technology sharing for surface-to-surface missiles, air defense systems, and submarine construction. India welcomed Indonesia's decision to join the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure early. Hence, the correct answer is option (A).

q: Which cricketer announced his retirement from T20Is after winning the 2024 T20 World Cup?
subject: currentaff
exp: Following the lead of Indian skipper Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli, Ravindra Jadeja has announced his retirement from T20 Internationals after India's victory in the T20 World Cup 2024. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Which ministry implements the Midday Meal Scheme in India?
o: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
subject: currentaff
exp: Mid-day Meal Scheme was started in 1995 throughout India by the Ministry of Education. Mid-day Meal Scheme is a school meal program of the Government of India. Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Which is the lightest and smallest type of aircraft in India?
subject: currentaff
exp: HAL Tejas is India’s lightest and smallest fighter aircraft due to its compact design and extensive use of composite materials.

q: Which country has opened military service to women for the first time in 25 years?
subject: currentaff
exp: 1. Colombia has opened military service to women for the first time in 25 years. 2. A cohort of 1,296 women enlisted in Colombia's Army in February. 3. Recruits must live on military bases, earning a monthly stipend of about $75, with hopes of building a career in the armed forces. 4. The Colombian army currently has around 200,000 soldiers, with approximately 1% being women who previously joined through military universities or administrative roles. 5. The move to allow female enlistment comes as Colombia's congress debates a bill to eliminate compulsory military service for men. 6. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: When is the Indian Air Force day celebrated?
subject: currentaff
exp: Indian Air Force Day is celebrated on October 8 every year. Every year, the Air Force Day is celebrated at Hindon base in presence of IAF chief and senior officials of the three armed forces. These celebrations include an air display where the most crucial and vintage aircraft put up a magnificent show. The Indian Air Force (IAF) is the air arm of the Indian armed forces with the primary mission to secure Indian airspace and conduct aerial activities during armed conflicts. Also known as ‘Bharatiya Vayu Sena’, the IAF was officially established on October 8, 1932, by the British Empire. The President of India holds the rank of Supreme Commander of the air force. The Chief of Air Staff, an air chief marshal is responsible for the operational command of the air force. Motto of the Indian Air Force is ‘Touch the Sky with Glory’ and it was taken from eleventh chapter of the Bhagavad Gita. Hence, the correct answer is Option B i.e. 8 October.

q: Who among the following is the winner of the US Open 2021 Men's Singles Final?
subject: currentaff
exp: Daniil Medvedev won the US Open 2021 Men's Singles Final by defeating Novak Djokovic in straight sets (6–4, 6–4, 6–4). This victory marked Medvedev's first Grand Slam title. Medvedev became the third Russian man, after Yevgeny Kafelnikov and Marat Safin, to win a major singles title, and the first to do so since Safin at the 2005 Australian Open. Djokovic was aiming for a calendar Grand Slam (winning all four major titles in the same year) but fell short in the final. Rafael Nadal and Roger Federer did not participate in the 2021 US Open due to injuries. Hence, the correct answer is Option B i.e. Daniil Medvedev.

q: Which city is scheduled to host the Summer Olympic Games 2024?
subject: currentaff
exp: Paris, France is the host city for the 2024 Summer Olympics, which will be held from July 26 to August 11, 2024. The games will be the biggest event ever held in France. Paris is the second city to host the Summer Olympics three times, after London. The other times Paris hosted the Summer Olympics were in 1900 and 1924. Paris was elected as the host city on 13 September 2017 at the 131st IOC Session in Lima, Peru. Hence, the correct answer is Option A i.e. Paris.

q: Who has won the Wimbledon men's singles title in July 2022?
subject: currentaff
exp: In July 2022, Novak Djokovic won the Wimbledon Men's Singles title by defeating Nick Kyrgios in the final. The match took place on July 10, 2022, and Djokovic won with a scoreline of 4–6, 6–3, 6–4, 7–6(3). This victory marked Djokovic’s 7th Wimbledon title and his 21st Grand Slam title overall, placing him one title behind Rafael Nadal, who had 22 Grand Slam titles at that time. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e. Novak Djokovic.

q: Who has been appointed as the Chairman of the eight-member high-level committee constituted by the Government of India to examine the proposal of "One Nation, One Election"?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Government of India constituted an eight-member high-level committee to examine the feasibility of implementing the concept of "One Nation, One Election". This reform aims to synchronize elections for the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies to reduce costs and ensure efficient governance. Former President Ram Nath Kovind was appointed as the Chairman of this committee due to his experience and stature in public life. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which organization is primarily responsible for monitoring and controlling air pollution in India?
subject: currentaff
exp: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is a statutory organization under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. Its main role is to monitor, control, and prevent air and water pollution across India. While ISRO focuses on space research, BIS deals with standards and quality, and NGT is a judicial body for environmental disputes, CPCB is the primary body directly tasked with air pollution control. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which one of the following statements is correct?
o: India is the largest importer of defence equipment in the world
The United States of America was the second largest exporter of defence equipment to India in 2014
Russia has stopped exporting defence equipment to India
India meets not more than 25 per cent of its requirement of defence equipment through import
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Defence and Security Explantion: India has consistently been the largest importer of defense equipment globally, accounting for a significant portion of worldwide arms imports, according to various defense reports. In 2014, Russia was still the largest exporter of defense equipment to India, although the U.S. has been increasing its defense sales to India in recent years. Russia continues to be one of India's major defense suppliers, including supplying key systems like fighter jets, air defense systems, and naval platforms. India imports defense equipment,comprising around 60-70% of its total defense needs, although efforts have been underway to boost indigenous defense production. Hence, the correct option is a.

q: Who among the following has authored the book 'The Idea of India'?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point : Famous book and authors Explanation: Sunil Khilnani is the author of the book "The Idea of India," . It explores the political, social, and cultural landscape of post- independence India. It was published in 1997. Khushwant Singh wrote books including "Train to Pakistan" and "The History of Sikhs." Shashi Tharoor is an Indian author, and some of his famous books are "The Great Indian Novel," "Inglorious Empire," and "An Era of Darkness." William Dalrymple is a Scottish historian and writer who wrote "City of Djinns," "White Mughals," and "The Anarchy." Hence, the correct option is c.

q: In order to create awareness among the people regarding endangered species of tortoise, in the month of February 2015, the third Tortoise Festival was held at :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:Place in news Explanation: The Tortoise Festival is organized to raise awareness about endangered tortoise species and promote their conservation. In February 2015, the third edition of this festival was held at Hajong Lake in Assam. Hajong Lake is known for its rich biodiversity, including a variety of freshwater tortoises and other rare species. The event aimed to engage the local community and increase public knowledge. Hence, the correct option is A.

q: In January 2015, Government of India approved the establishment of a Neutrino Observatory at :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:Science and technology Explanation: In January 2015, the Government of India approved the establishment of a Neutrino Observatory at Bodi hills in Tamil Nadu. The India-based Neutrino Observatory (INO) is proposed to be constructed in the Bodi West Hills of Theni district., Tamil Nadu. It is near the Kerala-Tamil Nadu border. The site is located close to the Mathikettan Shola National Park. The INO project aims to build a world- class underground laboratory with a rock cover, designed to conduct basic research on neutrinos, subatomic particles with a very small mass and no electric charge. Hence , the correct option is a.

q: Which one of the following Grand Slam Tennis Tournaments had the highest total prize money (in terms of US Dollars) in the year 2014?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:Sports Explanation: In 2014, Wimbledon had the highest total prize money among Grand Slam tournaments. Total money prize was approximately $42 million USD. Prize breakdowns were as follows: Australian Open - $33 million, French Open - $34.5 million, US Open - $38.25 million. Grand Slam in tennis means winning all four major championships—Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and US Open—within a single calendar year. This achievement is rare, with only six occurrences in tennis history. It has been accomplished by five different players to date. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Which one of the following cities has been identified as the best habitable city in the Mercer Quality of Life Study, 2016 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:Report and index Explanation: In the Mercer Quality of Life Study 2016 survey, Vienna was identified as the best habitable city. The report indicates that Vienna, the capital of Austria, offers the highest quality of life among cities globally. In contrast, Baghdad, the capital of Iraq, consistently ranks at the bottom of the livability index. The Mercer Quality of Life Study evaluates living conditions in over 450 cities worldwide. The study assessed living conditions across more than 450 cities, focusing on 39 factors categorized into 10 groups. These factors included aspects such as political stability, healthcare, education, infrastructure, and environment. The 18th annual Quality of Living survey was released on February 23, 2016. The survey covers 230 cities and assists companies in determining compensation and hardship allowances for international staff. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: The latest trophy introduced in Indian domestic cricket competition is:
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept:Sports Explanation: Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy is the latest addition among Indian domestic cricket trophies. It was named after the legendary Indian cricketer Syed Mushtaq Ali, a popular figure known for his aggressive batting style. The Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy was introduced in 2006 by the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) as India’s premier domestic T20 cricket competition. The Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy serves as a platform for emerging Indian cricketers to showcase their talent in the T20 format, potentially earning opportunities for selection in IPL teams and the Indian national team. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Recently French military announced to end 'Operation Sangaris', which was in operation since 2013, in which one of the following nations?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: International news Explanation: Operation Sangaris was a military intervention conducted by the French military in the Central African Republic (CAR) from late 2013 to 2016. In late 2012, former President François Bozizé requested international assistance from France and the United States to counter the Séléka rebel movement. The Mission to the CAR (MISCA) was established in 2013 following the adoption of Resolution 2127 by the United Nations Security Council. The primary objective of Operation Sangaris was to restore security in the CAR after a prolonged period of communal violence. During its course, Operation Sangaris involved deploying thousands of French troops to the region, working alongside United Nations and African Union forces to help restore peace. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Which of the following statements about Subedar Major Fateh Singh is / are correct ? 1. He was a big bore rifle shooter who won two gold medals during first Commonwealth championship 2. He retired from Bihar regiment before joining Defence Service Corps 3. He was killed during the terror attack at Pathankot Air Force Base Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept:Person in news Explanation: Subedar Major Fateh Singh was a distinguished figure in the Indian Army. He was known for his contributions both as a soldier and an athlete. He served in the 15th Battalion of the Dogra Regiment, and was also a sport shooter. In 1995, he achieved success at the Commonwealth Shooting Championships held in New Delhi. He won both a gold and a silver medal in the big-bore individual and team events. While on duty with the Defence Security Corps, he was martyred during the Pathankot attack in 2016. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Who among the following is the winner of the Human Rights Award by Amnesty International, Germany?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Awards and Prize Explanation: Henri Tiphagne was the winner of the 8th Human Rights Award from Amnesty International, Germany. He is a lawyer and human rights defender hailing from Tamil Nadu, India. Tiphagne is actively working to combat torture and discrimination within India. In the mid-1990s, he established the organization People's Watch. The Human Rights Award is given by Amnesty International Germany to recognize individuals or organizations that advocate for human rights in challenging conditions. The award includes a financial grant of 10,000 euros, funded by Stiftung Menschenrechte and Förderstiftung Amnesty. Amnesty International is a prominent non-governmental organization based in the United Kingdom People’s Watch focuses on: Researching and documenting human rights violations. Providing legal representation to victims of such crimes. Actively promoting human rights education. Hence, the correct option is d.

q: Which one of the following States won the National School Athletics Championship in the year 2016 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:Sports Explanation: Kerala won the 61st National School Athletics Championship in 2016. The Championship took place in 2016 and concluded in Kozhikode. This championship is an annual outdoor track and field competition organized by the Athletics Federation of India, serving as the national championship for the sport in India. The event was first held in 1961 and has since become a significant platform for identifying and nurturing athletic talent among school students across the country. Kerala excelled in the championship, amassing a total of 306 points. The state achieved remarkable success, winning 39 gold, 29 silver, and 17 bronze medals. Tamil Nadu finished in second place with 116 points, securing 11 gold, 8 silver, and 13 bronze medals. Maharashtra achieved third position with a total of 101 points. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Which of the following pairs of Scheme and Target Group, as initiated in the Railway Budget 2016, is / are correctly matched ? Schemes Target Group 1. Antyodaya Express For the Reserved Passengers 2. Deen Dayalu Coaches For the Unreserved Passengers 3. Humsafar For the Reserved Passengers 4. Tejas For the Unreserved Passengers Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:Government scheme Explanation: In the Rail Budget 2016-17, four new trains and a new type of coach were introduced. This budget marked the last separate Rail Budget in India, after which it was merged with the Union Budget. Antyodaya Express and Deen Dayalu Coaches cater to unreserved passengers. Humsafar, Tejas, and Uday are designed for reserved passengers. Antyodaya Express is a long-distance that operates on high-density routes. As of 2020, there are 16 Antyodaya trains in operation. In 2019, Indian Railways planned to manufacture over 1,000 Deen Dayalu coaches. Humsafar is a fully premium service offering AC-3 Tier and Sleeper Class accommodations. The first service was launched on December 16, 2016, between Gorakhpur and Anand Vihar Terminal, New Delhi. Tejas is High-speed trains equipped with world-class facilities. It is operated by private entities responsible for providing services. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Which one of the following cities hosted the Twelfth South Asian Games, 2016?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept:Sports Explanation: The Twelfth South Asian Games were hosted in 2016 in India, marking a major regional sports event that promotes unity and cooperation among South Asian countries. The games were held in Guwahati and Shillong from February 5 to February 16, 2016. Guwahati, the largest city in Assam, was one of the primary hosts, while Shillong, the capital of Meghalaya, also hosted various events. A total of eight South Asian nations participated in the games, including India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, Nepal, Bhutan, Afghanistan, and the Maldives. The games featured 23 different sports categories. India emerged as the top-performing country, winning the most medals across various categories. The games strengthened regional ties and promoted sportsmanship and cultural exchange among the South Asian nations. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Which of the following statements about Exercise IBSAMAR is / are correct ? 1. It is a joint exercise of Navy of India, Brazil and Sri Lanka 2. The fifth edition of IBSAMAR was held in Goa 3. All previous editions of IBSAMAR were held outside India Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Defence and Security Explanation: IBSAMAR stands for India-Brazil-South Africa Maritime Exercise. It was initiated in 2016. Exercise IBSAMAR is a trilateral naval exercise involving the navies of India, Brazil, and South Africa, not Sri Lanka. The fifth edition of IBSAMAR was indeed held in Goa, India, which took place in 2019. The previous editions of IBSAMAR have been conducted outside of India. Specifically: The first edition was held in Brazil in 2008. The second edition took place in Brazil in 2010. The third edition was also held in Brazil in 2012. The fourth edition occurred in South Africa in 2016. Hence the correct option is b.

q: The largest greenhouse gas leakage in the US history took place in February 2016. The gas was :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: International news Explanation: The incident occurred at the Aliso Canyon natural gas storage facility in California. It began in October 2015 and was capped in February 2016. The gas that leaked was primarily methane, which is a powerful greenhouse gas. An estimated 97,100 tons of methane were released into the atmosphere. This significant leak contributed to climate change and had serious environmental effects. Methane is about 25 times more effective than carbon dioxide at trapping heat over a 100-year period. The incident highlighted the risks of natural gas storage and the need for improved leak detection and prevention. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Which one of the following was the Theme State in the Surajkund International Craft Mela?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Places in news Explanation: The Surajkund Mela, held annually since 1987 in Surajkund, Faridabad, Haryana, celebrates India's handicrafts, handlooms, and cultural heritage. It is known as the largest crafts fair globally, drawing attention to the artistic diversity of the country. Surajkund Mela Authority and Haryana Tourism host the event with support from the Union Ministries of Tourism, Textiles, Culture, and External Affairs. The theme state for the 30th Mela event was Telangna. Each year, a Theme State is selected to showcase its unique culture. In 2020 (34th Mela), Himachal Pradesh was chosen as the Theme State. The fair includes participants from at least 20 countries and all Indian states, making it a global event. Multi-cuisine Food Courts offer traditional dishes from around the world, enhancing the cultural experience. The Mela hosts folk performances at two “Chaupals” and captivating evening recitals at the “Natyashala” for added entertainment. Visitors can also enjoy amusement areas, adventure sports, and joy rides specifically designated at the fair. Hence , the correct option is c.

q: Which one of the following fiction was selected for Man Booker Prize, 2015 ?
o: A Brief History of Seven Killings
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept:Awards and prizes Explanation: The Man Booker Prize is one of the most prestigious literary awards, recognizing excellence in fiction published in the English language. | | Defence The Man Booker Prize for Fiction in 2015 was awarded to "A Brief History of Seven Killings" by Marlon James. This novel explores the impact of the 1976 assassination attempt on the musician Bob Marley and delves into the social and political landscape of Jamaica during that era. Marlon James’s work was praised for its unique style, utilizing multiple perspectives and voices, which contribute to a rich and complex storytelling experience. Other novels nominated for the 2015 award included "A Little Life" by Hanya Yanagihara, "The Fishermen" by Chigozie Obioma, and "Satin Island" by Tom McCarthy, but they did not win the award. James's win was significant, as he became the first Jamaican author to receive the Man Booker Prize, highlighting the importance of Caribbean literature on the global stage. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Which of the following statements about 'Nairobi Package' adopted in the Tenth Ministerial Conference of WTO is / are correct ? 1. No safeguard mechanism for developing country members 2. Preferential rules of origin for least developed countries 3. Public stockholding for food security purposes Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point:International affairs Explanation: The Nairobi Package refers to the 10th Ministerial Conference of the WTO, held from 15 to 19 December 2015 in Nairobi, Kenya. The package includes 6 key decisions on agriculture, cotton, and other issues relevant to Least Developed Countries (LDCs). Developing countries will be able to use the Special Safeguard Mechanism (SSM) to protect agricultural products. A Ministerial Decision was adopted on Preferential Rules of Origin for LDCs, ensuring the rules are simple and transparent, facilitating market access for LDCs. Members committed to work towards a permanent solution on the issue of public stockholding for food security. The World Trade Organization (WTO) is the only global organization that manages trade rules between nations, with 164 members and 23 observer governments. Its headquarters is located in Geneva, Switzerland, and it has been operational since 1 January 1995 (with origins dating back to the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) in 1948). Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Identify from the following the novel written by Charles Dickens that critiqued the adverse impact of industrialization on the poor.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Book and authors Explanation: "Hard Times" is a novel by Charles Dickens, published in 1854. The book serves as a critique of the industrialization of England during the 19th century and its detrimental effects on the working class and the poor. The novel challenges the utilitarian philosophy that emphasizes efficiency and practicality over human values and emotions. Dickens illustrates how industrialization leads to the dehumanization of workers, poor living conditions, and a lack of education, particularly for children. The story revolves around characters like Thomas Gradgrind, who embodies the cold, factual approach of utilitarianism, and Stephen Blackpool, a factory worker representing the struggles of the working class. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Who among the following is the winner of the Jnanpith Award for the year 2015 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Awards and prizes Explanation: Raghuveer Chaudhary is an esteemed Gujarati writer, known for his novels, poetry, and critical essays. His works often explore social issues and human values. In 2015, Chaudhary was honored with the 51st Jnanpith Award for his significant contributions to Gujarati literature. Jnanpith Award is one of India's highest literary honors, awarded annually by the Bharatiya Jnanpith to an author for their outstanding contribution to literature. Bhalachandra Nemade won the Jnanpith Award in 2014 for his work in Marathi literature. Pratibha Ray is a noted Odia author, she won the Jnanpith Award in 1991. Girish Karnad is renowned Kannada playwright, awarded the Jnanpith in 1998. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Which one of the following statements regarding Zika virus is not correct ?
o: It was first isolated from a monkey in the Zika forest of Uganda
It is transmitted by daytime-active Aedes mosquitoes
It does not cause mother to child infection
Zika virus in pregnant women results in abnormal brain development of the fetus
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: Science and technology Explanation: Zika Virus is a flavivirus transmitted primarily by mosquitoes. It was first detected in 1947 in monkeys in Uganda and was later found in humans in 1952 in Uganda and the United Republic of Tanzania. This virus is mainly spread by Aedes mosquitoes, which are active during the day. Infection with the Zika virus can lead to babies being born with microcephaly and other congenital abnormalities. It can also be transmitted from mother to child. The estimated incubation period for Zika virus disease ranges from 3 to 14 days. Furthermore, Zika virus infections have been associated with conditions like Guillain-Barré syndrome, neuropathy, and myelitis, particularly in adults and older children. The first isolation of the virus occurred in 1947 from the blood of a sentinel rhesus macaque located in the Zika Forest near Entebbe, Uganda, during yellow fever surveillance activities. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: In February 2016, which one of the following countries has rejected the plea of the incumbent President through a referendum to contest the Presidential election for the third term?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Places in news Explanation: In February 2016, a referendum was held in Bolivia. It was held to determine whether the incumbent president, Evo Morales, could seek a third term in office. The proposal was to amend the constitution, which would have allowed Morales, who had already served two terms, to run again in the 2019 presidential elections. The referendum results indicated that a majority of voters opposed the amendment, leading to the rejection of Morales's bid for a third term. Morales had been in office since 2006. The outcome emphasized the democratic process in Bolivia, reflecting the people's will regarding leadership continuity and term limits. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: Lance Naik Hanmanthappa Koppad, who was buried alive under snow by deadly avalanche at Siachen Glacier, who later died in a hospital after he was miraculously rescued by army, was a soldier of :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key point: Person in news Explanation: In February 2016, Lance Naik Hanmanthappa Koppad was tragically buried under snow after a devastating avalanche struck the Siachen Glacier. It is one of the world's highest and harshest military zones. After being trapped under snow for six days, he was miraculously rescued alive by the Indian Army. However, despite the army's efforts and extensive medical care, he later succumbed to his injuries in a hospital.Lance Naik Hanmanthappa Koppad served in the Madras Regiment, one of the oldest regiments in the Indian Army, known for its distinguished service and contributions. Hence, the correct option is b.

q: Who among the following is one of the six winners of the Goldman Environmental Prize, for the year 2017 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prizes Explanation: The 2017 Goldman Environmental Prize has been given to Prafulla Samantara. The six winners of the Goldman Environmental Prize for 2017 include: Uroš Macerl (Slovenia) Prafulla Samantara (India) Mark Lopez (United States) Rodrigo Tot (Guatemala) Rodrigue Mugaruka Katembo (Democratic Republic of the Congo) Wendy Bowman (Australia) An iconic leader of social justice movements in India, Prafulla Samantara led a historic 12-year legal battle that affirmed the indigenous Dongria Kondh’s land rights and protected the Niyamgiri Hills from a massive, open-pit aluminum ore mine. Also known as the “Green Nobel Prize,” this annual award honours grassroots environmental champions worldwide for their outstanding contributions to environmental protection. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which of the following statements about the Paris Agreement on Climate Change is NOT correct?
o: The agreement has been signed by more than 190 countries (as of March 2017)
The agreement went into effect on 4 November 2016
The Intended Nationally Determined Contribution (INDC) pledged during the 2015 Climate Change Conference has been withdrawn in the Paris Agreement
Both USA and China joined the Paris Agreement
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Environment and Geography Explanation: The Paris Agreement is a legally binding international treaty on climate change. It was adopted by 196 Parties at the UN Climate Change Conference (COP21) in Paris, France, on 12 December 2015. It entered into force on 4 November 2016. Its overarching goal is to hold “the increase in the global average temperature to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels” and pursue efforts “to limit the temperature increase to 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels.” The INDCs (Intended Nationally Determined Contributions) are individualized targets that each country sets in order to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and adapt to the impacts of climate change. These INDCs are a key element of the Paris Agreement and continue to be a part of the agreement. To limit global warming to 1.5°C, greenhouse gas emissions must peak before 2025 at the latest and decline 43% by 2030. The United States and China, two of the largest contributors to global greenhouse gas emissions, have both joined the agreement and have been instrumental in its implementation. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which one of the following portals was launched on the 250th anniversary of Survey of India ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Government Schemes Explanation: The portal launched on the 250th anniversary of the Survey of India is Nakshe. This online portal was introduced to provide digital maps and related services to the public, commemorating the significant milestone of the Survey of India. Topographic maps or Open Series Maps (OSM) containing natural and man- made geographical features including terrain or topography are prepared by the SoI since its inception in 1767 and is in conformity with the National Map Policy-2005. These OSM maps have been made available for free download from “nakshe” web portal in pdf format on 1:50,000 scale through Aaadhar enabled user authentication process, in line with Digital India programme of the Government of India. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Who among the following has won the Dadasaheb Phalke award for the year 2016 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prizes Explanation: The 64th Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2016 was received by the filmmaker and actor K. Vishwanath. The Dadasaheb Phalke Award, named after Dhundiraj Govind Phalke, the pioneer of Indian cinema, is the highest honor in the film industry since 1969. India’s first feature film, “Raja Harishchandra,” was directed by Phalke in 1913. Recipients of this award receive a shawl, a medallion called the Swarna Kamal (Golden Lotus), and a cash prize of Rs. 10 lakh. The recipients of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award were Rajnikant (2019) and Amitabh Bachchan (2018). The Dadasaheb Phalke Award for the year 2024 was received by Shahrukh Khan. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Statement I: The Man Booker International Prize is now unlikely to be won by an English author Statement II : The Booker Prize Foundation announced that the Man Ronker International Prize was to evolve from 2016 onwards into a prize for fiction in translation
o: Both the statements are individually true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is NOT the correct explanation of Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prizes Explanation: The Booker Prize for Fiction was formerly known as the Booker- McConnell Prize (1969-2001) and then known as the Man Booker Prize (2002- 2019). It is a literary prize awarded each year for the best original novel written in the English Language and published in the United Kingdom. The Booker Prize Foundation on 7th July 2015 announced that the Man Booker International Prize is to evolve from 2016, to encourage more publishing and reading of quality fiction in translation. Hence statement Il is the correct and correct explanation for the statement I. As of 2016, the prize will be awarded annually on the basis of a single book, translated into English and published in the UK, rather than every two years for a body of work. For the 2016 prize, both novels and collections of short stories will be eligible. The International Booker Prize, formerly known as the Man Booker International Prize, is now open to authors from all nationalities and is not unlikely to be won by an English author. The International Booker Prize is open to authors from all nationalities who have work available in English and published in the UK or Ireland. The 2024 International Booker Prize was won by German author Jenny Erpenbeck for her novel Kairos, which was translated by Michael Hofmann Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which one of the following countries has officially recognized Bitcoin as a legal payment method since 1st April 2017 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Economy Explanation: Japan made history in 2017 by becoming the first country to recognize Bitcoin as a payment method. Japan officially recognized Bitcoin as a legal payment method in April 2017 under the Payment Services Act (PSA). In April 2017, Japan's PSA recognized Bitcoin and other virtual assets as legitimate property, making it one of the first countries to do so. The PSA requires cryptocurrency exchanges operating in Japan to be registered, keep records, take security measures, and protect customers. As of April 2017, cryptocurrency exchange businesses operating in Japan have been regulated under the PSA. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Padma Shri Sudarsan Pattnaik is well known as a
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Person in news Explanation: Sudarsan Pattnaik is one of India's most renowned sand artists from Puri, Odisha. He is known for his impressive sand sculptures and awards recognizing his talent and achievements in this unique art form. In 2014, the Government of India honored him with the Padma Shri, India's fourth-highest civilian award, for his contributions to seashore sand art.Some key facts about Sudarsan Pattnaik: In February 2017, he broke the Guinness world record for making the world's largest sand castle on Puri Beach. He has won numerous awards for his sand art, including the People's Choice Award at the Sand Sculpting World Cup 2014 in Atlantic City, US. He was the first Indian to win the Italian Sand Art Award in 2019 at the International Scorrano Sand Nativity event in Italy. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Which one of the following statements about Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana is NOT correct?
o: The aim is to provide clean cooking fuel to poor households across the country
The scheme was launched in the year 2015
A deposit free LPG connection is provided to adult women from poor households
The target under the scheme was to provide 8 Crore LPG Connections
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Government Schemes Explanation: The extension of the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) for the allocation of 75 lakh LPG connections over three years, from the Financial Year 2023-24 to 2025-26, was approved by the Union Cabinet, under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi. Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was started in May, 2016 to provide clean cooking fuel to poor households across the country. Under PMUY, a deposit free LPG connection is provided to adult women from poor households. The target under the scheme was to release 8 Crore LPG Connections Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Who among the following are awarded with the Special Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics for the year 2017 ?
o: Stephen W Hawking and Roger Penrose
Ronald Drever, Kip S Thorne and Kainer Weiss
Joseph Polchinski, Andrew Strominger and Cumrun Vafa
J Michael Kosterlitz, Duncan Haldane and David J Thouless
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prizes Explanation: The recipients of the 2017 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics were Joseph Polchinski, Andrew Strominger, and Cumrun Vafa. They were awarded for their transformative advances in quantum field theory, string theory, and quantum gravity. About the Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics: The Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics is one of the Breakthrough Prizes, awarded by the Breakthrough Prize Board. Initially named Fundamental Physics Prize, it was founded in July 2012 by Russia-born Israeli entrepreneur, venture capitalist and physicist Yuri Milner. The prize is awarded to physicists from theoretical, mathematical, or experimental physics that have made transformative contributions to fundamental physics, and specifically for recent advances. Worth USD$3 million, the prize is the most lucrative physics prize in the world and is more than twice the amount given to the Nobel Prize awardees. The 2024 Breakthrough Prizes in the Life Sciences category recognised groundbreaking research set to change the lives of those suffering from three Rare Diseases: Parkinson’s disease, Cystic fibrosis and Cancer. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: In March 2017, a High Court in India had accorded the status of living human entities to which two of the following rivers?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: National News Explanation: In March 2017, the Uttarakhand High Court accorded the status of living human entities to the Ganga and Yamuna rivers. This landmark decision recognized these rivers, along with their tributaries and streams, as juristic persons with corresponding rights, duties, and liabilities, aimed at preserving and conserving them. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Who among the following Mathematician is the recipient of the Abel Prize for the year 2017 ?

q: Who among the following was the Head of the Expert Committee on Variable Capital Company in India which has submitted its report recently?

q: Which one of the following States ranked first on Sustainable Development Goal India Index, 2020-21 released by NITI Aayog?

Initially awarded biennially for an author's work, since 2016, it has been awarded annually to a single translated book published in the UK or Ireland. Notable past winners include Ismail Kadare, the inaugural recipient, and Marieke Lucas Rijneveld in 2020. q: Scientists at CSIR-NCL Pune, with support from the Water Technology Initiative of the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India, have recently developed a novel hybrid technology to bring safe and healthy drinking water. What is the name of the hybrid technology?

SWASTIIK involves boiling liquid through pressure reduction (cavitation) and utilizes natural oils with antimicrobial properties. This technology efficiently eliminates harmful bacteria, including antibiotic- resistant strains. SWASTIIK is a cost-effective solution for water disinfection. Hence,the correct option is b.

q: The 'Stand-Up India Scheme' is related to which one of the following issues?

q: Consider the following missiles : 1. Agni-I 2. BrahMos 3. Dhanush Which of the above is/are cruise missile/missiles?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Defence and Security Explanation: The BrahMos missile is a supersonic cruise missile developed by BrahMos Aerospace, a joint venture between India's Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and Russia's NPO Machinostroyenia. It is a two-stage missile with a solid propellant booster engine as its first stage, which brings it to supersonic speed, and a liquid ramjet or the second stage that takes the missile closer to 3 Mach speed in cruise phase. The missile has a flight range of up to 290 km with supersonic speed all through the flight, leading to shorter flight time, consequently ensuring lower dispersion of targets, quicker engagement time and non- interception by any known weapon system in the world. It operates on ‘Fire and Forget Principle’, adopting varieties of flights on its way to the target. Prithvi and Dhanush are types of ballistic missiles. They follow a ballistic trajectory, meaning it is propelled by a rocket engine during the initial phase of its flight and then follows a trajectory governed by gravity. Hence, the correct answer is b.

Established in 1961, the Farakka Barrage Project oversees the construction and management of the barrage. The barrage was commissioned in 1975, representing a significant achievement in water resource management. In 1996, a treaty was signed to resolve disputes between Bangladesh and India regarding the sharing of Ganges waters at Farakka, aiming to promote cooperation and resolve differences.
