q: Which one of the following statements is not true with regard to tribal welfare?
o: The Comprehensive Tribal Development Programme aims at land development activities, improvement of roof houses, infrastructure development and so on.
As per the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers Act, villages in forests are provided with community rights.
The Special Area Development Programme aims at distribution of forest area produces to the tribals equitably.
The Special Central Assistance to Tribal Subplan provides dairy cows to tribal groups living below the poverty line.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Tribal Welfare programmes Explanation: A special area development programme is part of the planning policy of India for balanced regional development which focused on the development of those specific regions which failed to keep pace with other parts of the country. It is focused developmental programmes for backward areas are expected to help reduce imbalances and speed up the economic development of these regions. Comprehensive Tribal Development Programme (CTDP) provides land development activities, roof house improvements, road maintenance, infrastructure improvements in GTR schools, drinking water, economic development schemes, and training skills to tribal people living below the poverty line. As per the Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006- Individual Rights on land up to a maximum of 10 acres are granted to tribals who have lived in the forests before 13.12.2005. Villages in the forests are also granted community rights, which include a variety of rights. The Special Central Assistance to Tribal Sub Plan distributes dairy cows to tribal groups living below the poverty line. It also provides job placement training to Educated Tribal Youth for unemployed tribal youth through district employment centre. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: 'Dhivehi' is a principal language of which one of the following Union Territories?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: Art and culture Explanation: Malayalam and English serve as the official languages of Lakshadweep, while Dhivehi is a principal language. Dhivehi, an Indo-Aryan language, is the official language of the Maldives and is spoken by around 300,000 individuals, primarily in the Maldives and Lakshadweep. Within Lakshadweep, the Mahl dialect of Dhivehi is prevalent. Dhivehi has undergone linguistic influences from various languages over time, including Arabic, Sinhalese, Malayalam, Hindi, French, Persian, Portuguese, and English. Dhivehi shares a linguistic heritage with Sinhalese and is believed to have emerged around 500 BC. Traditionally, Dhivehi is written in the Thaana script, which is read from right to left, but it was previously written in Dhives Akuru, read from left to right. Hence, the correct option is c.

q: The term 'Sagittarius A*', sometimes mentioned in news, refers to-

two giant black holes spiralling around each other several lightyears away from the Earth
a Supernova explosion seen in real time for the first time ever by scientists
a huge asteroid that passed by very close to the Earth

Researchers noted the greater difficulty in imaging Sagittarius A* compared to M87* due to its much smaller size, being only one-thousandth the size of M87*. Hence,the correct option is a.

The scheme is linked with the Swachh Bharat Mission, the government's flagship program for sanitation. AMRUT focuses on several thrust areas, including: Water supply Sewerage and septage management Stormwater drainage to reduce flooding Development of green spaces/parks Promotion of non-motorized urban transport. q: The World Wetland Day is celebrated on which one of the following dates every year?
subject: currentaff

Wetlands are vital ecosystems crucial for biodiversity, climate regulation, freshwater supply, and global economies. The theme for the 2022 World Wetlands Day is "Wetlands Action for People and Nature," emphasizing the need for conservation and sustainable use of wetlands. 2022 marks the 51st anniversary of World Wetlands Day, commemorating the adoption of the Convention on Wetlands on February 2, 1971, in Ramsar, Iran. Hence, the correct option is d.

q: Consider the following statements with reference to the latest guidelines issued by the Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) : 1. Data centres and service providers shall compulsorily report cyber security breaches within 24 hours. 2. Virtual Private Network providers shall retain user data for at least five years and share records with authorities when required. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept:- Science and technology Explanation:- Established in 2004, the Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) operates as a functional organization under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. CERT-In is the national nodal agency dedicated to responding to computer security incidents promptly. Its focus is on serving the Indian Cyber Community. Data centers and service providers shall compulsorily report cyber security breaches within 6 hours. Virtual asset service providers, virtual asset exchange providers, and custodian wallet service providers are required to store all data collected during the know your customer (KYC) process and records of financial transactions. It should be maintained and stored for a period of five years as per CERT-In regulations. Hence, the correct option is (b).

q: 'The Kh-47M2 Kinzhal', a nuclearcapable hypersonic missile, belongs to which one of the following countries?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Defence and Security Explanation: The Kh-47M2 Kinzhal is a Russian- made nuclear-capable hypersonic aero-ballistic air-to-surface missile capable of carrying nuclear or conventional warheads. Ukraine said six of them were fired on Tuesday. It has a reported range of 1,500 to 2,000 km (930 to 1,240 miles) while carrying a payload of 480 kg. It may reach speeds of up to Mach 10 (12,250 kph), which is 10 times faster than the speed of sound. The missile weighs 9,500 pounds (4,300 kilogrammes). Once launched, a Kinzhal, which means “dagger” in Russian, rapidly achieves Mach 4 (3,000 miles per hour) and can reach speeds of up to Mach 10 (7,600mph). Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: The principle of 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities', often talked about in media, refers to-
o: contributions to the International Monetary Fund
contributions to the United Nations Peacekeeping Operations
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: Environment and geography Explanation: Within the UNFCCC, the 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR–RC) principle recognizes that countries have different abilities and responsibilities in tackling climate change. This principle divides nations into "Annex I" (developed) and "non-Annex I" (developing) categories. Developed nations, or those in Annex I, are assigned greater roles in mitigating climate change. The 1997 Kyoto Protocol enshrined CBDR–RC and the annex classifications into international law, making emissions reductions commitments mandatory for Annex I countries. CBDR–RC is related to the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions. It acknowledges the different capabilities and differing responsibilities of individual countries in addressing climate change. Hence, the correct option is (c).

q: Nowadays, 'nonfungible tokens' are talked about in the context of-

multifactor authentication security requirement in banking transactions

NFT is in context of digital assets on a blockchain Hence. the correct option is a.

q: 'Basel Convention', 'Rotterdam Convention' and 'Stockholm Convention' is related to which of the following?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: Environment and Geography Explanation: Basel convention was adopted in 1989 by the Conference of Plenipotentiaries in Basel, Switzerland, the “Basel Convention on the Control of Trans-boundary Movements of Hazardous Wastes and their Disposal”, generally known as the Basel Convention, came into force in 1992. It is an international treaty that was designed to reduce the movements of hazardous waste between nations, and specifically to prevent transfer of hazardous waste from developed to less developed countries. Rotterdam Convention on Prior Informed Consent Procedures (PIC) entered into force in 2004. It is a multilateral treaty to promote shared responsibilities in relation to importation of hazardous chemicals. Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants is an international environmental treaty, signed on 22 May 2001 in Stockholm and effective from 17 May 2004, that aims to eliminate or restrict the production and use of persistent organic pollutants (POPs) Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Consider the following statements about IPL cricket tournament : 1. Two teams won the tournament five times each. 2. Chennai Super Kings remained runner-up in five editions of the tournament. 3. On three occasions, the final was played abroad. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: The Indian Premier League, also known as the TATA IPL for sponsorship reasons, is a men's Twenty20 cricket league held annually in India. Explanation: In 2023, Chennai Super Kings (CSK) won their fifth Indian Premier League (IPL) championship, equivalent to the record set by Mumbai Indians. They secured a five-wicket victory over the Gujarat Titans (GT) amidst a backdrop of fireworks and jubilant celebrations. In 2009, the tournament was held in South Africa, while the 2014 (partial season), 2020 (full season) and 2021 (half season) were held in UAE. So, three times IPL has been played abroad. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct about KAVACH-2023? 1. It is a joint coordination committee of all three wings of India's armed forces to protect India's borders. 2. It is India's national level hackathon jointly launched by AICTE and BPRD to tackle cyber threats. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: KAVACH-2023 aims to to identify innovative ideas and technological solutions for addressing the cyber security and cybercrime challenges of the 21st century. Explanation: KAVACH-2023 is a National Level Hackathon to tackle cyber threats and provide effective solutions. It is a competition in which participants use technology to achieve an objective. The initiative is jointly conducted by Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell, All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), Bureau of Police Research and Development (BPR&D) and Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C). Its aims to challenge the country’s innovative minds to conceptualize frameworks focusing on cybersecurity issues while using artificial intelligence, machine learning, automation, big data and cloud computing. It includes 20 unique and important matters such as women’s safety apps, obscenity blocker solutions, phishing detection solutions, dark web crawlers, citizen safety apps and malware analysis tools. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following statements about Quad is/are correct? 1. It is a group of four countries, namely, India, Australia, USA and France. 2. Maritime cooperation is an important binding force among members of the Quad. 3. The Quad members formed a working group on COVID-19 vaccines. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Quad is aimed at promoting regional security and economic cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region. Explanation: QUAD, also known as the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue is a strategic forum comprising four countries: the United States, Japan, India, and Australia The Quad focuses on issues such as maritime security, infrastructure development, and supply chain resilience. The trilateral India-US-Japan Malabar naval exercises expanded to include Australia, marking the first official grouping of the Quad since its resurgence in 2017 and the first joint military exercises among the four countries in over a decade. It also developed a Quad Vaccine Partnership, to boost vaccine partnership and has a Covid-19 Global Action Plan to enhance coordination in recovery efforts during the Covid pandemic. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which of the following are India's G20 priorities? 1. Green Development 2. Women-led Development 3. Climate Finance 4. Digital Public Infrastructure Select the correct answer using the code given below:
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: India held the Presidency of the G20 from December 1, 2022 to November 30, 2023. Explanation: The theme of India’s G20 Presidency - “Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam” or “One Earth · One Family · One Future”. India's G20 Priorities are as follows: Green Development, Climate Finance & LiFE Climate change is a key priority for India’s presidential Presidency, with a particular focus towards not only climate finance and technology, but also ensuring just energy transitions | | Defence for developing nations across the world. India offers the world LiFE (Lifestyle for Environment) - a behaviour- based movement that draws from our nation's rich, ancient sustainable traditions to nudge consumers, and in-turn markets, to adopt environmentally-conscious practices. Accelerated, Inclusive & Resilient Growth An accelerated, resilient and inclusive growth is a cornerstone for sustainable development. During its G20 Presidency, India aims to focus on areas that have the potential to bring structural transformation. Accelerating progress on SDGs India acknowledges the detrimental impact of COVID-19, which changed the current decade of action into a decade of recovery. India wants to focus on recommitting G20's efforts to achieving the targets laid out in the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development Technological Transformation & Digital Public Infrastructure India can foreground its belief in a human-centric approach to technology, and facilitate greater knowledge-sharing in priority areas like digital public infrastructure, financial inclusion, and tech- enabled development in sectors ranging from agriculture to education Multilateral Institutions for the 21st century India's G20 priority will be to continue pressing for reformed multilateralism that creates more accountable,inclusive just, equitable and representative multipolar international system that is fit for addressing the challenges in the 21st century. Women-led development India hopes to use the G20 forum to highlight inclusive growth and development, with women empowerment. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: 'Operation Kaveri' is a rescue operation launched by the Government of India to evacuate Indian citizens who were stranded in:
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: During the evacuation under Operation Kaveri, Indians will be transferred from Sudan to the capital city of Khartoum where they will be flown back to India. Explanation: Operation Kaveri' is a rescue operation launched by Government of India to evacuate Indian citizens who are stranded in Sudan. It was launched on April 24, 2023 in response to the crisis in Sudan. A team of officials including the Ministry of External Affairs, the Indian Air Force, and the Indian embassy in Sudan has been appointed by the Indians to ensure the proper process of evacuation gets followed. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: The journey of the First Phase of Sagar Parikrama Yatra, an initiative of the Government of India, was started from :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Government of India has launched the ‘Sagar Parikrama’ program. Explanation: Sagar Parikrama program is being organized through a pre-decided sea route covering coastal states/UTs. The main objectives of ‘Sagar Parikrama’ are : to facilitate interaction with fishermen, coastal communities and stakeholders so as to disseminate information of various fisheries related schemes and programs being implemented by the Government; demonstrating solidarity with all fisher folk, fish farmers and concerned stakeholder as a spirit of Aatmanirbhar Bharat; to promote responsible fisheries with focus on sustainable balance between the utilization of marine fisheries resources for food security of nation and livelihoods of coastal fisher communities protection of marine ecosystems. Phase I Programme of 'Sagar Parikrama' was organized in Gujarat, started on 5th March, 2022 from Mandvi and ended on 6th March 2022 at Porbandar, Gujarat. Phase II of Sagar Parikrama started on 22nd September 2022 from Mangrol to Veraval and ended at Mul Dwaraka, on 23rd September 2022 from Mul Dwaraka to Madhwad. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Which of the following is/are not correct about the Mother's Absolute Affection Programme? 1. Allows all pregnant women to absolutely free and no-expense delivery in public health institutions. 2. Provides assured, comprehensive and quality antenatal care. 3. Promotes undiluted focus on breastfeeding. 4. Provides medical and nutritional care to severely acute malnourished children. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Mothers’ Absolute Affection (MAA) is implemented to improve breastfeeding coverage which includes early initiation of breastfeeding and exclusive breastfeeding for first six months Explanation: MAA-Mother’s Absolute Affection'’ is a nationwide programme launched in an attempt to bring undiluted focus on promotion of breastfeeding and provision of counselling services for supporting breastfeeding through health systems. The programme has been named ‘MAA’ to signify the support a lactating mother requires from family members and at health facilities to breastfeed successfully. The chief components of the MAA Programme are Community awareness generation, Strengthening inter personal communication through ASHA, Skilled support for breastfeeding at Delivery points in Public health facilities, and Monitoring and Award/recognition. The programme follows an age- appropriate complementary feeding practices through capacity building of frontline health workers and comprehensive IEC campaigns. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which of the following are the objectives of SAARC? 1. To promote the welfare of the peoples of South Asia and to improve the quality of life 2. To promote and strengthen collective self- reliance among the countries of South Asia 3. To contribute to mutual trust, understanding and appreciation of one another's problems 4. To work towards ending cross-border terrorism Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: SAARC stands for South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation is an intergovernmental organization for economic development and regional integration. Explanation: SAARC was established on 8 December 1985. Its member countries are—Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka and Afghanistan (2005) The Headquarters and Secretariat Of the Association are at Kathmandu, Nepal. SAARC comprises 3% of the world’s area, 21% of the world’s population and 3.8% (2018) of the global economy Objectives of the SAARC: To promote the welfare of the people of South Asia and to improve their quality of life. To accelerate economic growth, social progress and cultural development in the region and to provide all individuals the opportunity to live in dignity and to realize their full potentials. To promote and strengthen collective self-reliance among the countries of South Asia. To contribute to mutual trust, understanding and appreciation of one another’s problems. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: 'Rail Vikas Nigam Limited' is a :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited is an Indian central public sector enterprise which works as the construction arm of the Ministry of Railways for project implementation and transportation infrastructure development. Explanation: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited (RVNL) has now been promoted to 'Navratna' category of Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSE) from Category-I Miniratna. This means the company will have more autonomy in terms of investment, setting up joint venture and other financial decisions. RVNL has been assigned the following functions: 1. Undertaking project development and execution of works covering full project life cycle. 2. Creating Project specific SPVs for individual works, if required. 3. On completion of a Railway project by RVNL, the concerned Zonal Railway will undertake its operation and maintenance. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which one of the following Indian Naval Ships has recently concluded a 17,000-nm trans- ocean intercontinental voyage?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: Defence ans Security Explanation: INSV Tarini is returning home after successfully completing a 17000-nm long trans-ocean inter-continental voyage in seven months. The Navika Sagar Parikrama by six women naval officers increased the popularity of ocean sailing within the navy and the country. The current sailing expedition of INSV Tarini is part of the run up towards the Navy’s next big project to send a woman to circumnavigate the globe - Solo. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Exercise Ajeya Warrior is a biennial training event between the Indian Army and the army of :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: The 7th edition of Joint military exercise “AJEYA WARRIOR-23” between India and the United Kingdom is being conducted at Salisbury Plains, United Kingdom. Explanation: Exercise AJEYA WARRIOR is a biennial training event with the United Kingdom which is conducted alternatively in the United Kingdom and India, the last edition was held at Chaubatia, Uttarakhand in October 2021. The aim of the exercise is to build positive military relations, imbibe each other’s best practices and promote the ability to operate together while undertaking company-level sub- conventional operations in urban and semi-urban environments under UN mandate. It also aims to develop inter-operability, bonhomie, camaraderie, and friendship between the two armies. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: NISAR satellite is manufactured jointly by Indian Space Research Organisation and :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: NISAR will be the first satellite mission to use two different radar frequencies (L-band and S-band) to measure changes in our planet's surface. Explanation: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) and the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) have jointly manufactured an earth science satellite named, NISAR (NASA- ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar). It is an SUV-size satellite weighing 2,800 kilograms. It consists of both L-band and S-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) instruments, which makes it a dual- frequency imaging radar satellite. It is capable of penetrating clouds and can collect data day and night regardless of the weather conditions. The spacecraft will orbit the Earth in a sun synchronous orbit of 747 Km with an inclination of 98.4 degree for a 12- day repeat cycle. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following statements about Organization for Security and Cooperation in Europe (OSCE) are correct ? 1. There are total 57 states from Europe, Central Asia and America. 2. It offers a forum for political negotiations and decision-making in the field of early warning, conflict prevention, crisis management and post-conflict rehabilitation. 3. OSCE has the primary responsibility of providing military security in the European Region. 4. The decisions of OSCE are legally binding. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: With 57 participating States in North America, Europe and Asia, the OSCE – the Organization for Security and Co- operation in Europe – is the world’s largest regional security organization. Explanation: The OSCE works for stability, peace and democracy for more than a billion people, through political dialogue about shared values and through practical work that aims to make a lasting difference. The OSCE primary responsibility is enhancing border management and security while facilitating legitimate travel and commerce, protecting human rights and promoting human contacts. The OSCE works in conflict prevention and resolution, facilitating settlements of conflicts and promoting peacebuilding and post-conflict rehabilitation. Decisions are taken by consensus on a politically, but not legally binding basis. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct about 'Action for Climate Empowerment' (ACE)? 1. It is a term adopted by the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change. 2. This term is related to the Paris Agreement. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Implementation of ACE elements is crucial to the global response to climate change. Explanation: Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) is a term adopted by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) to denote work under Article 6 of the Convention (1992) and Article 12 of the Paris Agreement. The over-arching goal of ACE is to empower all members of society to engage in climate action, through the six ACE elements - climate change education and public awareness, training, public participation, public access to information, and international cooperation on these issues. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: The annual Kheer Bhawani Mela is organized at :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Kheer Bawani, the most revered Holy Temple Shrine of Kashmiri Brahmans Explanation: Mela Kheer Bhawani festival was celebrated in Jammu and Kashmir in the month of June. The Goddess Mata Ragnya Devi is symbolised as a sacred spring at Tula Mula village, near (27 kms) Srinagar, Kashmir in J&K India. The spring of Kheer Bhawani is quite large and has always been held in veneration by the Brahman population of Srinagar. The Temple, dedicated to the Goddess Mata Kheer Bhawani (originally Bhawani Mata), has been constructed over a Sacred Spring. The Temple-Spring complex is affectionately known as Kheer Bhawani as thousands of devotees offer milk and 'kheer' to the Sacred Spring. Kheer Bhawani is considered to be the Presiding Deity of most of the Kashmiri Brahmans. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: What is 'Climate Neutral Now' initiative? 1. It encourages organizations and other interested stakeholders to act now in order to achieve a carbon neutral world by 2030. 2. It derives its aims from the Paris Agreement. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key concept: The initiative was launched in 2015 based on a mandate to promote the voluntary use of carbon market mechanisms recognized under the Convention. Explanation: The Climate Neutral Now Initiative is an initiatives launched by the UNFCCC secretariat to increase climate action by engaging non-Party stakeholders (sub- national governments, companies, organizations, individuals). The Climate Neutral Now Initiative encourages and supports organizations to act now to achieve a climate neutral world by 2050, as enshrined in the Paris Agreement. It is a tool to promote additional voluntary action on climate and to provide recognition for it. The initiative is not a certification scheme for its participants. An organization can become a participant by signing the Climate Neutral Now Pledge, following the three steps (Measure, Reduce, Contribute) and reporting on its actions and achievements annually. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Consider the following statements about Khelo India University Games (KIUG) : 1. The first edition of KIUG was held in Odisha. 2. The name of the mascot of the third edition of KIUG was Jeetu. 3. The first edition of KIUG was postponed due to COVID. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept:bKhelo India University Games (KIUG) is an annual national sports competition organized by the Government of India with the aim of promoting sports culture and nurturing young talent at the university level. Explanation: The third edition of the Khelo India University Games (KIUG) began in Uttar Pradesh. The third edition of the Khelo India University Games concluded at the IIT BHU campus in Varanasi. The mascot of the 3rd edition was Jeetu. Mascot Jeetu represents Barasingha. It is an amazing mammal, with a powerful physique and remarkable speed. The first Khelo India University Games were hosted in 2020 at the Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology (KIIT), Odisha. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which one among the following cities was included as the 'City of Music' in the year in the UNESCO's Creative Cities Network?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Art and Culture Gwalior was designated as the UNESCO “City of Music'' while Kozhikode was designated as the UNESCO “City of Literature”, in June 2024. Explanation: Almost 300 cities around the world which currently make up this network. Their objective is to work together towards placing creativity and cultural industries at the heart of their development plans at the local level and cooperating actively at the international level. UNESCO recognised Gwalior as the City of Literature for its contribution to music. Alongside Gwalior, Kozhikode was recognized as the 'City of Literature'. The UNESCO Creative Cities Network (UCCN) was created in 2004. It aims to promote cooperation with and among cities that have identified creativity as a strategic factor for sustainable urban development. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Which one among the following statements with regard to India's maritime initiative, SAGAR, is correct?
o: It is a platform for conducting joint naval exercises under QUAD
It aimed at promoting maritime cooperation with countries of the Indian Ocean Region
It is an alliance of India, USA and Japan to provide safety to merchant ships passing through the Indian Ocean Region
It is the name given to India's participation in the Contact Group on Piracy set up by the UN Security Council
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: International Relations Why in news? Prime Minister Narendra Modi's swearing in ceremony was attended by Sri Lanka, Maldives, Seychelles, Bangladesh, Mauritius, Nepal and Bhutan which showcased India’s commitment to NFP and SAGAR vision. Explanation: Security And Growth for All in the Region (SAGAR) concept was first articulated in 2015. SAGAR is primarily focused on enhancing maritime cooperation and security among nations in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR), promoting collaborative efforts to address security challenges and foster economic growth through shared maritime resources and capabilities . It is India's strategic vision for the Indian Ocean Region (IOR). Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following does NOT show an improvement in rural areas from the National Family Health Survey 4 to National Family Health Survey 5 ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Reports and Index Why in news? The sixth round of the National Family Health Survey (NFHS-6) is being conducted in India between 2023 and 2024. Explanation: NFHS provides high quality, reliable and comparable data on population dynamics and health indicators as well as data on emerging issues in health and family welfare and associated domains, so as to assist the policy- makers and programme implementing agencies for setting the benchmarks 1. Infant mortality rate: This has shown improvement, decreasing from 46.0 per 1,000 live births in NFHS-4 to 38.4 in NFHS-5. 2. Obesity in women: The data indicates that the percentage of women with a Body Mass Index (BMI) below normal decreased from 26.7% in NFHS-4 to 21.2% in NFHS-5, suggesting an improvement in nutrition and health, but it doesn't directly address obesity rates. 3. Anaemia in children: The prevalence of anaemia among children aged 6-59 months increased from 59.5% in NFHS-4 to 68.3% in NFHS-5, indicating a deterioration rather than an improvement. 4. Total fertility rate: The total fertility rate decreased from 2.4 children per woman in NFHS-4 to 2.1 in NFHS-5, showing improvement. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which of the following statements with regard to the Anoop Barenwal v. Union of India ( ) case is/are correct? 1. The case was heard by a Constitutional Bench 2. According to the judgment, the grounds for removal of Election Commissioners shall be the same as the Chief Justice of India Select the answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Polity Why in news? The Supreme Court in January 2024 rejected a request for a interim stay to the new law that removes the Chief Justice of India from the panel responsible for selecting the Chief Election Commissioner and election commissioners. Explanation: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution states that the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) and Election Commissioners (ECs) are appointed by the President, subject to parliamentary law. In Anoop Baranwal vs Union of India – a judgment delivered on 2nd March 2023 – a Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court unanimously held that the selection of the Chief Election Commissioner and the Election Commissioners would be done by a three-member Committee consisting of the Prime Minister, the Leader of the Opposition (or the leader of the largest opposition party in Parliament), and the Chief Justice of India. Hence, 1 is correct. The Court, thus, altered the mode of selection, where the CEC is appointed by the President, acting on the advice of the Prime Minister. The judgment did not established that the grounds for removing Election Commissioners. Hence, 2 is not correct. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which of the following statements with regard to National Logistics Policy (NLP) is NOT correct ?
o: NLP would improve the competitiveness of Indian goods
NLP would enhance economic growth and increase employment opportunities
NLP would provide an opportunity for deleveraging balance sheets and providing fiscal space for investment in new infrastructure assets
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Economy Explanation: The National Logistics Policy (NLP) was launched by the Government of India in 2022. The policy aims to streamline and enhance the efficiency of logistics in India, reducing costs and improving the movement of goods across the country. The primary objectives of the NLP include reducing logistics costs, improving efficiency, and fostering a more integrated logistics ecosystem in India. It aims to create new job opportunities in the logistics sector and related industries. Deleveraging balance sheets typically refers to reducing debt levels on the balance sheets of companies or government entities. While NLP aims to improve logistics infrastructure and efficiency, it does not directly focus on deleveraging balance sheets or providing fiscal space for investment. The policy is more about optimizing logistics rather than financial restructuring or balance sheet management. Hence, Statement 4 is not correct. Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Consider the following statements about the emblem of the Milano Cortina Winter Olympic and Paralympic Games : 1. The name of the emblem is 'Futura' 2. The emblem of the Games was decided by a popular vote for the first time in the history of the Olympic Which of the above statements is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Sports Why in news? The 2026 Winter Olympics and also known as Milano Cortina 2026 is an upcoming international multi-sport event scheduled to take place in 2026 in three regions, with the Italian cities of Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo hosting. Explanation: This will be the fourth Olympic Games hosted in Italy, which previously hosted the 1956 Winter Olympics in Cortina d'Ampezzo, the 1960 Summer Olympics in Rome, and the 2006 Winter Olympics in Turin. The official emblem for the Milano Cortina 2026 Games is indeed named "Futura" . The emblem was chosen through a public vote, marking the first instance in Olympic history where the public participated in selecting the Games' logo. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: "Mission Shakti" (DRDO) is the name given by India to
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Science and Technology Why in news? The DRDO's Mission Shakti was a successful anti-satellite (ASAT) missile test that took place on March 27, 2019. Explanation: Mission Shakti was an anti-satellite (ASAT) missile test conducted by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) on March 27, 2019, where a satellite in low Earth orbit was targeted and destroyed. Mission Shakti was undertaken to use rocket to destroy an orbiting satellite. The successful mission demonstrated DRDO’s technical prowess and ability to defend country’s assets in space, the 4th dimension of warfare. The mission was one of the most complex operation under taken by DRDO in which a missile launched from ground had to hit and neutralise with pinpoint accuracy a fast-moving satellite in orbit hundreds of kilometres away. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which one among the following statements with regard to the United Nations Climate Change Conference ( ) is NOT correct?
o: The 'Global Stocktake' is the central outcome of
It recognized the need to limit global warming to
It directed the developing countries to lead the transition away from fossil fuel
It emphasized accelerated phasedown of coal-power
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Environment Why in news? In December 2023, the United Nations Climate Change conference, COP28, has concluded with a historic agreement to transition away from fossil fuels. Explanation: The major outcome from COP 28 included the decision on Outcome of the First Global Stocktake. Another major outcome of COP 28 is the agreement on the operationalization of the Loss and Damage Fund and its funding arrangements. After much deliberations, the final agreement called upon countries to contribute towards “transitioning away” from fossil fuels which is to achieve net zero by 2050”. COP28 also includes accelerating efforts towards the phase-down of unabated coal power, phasing out inefficient fossil fuel subsidies. In a major step forward, Parties agreed on targets for the Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) and its framework, which identify where the world needs to get to in order to be resilient to the impacts of a changing climate and to assess countries’ efforts. To limit the global temperature increase to well below 2°C above pre-industrial levels, while pursuing efforts to restrict it to 1.5°C was the goal of COP21 in Paris in 2015. Hence, b is not correct. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following statements with regard to the outcomes of the talks between the Prime Minister of India and the President of UAE held in February, is/are correct? 1. Both countries signed an agreement on inter-linking of domestic debit/credit cards 2. An MoU was signed on cooperation in digital infrastructure projects 3. Both countries agreed that a Bilateral Investment Treaty shall be signed in the next meeting at the ministerial level. Select the answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: International Relations Why in news? Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi officaillyl visited to the United Arab Emirates (UAE) from 13 to 14 February 2024. Explanation: During his visit to UAE, PM Narendra Modi met UAE’s President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi. During this meeting, India and the UAE signed eight pacts to bolster cooperation in key areas. Agreement on inter-linking domestic debit/credit cards - RuPay (India) with JAYWAN (UAE) to enhance the universal acceptance of RuPay across the UAE. Hence, 1 is correct. Bilateral Investment Treaty was signed between two countries. This agreement will be a key enabler for further promoting investments in both countries. Hence, 3 is not correct. A memorandum of understanding (MoU) has also been signed on cooperation in the field of electrical interconnection and trade. MoU on cooperation in Digital Infrastructure Projects which will create a framework for a wide-ranging cooperation, including investment cooperation, in the digital infrastructure sector. Hence, 2 is correct. Agreement on interlinking of the instant payment platforms - UPI (India) and AANI (UAE). This follows the MoU on interlinking payment and messaging systems signed in July 2023 during Hon’ble PM’s visit to Abu Dhabi. MoU for cooperation in the field of heritage and museums was also | | Defence signed. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which of the following countries chaired the 43rd ASEAN Summit?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Summits and Conferences Why in news? The 43 ASEAN Summit was held at the Jakarta Convention Center (JCC) on 5-7 September 2023. Explanation: The 43rd Association of Southeast Asian Nations Summit concluded in Jakarta, Indonesia, with the adoption of the ASEAN Leaders’ Declaration on ASEAN as an Epicentrum of Growth. About ASEAN: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) is a regional grouping that aims to promote economic and security cooperation among its ten members: Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam. India including the S., China, Japan, and Australia are its dialogue partners. It was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by the founding fathers of ASEAN: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. Secretariat: Jakarta, Indonesia. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: The National Council of Science Museums (NCSM), which is developing Science Centres/Museums/ Innovation Hubs in India, works under
o: The Ministry of Science and Technology
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Art and Culture Why in news? The National Council of Science Museums (NCSM) signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the Arunachal Pradesh government for setting up a Science Centre and Digital Planetarium in January 2024. Explanation: National Council of Science Museums (NCSM) is an autonomous scientific organization functioning under the Ministry of Culture, Government of India for science communication through its network of science museums or science centres spread across India. It is the largest chain of science centers/museums under a single administrative umbrella in the world. There are 24 own science centers or museums and one R & D laboratory and training centre. The NCSM has been built to co-ordinate all informal science communication activities in the museum space in the country. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following is NOT one of the pillars of India's 'Foreign Trade Policy- ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Economy Why in news? Foreign Trade Policy 2023 (FTP 2023) of India was launched on 31st March 2023 by the Union Ministry of Commerce and Industry. Explanation: Foreign Trade Policy (2023) is a policy document which is based on continuity of time-tested schemes facilitating exports as well as a document which is nimble and responsive to the requirements of trade. It is based on principles of ‘trust’ and ‘partnership’ with exporters. The Key Approach to the policy is based on these 4 pillars: (i) Incentive to Remission (ii) Export promotion through collaboration - Exporters, States, Districts, Indian Missions, (iii) Ease of doing business, reduction in transaction cost and e-initiatives (iv) Emerging Areas – E-Commerce Developing Districts as Export Hubs and streamlining SCOMET policy. The FTP 2023 aims at process re- engineering and automation to facilitate ease of doing business for exporters. It also focuses on emerging areas like dual use high end technology items under SCOMET, facilitating e-commerce export, collaborating with States and Districts for export promotion. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which of the following statements about 'Vibrant Village Programme' of the Government of India is/are correct? 1. It aims at comprehensive development of the identified villages 2. Fairs, festivals, sports meet etc. are organized under this programme Select the answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Government Schemes Why in news? In July 2024, Government of India reviewed the progress and pushed to expedite and assess the development initiatives under the Vibrant Villages Program for border villages. | | Defence Explanation: Vibrant Villages Programme (VVP) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme launched on 15th February,2023 for comprehensive development of the select villages in 46 blocks in 19 districts of abutting northern border in the States of Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Sikkim, Uttarakhand and UT of Ladakh. It is a centrally sponsored scheme. The scheme aims at comprehensive development of the identified villages. Hence, statement 1 is correct. It aids to identify and develop the economic drivers based on local natural human and other resources of the border villages on the northern border and development of growth centres on “Hub and Spoke Model'' through promotion of social entrepreneurship, empowerment of youth and women through skill development and entrepreneurship. One of the sectors identified for development is promotion of tourism and culture by augmentation of various tourism related infrastructure, promoting community managed home stays, organizing local fairs & festivals, promotion of eco-tourism, agro- tourism, wellness, wildlife, spiritual& adventure tourism, promotion of local cuisines, etc. Hence, 2 is correct. VVP has been conceived as an outcome oriented programme with outcome indicators at three levels- village, household & individual beneficiary Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Who, amongst the following, has won International Emmy Award- for 'Best Actor'?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prize Why in news? The 51st International Emmy Awards ceremony, presented by the International Academy of Television Arts and Sciences (IATAS), took place on November 20, 2023, in New York City. Explanation: The winner of the International Emmy Award 2023 for Best Actor is Martin Freeman for his role in The Responder. He won the award, defeating nominees including Jim Sarbh, who was nominated for Rocket Boys. Other categories of Emmy Awards: Best Drama Series: “The Empress” Best Actress: Karla Souza in “La Caida” (Dive) Best Actor: Martin Freeman in “The Responder” Best TV Movie/Mini-Series: “La Caida” (Dive) Best Non-Scripted Entertainment: “A Ponte – The Bridge Brasil” Best Short-Form Series: “Des Gens Bien Ordinaires” (A Very Ordinary World) Best Live-Action – For Kids: “Heartbreak High” Best Factual And Entertainment For Kids: “Built To Survive” Best Documentary: “Mariupol: The People’s Story” Best Sports Documentary: “Harley and Katya” Best Arts Programming: “Buffy Sainte- Marie: Carry It On” Best Telenovela: “Yargi” (Family Secrets) Best Animation for Kids: “The Smeds and The Smoos” Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Which of the following sectors have been identified as 'critical 'sectors' by the National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre? 1. Power and Energy 2. Industry 3. Banking and Financial services 4. Irrigation 5. Education Select the answer using the code given below :
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Economy Explanation: The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) has identified several sectors as 'critical' for national security and economic stability. The critical sectors include: Power & Energy Banking, Financial Services & Insurance Telecommunications Transport Government Strategic & Public Enterprises Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which one among the following Indian PSUs has been declared as the winner at the Brandon Hall Group's Excellence in Technology Award- ?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prizes Why in news? NTPC, India's largest integrated power company, has been declared as the winner at the Brandon Hall Group's Excellence in Technology Awards 2023. Explanation: NTPC was the Indian PSU that won multiple awards at the Brandon Hall Group's Excellence in Technology Awards 2023, including two silver awards and two gold awards: Gold Award for Developing Leaders of Tomorrow Gold Award for Best Results in acquiring Certificate of Proficiency for Boiler Operation Engineers Silver Award for Designing an Individual Centric Health Care Ecosystem Silver Award for Technological solution based on AR/VR for augmenting workforce capacity Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: JAYWAN is the domestic Debit Card of which of the following countries?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: International Relations Why in news? Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi officaillyl visited to the United Arab Emirates (UAE) from 13 to 14 February 2024. Explanation: During his visit to UAE, PM Narendra Modi met UAE’s President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi. During this meeting, India and the UAE signed eight pacts to bolster cooperation in key areas. One of the agreements is: Interlinking Domestic Debit/Credit Cards (RuPay and JAYWAN): Both countries signed a pact on interlinking domestic debit/ credit cards — RuPay (India) with JAYWAN (UAE). It is an important step in building financial sector cooperation and it will enhance the universal acceptance of RuPay across the UAE. UAE’s domestic card JAYWAN is based on the digital RuPay credit and debit card stack. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Consider the following statements : 1. The Finance Commission used fiscal effort as a criterion for horizontal devolution unlike the Finance Commission 2. Both the and the Finance Commission used pre demographic variables as a criteria for horizontal devolution Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Polity Why in news? The Fifteenth Finance Commission was constituted on November 27, 2017 and made recommendations covering the period of six years valid upto the financial year 2025-26. Explanation: The 15th Finance Commission was chaired under N.K. Singh. The 15th Finance Commission introduced the "Tax Effort" criterion, which was not emphasized in the same way by the 14th Finance Commission. The 15th Finance Commission computed this by considering the 2011 population metric along with tax revenue efficiency, marking a shift from previous practices. Hence, 1 is correct. This criterion has been used to reward states with higher tax collection efficiency. It is measured as the ratio of the average per capita own tax revenue and the average per capita state GDP during the three years between 2016-17 and 2018-19. The Terms of Reference of the Commission required it to use the population data of 2011 while making recommendations. 15th Finance Commission used the 2011 population as a criterion for horizontal devolution, along with other factors such as income, area, forest and ecology, demographic performance, and tax effort whereas the 14th Finance Commission utilized data from the 1971 Census. Hence, 2 is not correct. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Rashtriya Vigyan Puraskar, 2024' under the category of Vigyan Ratna Award' was given to
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Person in news Explanation: The Rashtriya Vigyan Puraskar, 2024 under the category of Vigyan Ratna Award was awarded to Prof. Govindarajan Padmanabhan Prof. Govindarajan Padmanabhan is a pioneer in molecular biology and biotechnology research in India, notably recognized for his work on malaria parasites. He has been honored with prestigious awards like the Padma Shri and Padma Bhushan previously. The Vigyan Ratna Award is given for lifetime contributions in the field of Science and Technology. In the first edition of the Rashtriya Vigyan Puraskar 2024, Prof. Padmanabhan received the top category Vigyan Ratna Award. The awards ceremony was held at Rashtrapati Bhavan with the President of India Smt. Droupadi Murmu presenting the awards. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: The 2024 ОРCW (Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons) - The Hague Award has been conferred upon which one of the following organizations?
o: The National Authority Chemical Weapons Convention
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Awards and Prizes Explanation: The 2024 OPCW (Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons) - The Hague Award has been conferred upon the Indian Chemical Council (ICC). The award was presented during the 29th Session of the Conference of the States Parties to the Chemical Weapons Convention at The Hague on November 25, 2024. This is the first time the award has recognized the efforts of a chemical industry body. The ICC represents over 80% of the Indian chemical industry and has played a critical role in promoting chemical safety, compliance with the Chemical Weapons Convention (CWC), and enhancing industry-wide chemical security. ICC initiatives like the Chemical Weapons Convention Helpdesks, Nicer Globe (real-time monitoring and emergency response for chemical transportation), and the Responsible Care program have been key in advancing safe and responsible practices in the industry. The OPCW-The Hague Award was established in 2014 to recognize significant contributions towards the global effort to eliminate chemical weapons. The OPCW was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2013 for its efforts to eliminate chemical weapons. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which among the following statements with regard to Grameen Bharat Mahotsav, 2025 is/are correct? 1. It aims at promoting economic stability and financial security among rural population. 2. It focuses on financial inclusion and supporting sustainable agricultural practices. Select the answer using the code given below.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: National news Explanation: The Grameen Bharat Mahotsav 2025 was inaugurated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi aiming to build a resilient rural India aligned with the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. Its objectives include: Promoting economic stability and financial security among the rural population, especially focusing on rural entrepreneurship and self-reliance. Focusing on financial inclusion and supporting sustainable agricultural practices, including workshops on organic farming, rural women entrepreneurship, and | | Defence development of agriculture in the North-East. The event also aims to foster innovation, enhance rural infrastructure, empower women, and showcase India’s rich cultural heritage. It is jointly organized by the Department of Financial Services and NABARD, among other stakeholders. The Mahotsav runs from January 4 to 9, 2025. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: India and Poland have agreed to implement the India-Poland Strategic Partnership' in terms of a Five-Year Action Plan. Identify the current Five-Year Action Plan.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: International Relations Explanation: India and Poland agreed to elevate their bilateral relations to a Strategic Partnership during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Poland in August 2024. Following this, both sides formulated and agreed to execute a Five-Year Action Plan for 2024-2028. The plan focuses on multiple areas including political dialogue, defense and security cooperation, trade and investment, climate and technology, transport and connectivity, and counter- terrorism. It also includes priority cooperation in high-tech sectors, renewable energy, cybersecurity, space exploration, and social security agreements. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: 'Collaboration for the Intelligent Age' was the theme of a meeting convened in 2025 under the aegis of:
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Summits and Conferences Explanation: The theme "Collaboration for the Intelligent Age" was the theme of the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting 2025 held in Davos, Switzerland. This meeting brought together global leaders from government, business, and civil society to discuss pressing global issues under this theme, emphasizing cooperation, technology, sustainability, and inclusive growth in the emerging intelligent age driven by artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: India's key climate targets include 1. Reduction of 33%-35% in greenhouse gas emissions intensity by 2030 2. Increase in non-fossil fuel energy capacity to 500 GW 3. Achieving net-zero emissions by 2070 Select the answer using the code given below.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Environment Explanation: The Global Biofuels Alliance (GBA) to be led by India, Brazil and the United States, as leading biofuel producers and consumers, will greatly help achieve the MDG Goals of India turning Net Zero by 2070. India stands at the forefront of addressing the global challenge of Climate Change and has committed to an ambitious Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) of reducing emission intensity by 33-35% in 2030 against the levels of year 2005. India is set to achieve its short term and long term targets under the Panchamrit action plan, like- reaching a non-fossil fuel energy capacity of 500 GW by 2030; fulfilling at least half of its energy requirements via renewable energy by 2030; reducing CO2 emissions by 1 billion tons by 2030. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which of the statements given below is/are correct? 1. The National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) was approved by the Union Cabinet, Government of India, in the year 2023. 2. The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Government of India, has launched the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme of India. Select the answer using the code given below.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Environment Explanation: The National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) was approved by the Union Cabinet in January 2023. The mission aims to make India a global hub for the production, usage, and export of green hydrogen, with a target to produce 5 million tonnes per annum of green hydrogen by 2030. It includes various components for demand creation, electrolyser manufacturing incentives, pilot projects, and green hydrogen hubs However, the Green Hydrogen Certification Scheme was launched by the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE), not the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: Which of the following statements with regard to the National Manufacturing Mission' is/are correct? 1. It covers small, medium and large industries. 2. It will support cleantech manufacturing. 3. It will facilitate promotion of employment opportunities in labour-intensive sectors. Select the answer using the code given below.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Government Schemes Explanation: The National Manufacturing Mission (NMM), announced in the Union Budget 2025-26, supports industries of all sizes— small, medium, and large—to further the "Make in India" initiative and strengthen domestic manufacturing. Supports cleantech manufacturing: The mission prioritizes cleantech manufacturing, fostering the domestic production ecosystem for solar PV cells, EV batteries, electrolyzers for hydrogen production, wind turbines, and other clean energy components. This is aligned with India’s climate goals and renewable energy transition. Facilitates employment in labour- intensive sectors: The mission includes provisions to promote employment and entrepreneurship in labour-intensive sectors like footwear, leather products, and toys. Specific schemes aim to enhance productivity, quality, competitiveness, and job creation in these areas Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: Which among the following States is not a part of the Purvodaya development initiative of the Government of India?
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Polity Explanation: The Purvodaya initiative, launched primarily by the Ministry of Steel and supported by the Union Budget 2024, targets the eastern states of India focused on boosting infrastructure, industrial growth, and human resource development. The five states officially included in the Purvodaya scheme are: Bihar Jharkhand West Bengal | | Defence Odisha Andhra Pradesh Although Chhattisgarh is an important mineral-rich state, it is not included in the Purvodaya development initiative focusing on the eastern region's revival. The initiative aims to leverage the mineral wealth, cultural heritage, and industrial potential of these five eastern states to transform the region into a major economic powerhouse by 2047. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct about recently elected Chancellor of Germany Mr. Friedrich Merz? 1. He lost in the first round of voting. 2. His coalition includes Christian Democratic Union and Christian Social Union. Select the answer using the code given below.
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Person in News Explanation: Friedrich Merz, the newly elected Chancellor of Germany, failed to secure the required absolute majority of votes in the first round of parliamentary voting on May 6, 2025. He received 310 votes but needed 316, marking the first time in post-war German history that a chancellor candidate did not win in the initial round. His coalition includes Christian Democratic Union (CDU) and Christian Social Union (CSU):Merz leads the CDU, which forms a coalition with the CSU, its Bavarian sister party. The coalition government also involved the Social Democratic Party (SPD) to form a majority and enable his appointment as Chancellor after the second vote. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: Which one of the following statements with reference to Jal Jeevan Mission is not correct?
o: It was established in the year 2016
It aims to improve quality of life in rural areas
It is to provide functional tap water connections to every rural household
It is a centrally sponsored scheme
subject: currentaff
exp: Key Concept: Schemes Explanation: The Jal Jeevan Mission was launched on 15 August 2019 by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, not in 2016. It is a centrally sponsored scheme aimed at providing functional household tap water connections (FHTCs) to every rural household by 2024, ensuring safe and adequate drinking water supply. The mission focuses on improving the quality of life in rural areas through clean tap water supply, significantly reducing collection time and improving health outcomes. It has a funding pattern between Centre and States, typically 50:50 for most states, 90:10 for Himalayan and North- Eastern states, and 100% for Union Territories, confirming its nature as a centrally sponsored scheme Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: The NISAR Mission is a joint collaboration between ISRO and which other space agency?
subject: currentaff
exp: NISAR is a joint Earth-observing mission between NASA and the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO). NASA and ISRO are providing two radars that are optimized each in their way to allow the mission to observe a wider range of changes than either one alone. NISAR will detect movements of the planet’s surface as small as 0.4 inches over areas about half the size of a tennis court. Hence, the correct answer is NASA

q: Narain Karthikeyan is a sportsman in the field of?
subject: currentaff
exp: Narain Karthikeyan is a pioneering Indian motorsport athlete best known as India’s first Formula One (F1) driver. He made his F1 debut in 2005 with the Jordan Grand Prix team, marking a historic moment for Indian motorsport. During his F1 career, he competed in 48 Grands Prix, starting 46 races, and scored a total of five championship points, notably finishing fourth at the 2005 United States Grand Prix—a race famous for its unusual circumstances where only six cars competed due to tire safety concerns Hence, the correct answer is d.

q: From which Country did India procure the aircraft carrier Admiral Gorshkov, to join Indian Navy as INS Vikramaditya?
subject: currentaff
exp: India procured the aircraft carrier Admiral Gorshkov from Russia, where it was originally built and served in the Soviet and later Russian Navy before being extensively refurbished and commissioned into the Indian Navy as INS Vikramaditya. India entered into negotiations with Russia and, after several years, finalized a deal in 2004 for the purchase and extensive refurbishment of the vessel. Hence, the correct answer is a.

q: The World Economic Outlook Report is published by?
subject: currentaff
exp: The World Economic Outlook (WEO) is a flagship report of the International Monetary Fund. It provides detailed analysis and projections of the global economy, including: GDP growth forecasts Inflation trends Trade balances Policy challenges for member countries The WEO is published twice a year — typically in April and October. Hence, the correct answer is c.

q: MALABAR is a Naval exercise between India and:
subject: currentaff
exp: Exercise MALABAR is a naval exercise that originally began in 1992 as a bilateral exercise between India and the United States. Over time, it has expanded to include Japan and Australia as permanent partners, but the foundational partnership is between India and the USA. Hence, the correct answer is b.

q: In India, Planning Commission was set up in year _______.
subject: economy
exp: The Planning Commission of India was established in 1950 by a resolution of the Government of India under the leadership of Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru. Its primary purpose was to promote a rapid rise in the standard of living of the Indian people by efficiently exploiting the country's resources, increasing production, and offering opportunities to all for employment in the service of the community. Hence, the correct answer is option C.

q: Which of the following is NOT a Maharatna company?
subject: economy
exp: Hindustan Copper Limited is not a Maharatna Company. Hindustan Copper Limited (HCL): HCL is a Schedule 'A' Miniratna Category - I Central Public Sector Enterprise. It operates under the administrative control of the Ministry of Mines, Government of India. Incorporated on 9th November 1967. Maharatna Status Eligibility Criteria: To qualify as a Maharatna CPSE, a company must meet one of the following criteria over three years: Average annual net profit of over Rs. 2,500 crore. Average annual turnover of Rs. 20,000 crore. Average annual net worth of Rs. 10,000 crore. Maharatna status grants certain benefits for investment decisions, including the authority to invest Rs. 1,000 crore - Rs. 5,000 crore in a project or up to 15% of their net worth in a project. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

q: The rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks is called
subject: economy
exp: The rate at which the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) lends money to commercial banks is called the "Repo Rate." Repo stands for Repurchase Agreement. The Repo Rate is the interest rate at which the RBI provides short-term loans to commercial banks by purchasing government securities or other eligible securities from them. It is a crucial tool used by the central bank to regulate liquidity, inflation, and credit conditions in the economy. Hence, the correct answer is option (C).

q: In India, inflation is measured by
o: Consumer price index for agricultural workers
subject: economy
exp: In India, inflation is primarily measured using two main indices: the Consumer Price Index (CPI) and the Wholesale Price Index (WPI). Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures the average change over time in the prices paid by urban and rural consumers for a basket of goods and services, including food, housing, transportation, medical care, etc. CPI is calculated separately for various categories such as CPI for Industrial Workers (CPI-IW), CPI for Agricultural Labourers (CPI-AL), and CPI for Rural Labourers (CPI-RL), among others. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: With reference to Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana, consider the following statements: I. It is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Labour and Employment. II. It was launched in the year 2014. III. Among other things, it will also impart training in soft skills, entrepreneurship, financial and digital literacy. IV. It aims to align the competencies of the unregulated workforce of the country to the National Skill Qualification Framework. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: I) It is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Labour and Employment.This statement is incorrect. - The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is not a scheme under the Ministry of Labour and Employment. Instead, it is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE). II) It was launched in the year 2014, This statement is incorrect. - The Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) was indeed launched on 16th July,2015. III) Among other things, it will also impart training in soft skills, entrepreneurship, financial and digital literacy.This statement is generally correct. - PMKVY aims to provide skill training in various domains, including soft skills, entrepreneurship, financial literacy, and digital skills, among others. IV) It aims to align the competencies of the unregulated workforce of the country to the National Skill Qualification Framework.This statement is generally correct. - The primary aim of PMKVY is to provide skill training and certification that aligns with the National Skill Qualification Framework (NSQF) to enhance the employability of the workforce. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: With reference to the PM CARES Fund, consider the following statements: I. The amount collected by it directly goes to the Consolidated Fund of India. II. It can avail donations from the foreign contribution and donations to fund can also avail 100% tax exemption. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: PM CARES Fund – Under the chairmanship of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the Prime Minister’s Citizen Assistance and Relief in Emergency Situation Fund was created on March 28th, 2020. The Prime Minister’s Citizen Assistance and Relief in Emergency | | Defence Situations Fund popularly known as PM- CARES Fund has been established by the government to address any emergency or distress scenario, including the COVID- 19 pandemic. This Fund was established as a result of several demands from the public to assist the administration in the wake of the COVID-19 emergency. Candidates can know detailed information about the PM CARES Fund on the Official Website (pmindia.gov.in). PM CARES fund is covered under the PM Schemes which is an important static GK topic with regards to UPSC or any other competitive examination. About PM CARES Fund: With the Prime Minister serving as its Chairman, the Fund is a public charitable trust. The ministers of defence, home affairs, and finance are additional members. As a result of the Fund’s ability to accept micro-donations, many people will be able to make donations in even the smallest amounts. The Fund will enhance capabilities for disaster management and promote research on citizen protection. Objectives of PM CARES Fund: To undertake and support relief or assistance of any kind relating to a public health emergency or any other kind of emergency, calamity or distress, either man-made or natural, including the creation or upkeep of healthcare or pharmaceutical facilities, other necessary infrastructure, funding relevant research or any other type of support. To render financial assistance, provide grants of payments of money or take such other steps as may be deemed necessary by the Board of Trustees to the affected population. To undertake any other activity, which is not inconsistent with the above Objects. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: The Mid-Day Meal scheme comes under the purview of which ministry?
o: Ministry of Women and Child Development
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
subject: economy
exp: The Mid-Day Meal scheme is a school meal program designed to improve the nutritional status of school children and encourage school attendance. It falls under the Ministry of Education (formerly known as the Ministry of Human Resource Development) in India. The scheme aims to provide cooked meals to children in government schools and government-aided schools across the country, with the objective of boosting school attendance, retention, and the overall health and nutrition of students. The Mid-Day Meal Scheme in India was indeed started on August 15, 1995, but it was initially known as the 'National Programme of Nutritional Support to Primary Education.' This program was later renamed and expanded into what is now commonly referred to as the Mid-Day Meal Scheme. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: "Global Competitiveness Report" is released by which of the following organization?
subject: economy
exp: WEF stands for the World Economic Forum. The World Economic Forum is an international organization that brings together leaders from various sectors, including business, government, academia, and civil society, to engage in discussions and collaborations aimed at | | Defence addressing global challenges and shaping economic agendas. Key aspects of the World Economic Forum include: 1. Annual Meeting: The WEF is best known for its annual meeting in Davos, Switzerland, where leaders gather to discuss and deliberate on pressing global issues. The meeting provides a platform for dialogue and collaboration across different sectors. 2. Reports and Research: The WEF conducts research and publishes reports on a wide range of topics, including global competitiveness, technology, sustainability, and more. These reports often provide insights into key trends and challenges facing the world. 3. Global Initiatives: The WEF initiates and supports global projects and initiatives aimed at finding solutions to complex issues. These initiatives often involve public-private partnerships and collaboration between various stakeholders. 4. Public-Private Cooperation: The organization emphasizes the importance of public-private cooperation in addressing global challenges. It seeks to foster dialogue and collaboration between governments, businesses, and other stakeholders to achieve positive societal impact. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Which of the following are commercial banks assets?
subject: economy
exp: A bank can have different types of assets, including physical assets, such as equipment and land; loans, including interest from consumer and business loans; reserves, or holdings of deposits of the central bank and vault cash; and investments, or securities. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Which of the following is an example of Mixed Economy?
subject: economy
exp: The coexistence of both public and private sectors within an economy is a characteristic of a mixed economy. So, "Public & Private Sector" would indeed be an example of a mixed economy. A mixed economy features a combination of both public (government) and private sector enterprises, allowing for both state intervention and free- market principles, aiming to balance economic efficiency with social welfare. Hence, the correct answer is option (C).

q: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is based on which of the following factors?
subject: economy
exp: National Income: GDP is a key component of calculating national income, as it represents the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders. Per Capita Income: While not directly defining GDP, dividing the total GDP by the population gives you the per capita income, which is often used to compare living standards across countries. Purchasing Power Parity (PPP): When comparing GDP between countries, PPP is often used to adjust for differences in the cost of living, providing a more accurate picture of relative economic output. While GDP is primarily calculated based on the total value of goods and services produced within a country, it is interconnected with concepts like national income, per capita income, and purchasing power parity, making all of these factors relevant to understanding GDP. Hence, the correct answer is option (D).

q: As of February 2025, what was the reverse repo rate in India, which is set by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to regulate liquidity in the economy?
subject: economy
exp: As of the RBI's February 7, 2025, policy announcement, the reverse repo rate in India remained unchanged at 3.35%. In detail: What is the reverse repo rate? The reverse repo rate is the interest rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks by holding the government securities as collateral. Why is it important? The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) uses the reverse repo rate as a tool to manage liquidity in the financial system and regulate the flow of money in the economy. Recent Changes: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) reduced the repo rate by 25 basis points to 6.25% on February 7, 2025. However, the reverse repo rate remained unchanged at 3.35%. Other policy rates: Repo Rate: 6.25% Bank Rate: 6.50% Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Rate: 6.50% Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) Rate: 6.00% Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: Which of the following are considered legal tender in India? 1. Bank notes 2. Coins 3. Cheques 4. e-Rupee 5. Bitcoins Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
subject: economy
exp: In India, bank notes, coins, and the e-Rupee (Central Bank Digital Currency - CBDC) are considered legal tender. Cheques are not legal tender as they are a negotiable instrument requiring acceptance. Bitcoins and other cryptocurrencies are not recognized as legal tender in India. Hence the correct answer is option B

q: Which is the first state in India to impose an agriculture tax?
subject: economy
exp: Bihar was the first state in India to impose an agricultural tax. This tax was introduced to generate revenue from agricultural produce, given that agriculture is a significant part of the state's economy. Hence, the correct answer is option (B).

q: What is meant by 'Sex Ratio'?
subject: economy
exp: Sex ratio is defined as the number of females per 1000 males in a given population. In a society that has males and females equal in number, the sex ratio is 1:1 or 1000 females for every 1000 males. This ratio is an important indicator of gender balance in population. Historically there were more females than males in most countries of the world. This phenomena occurred due to two reasons: i) Girl babies enjoy better immune system and resistant to diseases in comparison of male child. ii) Females live longer than males in most of the societies. Hence, the correct answer is Option B i.e Number of females per 1000 males.

q: Which one of the following is not a part of the service sector in India?
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Construction is a part of the manufacturing sector and not the service sector. Explanation- Construction is a part of the manufacturing sector, which is a sector which derives its raw materials from the primary sector. Various raw materials such as limestone, sandstone, cement, plaster etc are derived from the primary sector.
