Transport, hotels and restaurants and insurance are part of the service sector, which produces services rather than goods. Hence, options a, c and d are incorrect. Hence the correct option is ‘B’ i.e Construction.

q: Rise in the price of a commodity means
o: Rise in the value of currency only
Fall in the value of currency only
Rise in the value of commodity only
Fall in the value of currency and rise in the value of commodity
subject: economy
exp: Key concept:- Inflation-Inflation refers to the increase in the price of most goods and services that are routinely or commonly used. Food, clothing, housing, recreation, transportation, essentials, etc. Inflation measures the change in the average price of a basket of goods and services over time. Explanation:- As the demand for goods and services increases, the prices of goods and services increase, and raw materials are used to produce those goods and services. As the demand for a product increases, so does the price of the product. Hence, the correct answer is Option D i.e., fall in the value of currency and rise in the value of commodity

q: What is meant by 'Public Good'?
o: A commodity produced by the Government.
A commodity whose benefits are indivisibly spread among the entire community.
A Government scheme that benefits the poor households.
Any commodity that is very popular.
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Public good are the type of goods whose benefits are spread among the entire community and are provided free of cost by the government. Explanation- Public goods are provided by the government free of cost for the welfare of the public at large. These goods are also called collective or social goods. People cannot be stopped from using them just because they failed to pay for them, i.e, these goods are both are non- excludable and non-rivalrous. Hence the correct option is ‘b’ i.e, a commodity whose benefits are indivisibly spread among the entire community.

q: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?
o: India is the third largest importer of the defence equipment.
India meets more than 50 per cent of its defence equipment through imports.
Defence budget for 2015-2016 accounts for less than 2 per cent of the GDP.
Certain financial powers are delegated to the Vice Chief of Army Staff for capital acquisitions.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: India tops the list of the largest buyer or importers of weapons / defence equipment by accounting for approximately 11 percent of the Global Market. Explanation: In recent researches, the biggest defense equipments exporter to India was France, which 36 percent of the year's total defense import. Russia followed closely by exporting around 35 percent of the Year total defense import. Hence, the correct option is Option (B) i.e, India meets more than 50 per cent of its defence equipment through imports. Additional Information: After India, Saudi Arabia follows closely in the list of largest importers of arms by accounting for 11 percent market share from 2017 to 2022.

q: Which of the following phrases were used in the theme of the Twelfth Five-Year Plan (2012- 17)? 1. Inclusive 2. Faster 3. More inclusive 4. Sustainable 5. More sustainable Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
subject: economy
exp: Explanation Planning Commission It was established on March 15, 1950 and was chaired by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru. The Planning Commission of India was an organization of the Government of India which, among other things, formulated India's five-year plans(there were 12 Five Years Plans till 2014). The Planning Commission was charged with serving all community service employment opportunities. The Planning Commission did not derive its origin from either the Constitution or the Statute but it was formed through Cabinet’s decision. Its objective was to promote a rapid rise in the standard of living of Indians by productively utilizing the country's resources, increasing production and providing opportunities for everyone for employment in the service of society. Slogan for 12th Five Year Plan "Faster, Sustainable, and More Inclusive Growth." It was replaced by NITI Aayog, a more robust organization, on 1 January 2015. It is more of a think tank that works with stakeholders to develop the country. Hence, the correct answer C, i.e 2, 3 and 4

q: Which of the following statements about the New Development Bank is/are correct? 1. The Headquarters of the Bank is situated in Moscow, Russia. 2. K. V. Kamath was the first President of the Bank. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The headquarters of the New Development Bank BRICS are located in Shanghai, China. Explanation: The headquarters of NBD was officially inaugurated on the 9th of December 2004 in Shanghai, China Mr. K.V. Kamath from India was the first President of the New Development Bank. He served the bank from 2015 to 2020. Overview: Formation: Established at the 6th BRICS Summit in Fortaleza, Brazil, 2014 Headquarters: Shanghai, China Observer Status: Received in the United Nations General Assembly in 2018 Objectives: Foster development in member countries Support economic growth Promote competitiveness and job creation Build a knowledge-sharing platform among developing countries Support public and private projects through loans, guarantees, equity participation, and other financial instruments Recent Proposal: India's Proposal: Expand NDB’s scope to include strengthening social infrastructure (e.g., healthcare, education, housing) alongside promoting industry Major Projects Funded in India: Mumbai Metro Rail Delhi-Ghaziabad-Meerut Regional Rapid Transit System Various Renewable Energy Projects Total Approved Projects: 14 projects amounting to nearly USD 4.2 billion Recent Developments: 2020 Loan Pact: India secured a USD 1 billion loan from NDB to boost rural employment and infrastructure Hence, the correct answer is Option (B).

q: Which of the following statements in the context of income-environment relationship is/are correct? 1. Pollution trends tend to follow an inverse-U shaped relationship across different stages of economic development. 2. In the beginning stage, pollution increases due to urbanization and industrialization. 3. In the later stage, pollution declines due to structural changes towards services sector. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- As there is a structural change in society towards the service sector, the level of pollution caused keeps declining. Explanation- In the early stage of economic development, forests are converted into manufacturing hubs which leads to high environmental degradation and pollution. Service sector causes less pollution than other sectors, thus after the structural change towards the service sector, there is a decline in the level of pollution. Hence the correct option is Option C i.e. 1,2 and 3.

q: Which of the following statements is not true?
o: The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) had regulated global trade since 1947.
GATT was replaced by the World Trade Organisation (WTO) in 1995.
The Most Favoured Nation principle under GATT provided that preferential trading agreements reached with one country should be extended to other countries.
The WTO has been able to cover in its agreements the agriculture and textile sectors which are the principal concerns for the Least Developed Countries (LDCs).
subject: economy
exp: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) and World Trade Organisation(WTO): The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) was signed by 23 countries in October 1947 after World War II and entered into force on January 1, 1948. The purpose of the GATT was to facilitate international trade. The GATT was held eight times in total from April 1947 to December 1993, each with great success and results. In 1995, the GATT was merged into the World Trade Organization (WTO) and the WTO was enlarged. All signatory members of the GATT should be treated equally with all other members. This is known as Most Favored Nation and has carried over to the WTO. The practical result of this is that when a country negotiates a tariff reduction with another country (usually a major trading partner), the same reduction automatically applies to all GATT signatories. The Agreement on Agriculture (AoA), a WTO treaty negotiated during the Uruguay Round of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT), was formally ratified in 1994 in Marrakech, Morocco. It came into effect in 1995. Preferential Trade Agreement (PTA) term used in the WTO for preferential trade such as: reduced or zero tariffs that Member States can unilaterally offer to their trading partners. This includes the Generalized System of Preferences, under which developed countries grant preferential tariffs on imports from developing countries. | | Defence It includes non-reciprocal schemes granted by the General Council. Member States are therefore exempt from the application of the Most Favored Nation (MFN). Hence, the correct answer is option (C).

q: Under flexible exchange rate system, the exchange rate is determined:
o: As a weighted index of a group of currencies
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Flexible exchange rate system is a type of exchange system where the exchange rate is predominantly on demand and supply of money in the market. Explanation- In a flexible exchange rate system, as per the changes in demand and supply of foreign exchange, the currency is allowed to fluctuate freely. There is no involvement of the central bank in this system and neither is there any need of maintaining foreign reserves. Appreciation and Depreciation of currency take place in this type of exchange system. Hence the correct option is Option A i.e. predominantly by market mechanism.

q: Which one of the following is an example of a 'natural monopoly'?
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Delhi Jal Board is an example of natural monopoly and it is the most efficient provider in its industry. Explanation- Natural Monopoly is a type of monopoly where a single company is capable of providing a product or service at a cost which is lower as compared to its competitor in the industry. It arises due to natural market forces. Examples include industries related to electricity and water services. Hence the correct option is Option B i.e. Delhi Jal Board.

q: In view of the fact that kerosene is an inferior good in India, what is/are its implication(s)? 1. As households get richer, they consume less kerosene. 2. Over time there is a decline in quality of kerosene. 3. Government needs to stop subsidies on kerosene. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Inferior goods refer to those type of goods whose demand decreases with increase in income of households. Explanation- Since kerosene is an inferior good, its demand will decrease with the rise of income of households. With the rise in income of households, they can afford other costlier alternatives in place of inferior goods. Thus, Statement 1 is correct. Statements 2 and 3 are incorrect as there is not necessarily a decline of quality of inferior goods after a period of time. Hence the correct answer is Option A i.e 1 only is the correct answer.

q: Which of the following factors led to a decline in inflation rate in India during 2014-2015? 1. Persistent decline in crude oil prices. 2. Softness in global prices of tradables such as edible oils and coal. 3. Tight monetary policy pursued by the Reserve Bank of India. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Persistent decline in crude prices, Softness in global prices of tradables such as edible oils and coal and Tight monetary policy pursued by the RBI are the main factors which led to the decline in inflation rate in India during 2014- 2015. Explanation- Inflation refers to the general increase in prices of goods and services in an economy. Decline in prices of crude oil, edible oil and coal led to control of inflation. RBI through its monetary policy reduced bank rate, CRR and SLR which led to decline of money flow and thus decline of inflation. Hence the correct option is Option D i.e. 1,2 and 3.

q: Private investment in Indian agriculture is mostly on labour-saving mechanization. This could be a response to
o: Rising wages and tighter labour market
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Private investment in agriculture is mostly on labour- saving mechanization due to rising wages of labour and tighter labour market. Explanation- People from rural areas are migrating to cities for better opportunities. This has led to the scarcity of labour. The scarcity of labour has, in turn, led to increasing wages of labour. Hence the correct option is ‘C’ i.e rising wages and tighter labour market.

q: Which one of the following represents a progressive tax structure?
o: Tax rate is the same across all incomes.
Tax rate increases as income increases.
Tax rate decreases as income increases.
Each household pays equal amount of tax.
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- A progressive tax structure is a type of taxing mechanism wherein the tax increases as the income increases. Explanation- The taxing authority in this type of mechanism increases the taxes charged from people as their income increases. High income earners pay more taxes as compared to low income earners in this type of system. This structure is based on the concept of ability to pay. India follows this type of system. Hence, the correct answer is Option B.

q: Inclusion strategy does not focus on:
subject: economy
exp: Key point: Inclusion strategy provides equal access to opportunities and resources for people under marginalized,excluded or deprived sections. Explanation: Inclusion strategy aims to reduce inequality and poverty,diversifying livelihood for tribal population etc.Irrespective of their caste, gender,place of birth and family background. Hence, the correct option is (d).

q: Demand for a commodity refers to
o: Quantity demanded at certain price during any particular period of time.
subject: economy
exp: Explanation Demand of a Commodity Demand for a commodity refers to the quantity of a commodity demanded at a given price and a particular time. Demand for a commodity is the quantity that a consumer can and wants to buy at various prices in a given period of time. Supply of a Commodity The quantity of goods that can be sold at a given price per unit of time. Supply is a flow variable. The supply of goods therefore refers to the quantity of goods sold at a certain price per unit of time. Hence, the correct answer is option D, i.e. Quantity demanded at certain price during any particular period of time.

q: Which one of the following is not an example of externalities?
o: Pollution of air by a cement factory
Health hazard caused to the person due to smoking by himself/herself
Smoke coming out of neighbor's vehicle
Increase in land price of adjacent plots due to construction of a road by the Government
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Externalities occur when production or consumption of a good or service impacts a third party who is not directly related to the production or consumption of that specific good or service. Explanation- The externalities can be both positive or negative. Pollution and smoke are both examples of negative externalities as person causing these do not take into account the indirect costs of those harmed. Hence the correct option is Option D i.e. Increase in land price of adjacent plots due to construction of a road by the Government.

q: When two goods are completely interchangeable, they are
subject: economy
exp: Key concept:- Substitute goods: Substitutes are products that can replace each other in whole or in part. Explanation:- A good that can be used in place of another good that has exactly the same function is said to be a perfect substitute for the other good. In the case of such products, if the price of the product rises, the demand for replacement will increase. For example, if someone gets the same satisfaction and benefits from tea or coffee and can substitute for each other, the two are perfect substitutes. Hence, the correct answer is Option i.e., Perfect substitutes

q: Which of the following statements with regard to the proposed Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank is/are correct? 1. India is one of the founding members of the Bank. 2. The Bank is to be headquartered in Shanghai. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank is a multilateral development bank, Its aim is to improve the economic and social outcomes in Asia. Explanation- India has a shareholding of 7.6% in the bank which is second largest after China. Thus, statement one is correct. The Bank is headquartered in Beijing. Thus, statement 2 is incorrect. Hence the correct option is ‘A’ i.e. 1 only.

q: Which one of the following statements with regard to India's economy between 1814 to 1860 is not correct?
o: Between 1814 and 1850 , four commodities dominated India's exports-raw silk, opium, cotton and indigo.
Between 1814 and 1860 , five commodities dominated India's exports-raw silk, opium, cotton, indigo and jute.
Indigo and raw silk required processing techniques.
Indigo and raw silk were financed by foreign capital.
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Raw silk, opium, cotton and indigo were the four main commodities that dominated India’s exports between 1814 and 1850. Explanation- First Jute mill of India was established in Calcutta in 1855. Thus, it cannot be a dominating commodity of India between 1814 and 1850. Thus, option b is incorrect. Indigo and raw silk neither required processing techniques and were neither financed by foreign capital. Thus options c and d are incorrect. Hence the correct option is Option A i.e. Between 1814 and 1850 , four commodities dominated India's exports-raw silk, opium, cotton and indigo.

q: Shishu, Kishor and Tarun are the schemes of :
o: Micro Units Development & Refinance Agency Ltd. (MUDRA).
Small Industries Development Bank of India.
subject: economy
exp: Key point: Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) Shishu: covers loans upto 50,000/-. Kishor: covers loans above 50,000/- and upto 5 lakh. Tarun: covers loans above 5 lakh and upto 10 lakh Explanation: It was introduced by the government in 2015 for giving loans up to Rs. Ten lakh to the non-corporate and non-farm micro- enterprises. MUDRA stands for Micro Units Development & Refinance Agency Limited is a financial institution set up by Government. Hence, the correct option is Option (B) i.e, Micro Units Development & Refinance Agency Ltd. (MUDRA).

q: In India, the term 'hot money' is used to refer to:
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Hot money is a term used to describe funds which are involved in managing shareholders looking for quick profits. Explanation: An entity located in a different country makes an investment in the form of a controlling ownership stake in a company in another country. Investments made by foreigners in a country's stock or bond markets or through bank deposits are referred to as foreign portfolio investments. The securities or enterprises in FPI are not directly under the investor's control. Typically, FPI is used to refer to hot money. Hence, the correct answer is option (D) i.e, Foreign Portfolio Investment Additional Information: Throughout troubled times, these enormous portfolio transfers may worsen current financial issues.

q: Which of the following is/are example(s) of 'Near Money'? 1 Treasury Bill 2 Credit Card 3 Savings accounts and small time deposits 4 Retail money market mutual funds Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Treasury bill, saving accounts and small time deposits, and retail money market mutual funds are the examples of near money. A credit card allows a user to make a purchase without having to make the payment right away. Explanation: Near Money refers to highly liquid assets that aren't actual money but can be quickly changed into it. Treasury Bills: Treasury Bills is a short-term borrowing tool. T-Bills are issued at a discount to face value; however, upon maturity, the holder receives the face value. Saving accounts and small time deposits: Individuals make deposits into savings accounts and small time deposits in order to save money and earn interest. Retail Money Market Mutual Funds: These are used to temporarily deposit funds. Treasury bills, short-term debt, tax-free bonds, and other securities are included in the money market funds' investment portfolio. Hence, the correct option is Option (D) i.e, 1,3 and 4 only

q: Who among the following is the Chairman of the Fourteenth Finance Commission?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Finance Commission is a constitutional body that is setup under article 280 of the Constitution by the President of India. The body is mandated to define differential relations between the center and the state governments. Explanation: The 14th finance commission was constituted on 2nd January 2013 to make recommendations for a period of 5 years from 2015 to 2020. Former Governor of the Reserve Bank of India, Y.V. Reddy was the chairman of the commission. Hence, the correct answer is Option C i.e, Y V Reddy Additional Information: Other members of the 14th finance commission were M. Govinda Rao, Abhijeet Sen, Sushma Nath, AN Jha and Sudipto Mundle.

q: Which of the following statements are correct ? 1. Ability to pay principle of taxation holds that the amount of taxes people pay should relate to their income or wealth. 2. The Benefit Principle of taxation states that individuals should be taxed in proportion to the benefit they receive from Government programmes. 3. A progressive tax takes a larger share of tax from poor families than it does from rich families. 4. Indirect taxes have the advantage of being cheaper and easier to collect. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Taxes are a required financial burden placed on taxpayers by the government in order to collectively cover public expenses. Explanation: The principle of "ability to pay" believed that those who can pay more taxes should pay more, as measured by their income and wealth. Benefit-based taxation is based on the idea that those who gain more from government spending or expenditures should pay more taxes than those who do not. Indirect taxes are usually levied on the manufacturer or supplier and passes the tax on to the consumer. Indirect taxes are less burdensome for taxpayers and are convenient because they are only paid at the time of purchase. They are cheap and easy to collect. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, 1,2 and 4 only Additional Information: Progressive tax: Very few income earners are subject to a lower tax rate, and more income earners are subject to a higher tax rate. As a result, the average tax rate rises as the taxable amount does. Thus, option 3 is incorrect.

q: Which of the following statement(s) is/are false ? 1. Wage Boards are tripartite in nature, with representatives from workers, employers and independent members. 2. Except for the Wage Boards for Journalists and Non-Journalists, all the other wage boards are statutory in nature. 3. Second National Commission on Labour has recommended against the utility of wage boards. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Wage Boards consist of members who are representatives of workers, employers, and independent members who finalize the recommendations. Journalists and non-journalists news agencies employees have a statutory Wage Board and all the other Wage Boards are non-statutory in nature. Explanation: Wage Boards in India consist of representatives from all three - industry, trade unions and the Government. This way determining the wages of the workers in different categories is easily and correctly done. The board finalizes and rechecks the different parts of wages like HRA, DA, Basic Pay, etc. Hence, the correct option is Option b i.e, 2 only Additional Information: The Second Commission advised that there should be no need for any wage board for finalizing wages for employees or workers in any industry. It said that each state or union territory should have the jurisdiction to fix minimum wages.

q: Capital deepening refers to
o: Going for more fixed capital per worker
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Capital Deepening refers to the rise within the ratio of the capital stock to labour hours (worked). It can also be defined as the circumstance when the economy is experiencing an increase in investment per worker. Explanation: A rise in capacity utilization is another term for this. The variation in invested capital every labour hour is frequently used to gauge capital deepening. The investment required to generate one production unit is known as the capital output ratio. Hence, the correct answer is option (A) i.e, going for more fixed capital per worker Additional Information: With advancements in technology (like a quicker copier) which allow greater productivity per worker, capital deepening often boosts production. In other words, capital deepening raises work efficiency.

q: Which one of the following is not among the aims of the Second Five-Year Plan?
o: Rapid industrialization with particular emphasis on the development of basic and heavy industries
Achieve self-sufficiency in food grains and increase agricultural production to meet the requirements of industry and exports
Reduction of inequalities in income and wealth and a more even distribution of economic power
subject: economy
exp: Explanation Planning Commission It was established on March 15, 1950 and was chaired by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru. The Planning Commission of India was an organization of the Government of India which, among other things, formulated India's five-year plans(there were 12 Five Years Plans till 2014). The second five-year plan followed the Mahalanobis model. The heart of this model followed the pattern of industrial investment shifting towards building a domestic consumer goods sector. The main goal of the Second Five-Year Plan was rapid industrialization and increased productivity through technological modernization. Improving living standards through a significant increase in national income Creating social and economic justice and increasing employment The Planning Commission did not derive its origin from either the Constitution or the Statute but it was formed through Cabinet’s decision. It was replaced by NITI Aayog, a more robust organization, on 1 January 2015. It is more of a think tank that works with stakeholders to develop the country. Hence, the correct answer is option C, i.e. Achieve self-sufficiency in food grains and increase agricultural production to meet the requirements of industry and exports

q: According to the Classical Theory of Employment, deviations from the state of full employment are
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: As per the statement by Classical Economists, the state of unemployment, if occurred, will be a temporary or abnormal condition in the economy and it will occur due to the government's interference into the natural mechanism of the market. Explanation: According to classical economics, over production is a common condition in the economy hence the condition of unemployment does not occur generally. The Classical theory of unemployment is based on the assumptions of interest, prices and flexible wages so, it is clear that interest rates, prices and wages keep changing according to the change in demand and supply. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) i.e, purely temporary in nature

q: Which of the following is/are the example(s) of Transfer Payment(s)? 1 Unemployment Allowance 2 Payment of Salary 3 Social Security Payments 4 Old age Pension Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: A few examples of transfer payments are subsidy, social student grants, social security payments, unemployment allowance and payments made to individuals by the government through any social welfare program. Explanation: Transfer payment is defined as a kind of one way payment which does not involve any kind of transfer of goods or services in it. Unemployment allowance, social security payments and old age payments do not require any kind of exchange of goods or services. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, 1,3 and 4 only Additional information: Payment of salary is done after the individual provides a kind of service to the institution, that's why it is not a type of transfer payment. A few old age pension programs by the Government are: National pension scheme, Atal Pension Yojana.

q: Which one of the following statements is correct with respect to the composition of national income in India?
o: The share of manufacturing sector has declined.
The share of the services sector has increased sharply.
The share of agriculture has remained static.
The share of services sector has declined.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: National income, which includes wages, bonuses, earnings, lease, or tax, is the aggregate of all the earnings that accrue to a nation's population over a given time period. Explanation: India's major industry is the services sector. It makes up 54.40% of India's overall Gross Value Added (2014). The services sector's contribution to the GDP rose from 28.5 percent in 1950–1951 to 39.6 percent in 1990–1991 dollars. Agriculture and associated sectors' share fell to 18.20% in 2013–14. The GDP contribution of the industrial sector is also increasing. Hence, the correct answer is option (B) i.e, The share of the services sector has increased sharply. Additional Information: Overall, the transfer payments and all final items and services that a nation produces in a given year is its national income.

q: Which of the following will be the outcome if an economy is under the inflationary pressure? 1. Domestic currency heads for depreciation. 2. Exports become less competitive with imports getting costlier. 3. Cost of borrowing decreases. 4. Bondholders get benefitted. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Inflation causes the currency of the country to go through depreciation and the cost of borrowing decreases. Explanation: Rise in the normal price of goods due to the demand and supply side pressure is referred to as inflationary pressure. Due to higher incomes, increased production, and increased spending, the overall volume of domestic trade tends to rise during an inflationary period. However, a rise in domestic product prices is likely to hurt the export trade. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) i.e, 1 and 2 only Additional Information: Debtors get profit during inflation period because they repay creditors with the current currency with less worth in terms of purchasing power. Bondholders receive a predetermined sum as interest. This interest, which is declared at the time the bond is issued and is not affected by inflation,

q: Which one of the Five Year Plans had a high priority to bring inflation under control and to achieve stability in the economic situation?
subject: economy
exp: Key point: Our first Prime Minister, Jawaharlal Nehru on 8 December 1951, presented the five-year plan to the Indian Parliament Explanation: Fifth five year plan(1974-1979) laid stress to bring inflation under control to stabilise economic situation focussing on employment, checking inflation, poverty alleviation,self reliance in agricultural production, defence and justice. Hence, the correct option is (b). Additional information: Fourth five year plan(1969-74) focused o n Economic growth and modernisation. Sixth five year plan (1980-85) laid stress on economic liberalisation through creating technological self-sufficiency and eliminating poverty. Seventh five year plan(1985-1990) focused on socialism and energy production at large.

q: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true with respect to Phillips Curve ? 1 It shows the trade-off between unemployment and inflation 2 The downward sloping curve of Phillips Curve is generally held to be valid only in the short run 3 In the long run, Phillips Curve is usually thought to be horizontal at the non- accelerating inflation rate of unemployment (NAIRU) Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Phillips curve is an accurate graphic representation of the economic relationship between the rate of unemployment and the rate of change of money wages. Explanation: According to the Phillips curve, there is a consistent inverse relationship between unemployment and inflation. According to this argument, inflation follows economic growth and should consequently produce more jobs and lower unemployment. But only in the short term is it claimed that this is true. Whereas in the long-run trade off the Phillips curve is a vertical line depicting that there is no long run tradeoff between inflation and unemployment. Hence the correct option is Option C i.e, 1 and 2 only Additional Information: It was named after the famous economist A. William Phillips. It indicates that wages tend to rise faster when unemployment is low. The specific unemployment rate at which the rate of inflation stabilizes and inflation neither rises nor falls is known as the non- accelerating inflation rate of unemployment (NAIRU).

q: Which one of the following is not a component of Revenue Receipts of the Union Government?
subject: economy
exp: Explanation Receipts- It shows the various sources from which the government collects revenue. These revenues are of two types: 1. Revenue Receipts These are income of the government which does not create any liability nor cause any reduction in the reduction of the asset of the government. Tax revenues- Taxes are statutory payments made by individuals and businesses to the government of a country, regardless of the direct benefits in return. Non-Tax Revenues- Income generated by the Government from non-tax sources is non-taxable income. The main sources of non-taxable income of the Central Government of India are: Commercial Revenue: electricity, railways, stamps, tolls, etc. Administrative Revenue: passport fees, government hospitals, fees, education and court costs. Fines and penalties, such as: Collected by the government when lawbreakers fail to follow rules and regulations.License Fees and Permits, Pledges and Interest Income Public Enterprise Profits. 1. Capital Receipts It is the government income that creates a liability or causes a reduction in government assets. The main sources of capital income for the central government are: Borrowings Recovery of Loans and Disinvestment – Sale/Resale of shares of public sector undertakings(PSUs). | | Defence Hence, the correct answer is option C i.e Disinvestment receipts because it is part of Capital Receipts

q: The monetary policy in India uses which of the following tools? 1. Bank rate 2. Open market operations 3. Public debt 4. Public revenue Select the correct answer using the code given below
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Central Bank uses various instruments to implement monetary policy such as repo rate, bank rate, open market operations, moral suasion, margin requirement, etc. Fiscal policy includes public debt and public revenue. Explanation: Bank Rate is the rate at which the central bank of a country lends money to commercial banks to meet their long term needs. Open market operations refers to buying and selling of government securities by the central bank from/to the commercial banks. Both are the instruments of monetary policy. This made statements 1 and 2 correct Hence, the correct answer is option (a)1 and 2 only

q: Which one of the following criteria got the highest weight for determination of shares of States in the formula given by the 14 th Finance Commission?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: 14th Finance commission. Explanations: 1. According to Article 280 of the Indian Constitution, the Finance Commission's membership must be changed every five years. 2. The division of the Union's net tax revenues between the Union and the States must be recommended by the Finance Commission. 3. The tax devolution suggestion has increased dramatically from the 13th Finance Commission's 32% recommendation to 42%. 4. The head of the 14th Finance Commission is Y. V. Reddy. The following criteria and weights govern how the tax is distributed horizontally. 5. 50% Income distance 6. 17.5% of the population in 1971. 7. Local Population Change (2011) 10% 8. 7.5% forest cover. Hence, the correct answer will be (B) ie. Income distance

q: Consider the following statements about the Second Five-Year Plan : 1. It was drafted under the leadership of K. N. Raj. 2. It proposed that industries like electricity, railways, steel, machineries and communication could be developed in the public sector. 3. The drafters found balancing industry and agriculture very difficult. 4. The drafters found balancing industry and agriculture really easy. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Second Five Year Plan (1956 - 1961) was subjected to the idea of improving the public sector and rapid industrialisation. Balancing between the industry and agriculture was challenging for the drafters. Explanation: The second five year plan was based on Mahalanobis model named after Indian statistician, Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis. The second five-year plan focused on industry, especially heavy industries like electricity, railways, steel, machineries and communication The scale and methods of production were given a push for better. The growth rate targeted by the second five year plan was 4.5% and the growth rate achieved was 4.27%. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, 2 and 3 only

q: Which one of the following sectors is not affected by the changes made in the Foreign Direct Investment Policy in June 2016?
o: Manufacturing of small arms and ammunitions covered under the Arms Act, 1959
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Foreign Direct Investment: A foreign direct investment (FDI) is an investment which is made by a company or an individual ,in one country and it is based in another country. Explanation: Foreign Direct Investment Policy in June 2016 was released by the department of industrial policy and promotion , ministry of commerce and industry. Multi -brand retailing sectors are not affected by the changes made in the FDI Policy in June 2016. Additional Information:- The main factors that affect FDI are Infrastructure and access to raw material. Communication and transport links. Skill and wage costs of labor. Hence, The correct answer is option(a)Multi - brand retailing.

q: Arrange the following events in sequential order as they happened in India : 1. Mahalanobis Model 2. Plan Holiday 3. Rolling Plan Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept:- Events that are in sequential order as they happened in India…… 1. Mahalanobis Model 2. Plan Holiday 3. Rolling Plan Explanation:- These all are related to five year plans of the planning commission. Mahalanobis Plan:-This was India’s IInd five-year plan (1956-61) proposed by Professor Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis. Plan Holiday:-This was developed due to the failure of the Third Five Year Plan. Rolling Plan:- The Janata government's sixth five-year plan was introduced for the years 1978 to 1983. Hence, The correct answer is Option (a) ie. 1,2,3

q: Who among the following scholars argued that "capital created underdevelopment not because it exploited the underdeveloped world, but because it did not exploit it enough"?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Identifying the statement . Explanation: The following statement is said by Geoffrey Kay in his book “Development and Underdevelopment: A Marxist Analysis” The book is based on the ideology of Karl Marx. In his book he tried to provide insight into the impact capitalism has on our society. Hence the correct answer is option (c) i.e., Geoffrey Kay

q: Devaluation of currency will be more beneficial if the prices of
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Devaluation refers to reduction in price of domestic currency in terms of all foreign currencies under fixed exchange rate regime. It helps a country's economy by increasing its competitiveness in the international market. Explanation: Under the fixed exchange rate system, the exchange rate for a currency is fixed by the government to ensure stability in foreign trade and capital movements. To achieve stability, the government undertakes to buy and sell foreign currencies. Hence, the correct answer is Option (b).

q: Goods and Services Tax likely to be levied in India is not a
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept:- Goods and Services Tax( GST) likely to be levied in India is not a gross value tax. Explanation:- Goods and Services Tax (GST) is a type of indirect tax. It is used in India for the supply of goods and services. Goods and services are divided into five different tax slabs for collection of tax 0%, 5%, 12%, 18% , 28%. GST is not applicable for diesel, petrol, natural gas, aviation fuel, electricity and liquor. Additional Information:- Type of tax:- Indirect First Idea:- The Idea towards GST was the first mooted in the budget for 2006-2007. Nature of Tax:- Destination based tax, Value added tax, Consumption Tax. Here, The correct answer is Option ( a)

q: UBI, as an alternative for subsidies in poverty alleviation, stands for
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: UBI is a government program in which every citizen would get a certain amount of money to cover their basic expenses as well as additional assistance, if they require it during challenging times. Explanation: UBI stands for Universal basic income According to the survey, UBI would cost between 4% and 5% of GDP to reduce poverty to 0.5%. Hence, the correct answer option is (d)Universal Basic Income Additional Information: Former Finance Minister, Arun Jaitley suggested a Universal Basic Income. It was suggested in the Economic Survey for 2016–17. Iran was the first nation to implement a national basic income in the fall of 2010.

q: Which one of the following indices is now used by the Reserve Bank of India to measure the rate of inflation in India?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Consumer Price Index (CPI), which calculates the percentage change in the cost of a basket of products and services that families typically use, is the most well-known measure of inflation. Explanation: The Reserve Bank of India has been tasked to keep in check that the retail inflation remains at 4% with margin of 2%. Retail inflation has constantly been above the upper tolerance limit for 11 months in a row due to some external factors. According to Trading Economics, global macro models and analyst's assumptions, inflation rate in India is said to be 5.30 percent by the end of this quarter. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Consumer Price Index

q: The Most Favored Nation (MFN) Clause under WTO regime is based on the principle of
o: differential treatment between locals and foreigners
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The principle of non- discrimination between nations is known as Most Favored Nation (MFN) treatment. Explanation: The World Trade Organization's 164 members make sure to treat other members equally so, all of them benefit from each other's lowest tariffs, highest import quotas and fewest trade barriers for goods and services. Most Favored Nation treatment requires members to accord the most favorable tariff and non-supervisory treatment given to the pro- conduit of any one member at the time of import or import of “like products” to all other members. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) i.e, non-discrimination between nations

q: The Twelfth Five-Year Plan focussed on inclusive growth. Which of the following were considered as challenges for inclusiveness? 1. Poverty 2. Group inequality 3. Regional imbalance 4. Unemployment Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Twelfth Five Year Plan (2012-2017) was focused on faster, sustainable and more inclusive growth. Unemployment, group inequality and regional imbalance were the biggest challenges faced by the program. Explanation: The Twelfth Five Year Plan ran from 2012 to 2017 and was presided over by Manmohan Singh. Even though many of the objectives outlined in the plan weren't entirely achieved, several economic sectors still experience significant improvements in output. Consumer products climbed by 87%, capital goods by 158%, and overall industrial output increased by 118%. Hence, the correct option is Option (B) i.e, 1,2,3 and 4

q: The phenomenon of 'demographic dividend' of a country relates to
o: a sharp decline in the total population
an increase in working age population
a decline in the infant mortality rate
an increase in the sex ratio
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The phenomenon of 'demographic dividend' of a country occurs when there is an increase in the working age population. Explanation: Demographic dividend means when the economic growth results from the shift in the population’s age structure The demographic dividend is the increase in economic growth , which results when the working age population is more than the non working age population Additional Information: David Bloom is the author of the phrase "demographic dividend". Hence, The correct answer is option (b) an increase in working age population.

q: Which one of the following statements about Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) is not correct?
o: It experiences price changes throughout the day.
It typically has lower daily liquidity and higher fees than mutual fund shares.
An ETF does not have its net asset value calculated once at the end of every day.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Exchange traded funds, or ETFs, are marketable securities that follow an index, a commodity, bonds, or a portfolio of assets, similar to index funds. ETFs are a desirable alternative for individual investors since they often offer higher daily liquidity and lower fees than mutual fund schemes. Explanation: As an ETF is bought and sold on the stock market throughout the day, its trading price fluctuates just like the price of any other stock. The net asset value of the stock basket that the ETF is investing in is reflected in its pricing. Hence, the correct answer is Option (c).

q: The natural rate of unemployment hypothesis was advocated by
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Natural Unemployment- Natural Unemployment or natural rate of unemployment can be defined as the minimum level required for a sustainable economy without being the cause of inflation. The number of jobs available should be equal to the number of unemployed. There are various two types of unemployment- Frictional Unemployment - Some people keep changing jobs frequently. So, they may be unemployed for some period. This may be called a temporary form of unemployment. Structural Unemployment- This means that jobs are available in the market. But people lack the required skills. So, they hire temporary people or hire fewer people. This is a structural form of unemployment. Explanation- A.W. Philips was the academic professor known for the Philips Curve. J.M. Keynes is an English economist, known founder of modern macroeconomics. R.G Lipsey is known for his theory of second best in Economics. The hypothesis of Natural Rate of Unemployment was given by Milton Friedman. Hence, the correct option is ‘a’.

q: The following item consists of two statements, Statement I and Statement II. Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the code given below. Statement I: There has been a sharp decline in savings rate in Indian economy between 2007- 2008 to 2015-2016. Statement II: There has been a fall in household and public savings.
o: Both the statements are individually true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- According to CEIC data, there is sharp decline in savings rate from Explanation- Fall in household and public savings are one of themain reason for this decline. This fall is not a continuous downward . Low in household savings rate is due to the lower returns of invaluable assets. Hence the correct option is ‘a’ i.e. Both the statements are individually true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.

q: Which of the following statements about the India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) is/are correct? 1. It has been incorporated as a Public Limited Company. 2. It started its operation by establishing two pilot branches at Hyderabad and Varanasi. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- India Post Payments Bank aimed at making the bank reach the locals through the post. India has one of the largest and oldest post services. It is governed by the RBI and has many facilities like zero balance accounts and many more considering the situation of rural people. Its first two operational branches were at Raipur and Ranchi. Now, it has expanded up to 650 branches. Explanation- India is still a developing country. It has a rural economy. There are many remote areas which lack basic facilities. There were fewer banking opportunities in many areas. So, the government started the IPPB initiative under the leadership of the Prime Minister in 2018. Hence, the correct option is ‘a’.

q: The Fourteenth Finance Commission assigned different weights to the following parameters for distribution of tax proceeds to the States : 1. Income distance 2. Population 3. Demographic changes 4. Area Arrange the aforesaid parameters in descending order in terms of their weights.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Taxes- Taxes are the mandatory finances levied by the government to the nationals to incur their expenses. Explanation- We can see that the maximum weight was given as: Income Distance > Population > Area >Demographic changes Finance Commission- According to Article 280, the Finance Commission is built every 5 years under the president. The main objective is to govern the money flow and decide how to distribute it. Mr Y.V. Reddy, the governor of RBI, formed the Fourteenth Commission. It was constituted on 2 january 2013. The maximum weight was given to the Income distance which shifted from 47.5% to 50%. It focuses on the gross per capita income of all states. The state with the highest GDP is considered a reference for others. The weight of the population was shifted from 25% to 17.5%%. The weight of the area was increased to 15%. The demographic changes were at least a priority from the above list. Hence, the correct option is 'b'.

q: Which one of the following is the maximum age of joining the National Pension System (NPS) under the NPS-Private Sector?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: National Pension Scheme is a voluntary pension system where citizens can join the scheme up to an age of 65 years. Earlier, the age limit was 60 years but later it was increased. Explanation: One can exit from the scheme on completion of 5 years. PFRDA has recently announced that the National Pension System (NPS) will no longer compel investors to convert 40% of their accumulated retirement corpus into an annuity, as poor yields on annuities and high inflation are translating into negative returns. It has also announced that the retirees will be able to take out the entire Rs.5 lakh savings in the NPS against Rs. 2 lakh at present. Hence, the correct answer is Option (c).

q: The Economic historian, who has used the data collected by Buchanan Hamilton to support the thesis of deindustrialization in the 19th century India, is
subject: economy
exp: Explanation The Buchanan-Hamilton data were utilized by Amiya Kumar Bagchi to bolster his deindustrialization argument in the 19th century. Hence the correct option is ‘B’ i.e. Amiya Kumar Bagchi.

q: The following item consists of two statements, Statement I and Statement II. Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the code given below. Statement I: Private investments in research have severely lagged public investments in India. Statement II: Universities play a relatively small role in the research activities of the country.
o: Both the statements are individually true and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Private Investment is money invested by companies, financial organizations, or investors rather than by a government. Explanation- According to Economic Survey the investments by the private sector in research have severely lagged public investments in India. In comparison to developed countries,major research source are carried out by them in India. The survey also states that over the past two decades, public investment in research is stagnant to of GDP. Reason is the small role played by Universities in the research activities of India. Hence the correct option is ‘a’ i.e.Both the statements are individually true, and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I

q: According to the latest Reserve Bank of India study on State finances, capital spending is maximum on
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Reserve Bank of India is the body responsible for regulating the Indian Banking System. The money that government or state spends on the infrastructure, schools, public facilities and development. The responsibility of keeping and maintaining the records of the capital is given to RBI. The RBI or Reserve Bank Of India publishes the reports on the capital spending of the state every year. This year also this report was published. Explanation- According to the latest reports, the spending was maximum on Education Sports And Culture . This is followed by Rural Devoplement Hence, the correct option is 'D'.

q: Consider the following statements about the impact of tax : 1. A tax is shifted forward to consumers if the demand is inelastic relative to supply. 2. A tax is shifted backward to producers if the supply is relatively more inelastic than demand. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Impact of Tax- The initial burden of tax is called as Tax Impact. The one who is liable to pay taxes bears its impact. The consumers and producers both have to pay the taxes. Explanation- Taxes- Taxes are the mandatory finances levied by the government to the nationals to incur their expenses. Taxes also keep on changing with the new rules and are changed accordingly. It has various effects on people, which may be positive or negative. A tax is shifted forward to consumers if the demand is inelastic relative to supply. This statement is correct. When demand is inelastic (less responsive to price changes) compared to supply, producers can pass on the tax burden to consumers without much decrease in quantity demanded, hence shifting the tax burden forward to consumers. A tax is shifted backwards to producers if the supply is relatively more inelastic than demand. This statement is also correct. When supply is relatively more inelastic (less responsive to price changes) than demand, producers bear the brunt of the tax burden because they cannot easily reduce production in response to higher taxes. Consequently, they may absorb a larger portion of the tax burden, leading to a backward shift of the tax burden to producers. Hence, the correct option is 'c'.

q: As per the extant policy, Foreign Direct Investment is permitted in the defence sector under the automatic route up to which one of the following limits?
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Defence Industry’s Foreign Direct Investments(FDI). Explanation- Under the automatic route, Foreign Direct Investment is permitted in the defense sector. Reason - to access modern technology. The defense sector has two laws- 1. Arms Act, 2. Industries(Development & Regulation) Act, . Hence the correct option is ‘d’ i.e. .

q: The following item consists of two statements, Statement I and Statement II. Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the code given below. Statement I: Agriculture in India still accounts for a substantial share in total employment. Statement II: There has been no decline in volatility of agricultural growth in India.
o: Both statements are individually true, and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Agriculture is a primary sector of India. Explanation- Total contribution in GDP of India by Agricultural sector is . Thus, Statement I is true in its sense that in India, Agriculture still accounts for a substantial share in total employment. The annual volatility (measured by standard deviation) is reduced from to . Thus, Statement II is wrong. Hence the correct option is ‘c’ i.e.Statement I is true but Statement II is false

q: According to the law of diminishing marginal utility, as the amount of a good consumed increases, the marginal utility of that good tends to
subject: economy
exp: The Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility states that marginal utility from consuming each additional unit of a commodity declines as its consumption increases. The law of diminishing marginal utility states that the "as a consumer consumes more and more units of a commodity, the utility with every unit consumed goes diminishing' The theory can be explained with an example suppose a consumer has 5 apples, if he gets 50 utils with consuming ISt apple, then marginal utility gained after consuming 2nd apple will be less than 50 utils and goes on decreasing with every extra unit consumed.

q: The Reserve Bank of India has recently constituted a high-level task force on Public Credit Registry (PCR) to suggest a road map for developing a transparent, comprehensive and near-real-time PCR for India. The task force is headed by
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Public Credit Registry (PCR)- It is a repository in which the information of all the borrowers is stored. It aggregates the financial and non- financial information of all the borrowers. It was setup in the year 2018. The government constituted a high level task force on PCR to suggest a road map for the system. Explanation- Public Credit Registry or PCR is necessary as it helps to distinguish between good and bad borrowers. This committee was headed by Y.M. Deosthalee along with 10 other people. Hence, the correct option is 'd'.

q: According to the World Bank's Doing Business Report, 2018, India's ranking has improved in 2018 as compared to 2017 in which of the following areas? 1. Paying taxes 2. Resolving insolvency 3. Starting a business 4. Getting electricity Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Ease of Doing Business Index- The ease of doing business index is released by the world bank every year. It gives the rank to countries by rating them on a scale of 100. The better the score the greater the rank will be. The rating is given based on 11 key points. The better rank emphasizes that the procedures are less hectic and with maximum protection of rights. Explanation- India- India jumped from its rank of 130 to 100. The areas in which we improved the ranking was paying taxes and resolving insolvencies. Hence, the correct option is 'b'.

q: The following item consists of two statements, Statement I and Statement II. Examine these two statements carefully and select the correct answer using the code given below. Statement I: The overall fiscal deficit of the States in India during 2017-2018 stayed above the FRBM threshold level of 3 percent for the third successive year. Statement II: Special Category States had run up a higher level of fiscal deficit in 2017-2018 compared to 2016-2017.
o: Both statements are individually true, and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I
Both the statements are individually true but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- There is a fall in fiscal deposits of FY in comparison to FY Fiscal deposits are a difference between total revenue and total expenditure. Explanation- The overall Fiscal deficit stated in FY 2017-18 fell to , which was at in FY , according to RBI reports. Thus, the first statement is wrong. While fiscal deposits of Special category states in FY were at deteriorated to in FY . Thus, statement II is correct. Hence the correct option is ‘d’ i.e. Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

q: Which one of the following is an example of a price floor?
o: Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Jowar in India
Subsidy given to farmers to buy fertilizers
Price paid by people to buy goods from ration shops
Maximum Retail Price (MRP) printed on the covers/packets of goods sold in India
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Minimum Support price (MSP) for Jowar in India is an example of price floor. Explanation: The price floor is the lowest- price for a commodity to be sold at fixed legally. When the demand of a product is less, the price floors are used by the government to prevent the price of that product from being too low. For example, if a government wants to promote the business of coffee beans, it can set one in the coffee grain market. Government introduced floor prices in markets where demand is inelastic and prices are naturally very low. Hence, the correct option is Option A i.e, Minimum Support Price (MSP) for Jowar in India

q: Which one of the following factors is not considered in determining the Minimum Support Price (MSP) in India?
o: Price trends international and in domestic markets
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The cost of living index is not considered in the Minimum Support Price (MSP). Minimum Support Price (MSP) is the minimum amount fixed by the government for particular agricultural products. Products would be bought from the farmers directly at this fixed price if the open market prices are lower than the cost incurred. Explanation: According to the opinions of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices, the Department of Agriculture and Cooperation, Government of India, announces the Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for generally 22 crops before their sowing seasons. There are some factors regarding the minimum support price like demand, supply, cost of production, intercrop price, terms of trade between agriculture and non agricultural, etc. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Cost of living index

q: Gini Coefficient or Gini Ratio can be associated with which one of the following measurements in an economy?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: In economics, the Gini coefficient is used to calculate the degree of income disparity among a country's citizens. Explanation: A Gini coefficient of zero shows maximum equality, whereas a Gini coefficient of one expresses the maximum degree of inequality. The Consumer Price Index is used to calculate inflation in India (CPI). Additional Information: Many factors, such as the Global Multidimensional Poverty Index and the Human Poverty Index, are used to calculate an index of poverty. Wages/salaries and money the government transfers to you in the form of transfer payments are both considered personal income. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Income inequality

q: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
o: When total utility is maximum, marginal utility is zero.
When total utility is decreasing, marginal utility is negative.
When total utility is increasing, marginal utility is positive.
When total utility is maximum, marginal and average utility are equal to each other.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: consumer theory Explanation: Total utility is the overall content a consumer gets from the consumption of a specific service or goods Total utility is the net of marginal utilities of all the individual goods. When the consumption of an extra unit of the item occurs, it results in marginal utility. Hence, the correct option is Option (D) i.e, When total utility is maximum, marginal and average utility is equal to each other. Additional Information: Total utility works hand in hand with marginal utility. When the marginal utility is positive, the total utility will increase and once the marginal utility is negative, then the total utility will decrease.

q: The value of the slope of a normal demand curve is
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The demand curve is normally downward sloping hence, the value of the slope of a normal demand curve is negative. Explanation: The demand curve is normally inclined downwards because it indicates an inverse relationship between price and quantitative demand. The demand curve predicts that as the price of the product goes up, the amount requested goes down, and as the price goes down, the demand of the product goes up. In some cases if there is a straight-line demand curve then, the slope is equal at all its points. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, negative

q: A market situation when many firms sell similar but not identical products is termed as
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: A situation in the market when many firms sell a similar but not identical product then, it is termed as Monopolistic competition. Explanation: In Monopolistic competition, economists refers to the industries that consist of many firms competing against each other, but selling products that are distinctive in some way. For example, when grocery stores are selling products, as there are a large number of firms that sell many of the same goods but with distinct branding and marketing. This exists in monopolistic competition. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, monopolistic competition

q: Which one of the following equals Personal Disposable Income?
o: Personal Income - Direct taxes paid by households and miscellaneous fees, fines, etc.
Private Income Saving of Private Corporate Sectors - Corporation Tax
Total expenditure of Households - Income Tax - Gifts received
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Personal Disposable Income can be described as the amount of money left for the household after deduction of all forms of taxes like the direct tax (Income Tax), fees, and fines, etc. Explanation: Personal Disposable income is the left money of our income after we pay all the direct taxes. Personal Disposable Income is measured using the following formula: Disposable income = Personal income – Personal current taxes. Hence, the correct option is Option A, i.e Personal Income - Direct taxes paid by households and miscellaneous fees, fines, etc.

q: Which one of the following is not an assumption in the law of demand?
o: There are no changes in the taste and preferences of consumers.
Consumers are demonstrating the effect affected by
There are no changes in the price of substitute goods.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The law of demand states that the quantity purchased varies inversely with the price. Explanation: The law of demands principle conveys that there is a negative relation between the price and quantity demanded of a commodity over a particular period of time. There are three exceptions to the Law of Demand- Giffen goods Veblen effect Change in income Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Consumers are demonstrating the effect affected by

q: Consider the following statements: 1. Inflation in India continued to be moderate during 2017-18. 2.There was significant reduction in food inflation, particularly pulses and vegetables during the period. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Inflation remained moderate during 2017-18 in India. During this period, reduction in inflation was broadly based on major commodities except for housing fuel and electricity. Explanation: The average of inflation measured by the method of CPI (Consumer Price Index) was 3.3% during 2017-18. It is the lowest value calculated in the last six financial years, according to the Economic Survey. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) i.e, 1 only Additional Information: A moderate inflation shows the economy of the country healthy, because as the economy grows, demand for goods also grows. This increase in demand forces prices a little high as industries try to produce more of the goods that are in demand and consumers and businesses want to buy.

q: Consider the following statements about a joint-stock company : 1. It has a legal existence. 2. There is limited liability of share- holders. 3. It has a democratic management. 4. It has a collective ownership. Which of the statements given above are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The companies are registered under the Companies Act, 2013. Hence a company acts as an artificial legal person which can buy and sell products. It can also sue and get sued. Explanation: A business organization that is owned by its investors is called a Joint-stock company. In a joint stock company the company's shares of the company can be bought and sold by the shareholders. Shareholders must have ownership of at least one stock of the company in order to be considered as a partial owner. Hence, the correct option is Option (D) i.e, 1, 2, 3 and 4

q: Consider the following statements about. indifference curves : 1. Indifference curves are convex to the origin. 2. Higher indifference curve represents higher level of satisfaction 3. Two indifference curves cut each other. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: One of the four features of the indifference curve is that they do not intersect each other. Explanation: An indifference curve is the group of various points depicting different combinations of two different products providing equal utility to the consumer. There are four features of an indifference curve- Indifference curve slopes downwards to the right. Indifference curve is convex to the origin. It cannot intersect with each other. The higher indifference curve shows the higher satisfaction is in comparison to the less higher indifference curve. Hence, the correct option is Option (B) i.e, 1 and 2 only

q: Indexation is a method whose use can be associated with which one of the following?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Indexation considers the period from when you acquired the asset until when you sold it. It considers both the inflation rate at the time of asset purchase and the inflation rate at the time of asset sale. Explanation: Indexation is a technique for adjusting income payments using a price index to preserve the public's purchasing power after inflation. Indexation offers the chance to raise the selling price by accounting for the effects of inflation. Thus, indexation would enable beneficiaries to gain from a reduction in tax liability. Hence, the correct option is A. Which is Controlling inflation

q: Which one of the following statements with regard to economic models is not correct?
o: They involve simplification of complex processes.
They represent the whole or a part of a theory.
They can be expressed only through equations.
They help in gaining an insight into cause and effect.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The economic models are not necessarily represented in equations only. They are presented in a way to show the whole or a part of theory. Explanation: An economic model is a simplified definition of reality, modeled in a way to yield hypothesis about economic behavior that can be tested. An important feature of an economic model is that, it is necessarily subjective in design because there are no objective measures of economic outcomes. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, They can be expressed only through equations.

q: Who among the following has given the concept of Human Development?
subject: economy
exp: Question Demand: Human Development Index. Key Concept: Human Development Index. Explanations: 1. In the late 1970s and early 1980s, the economist and statesman Mahbub-ul-Haq of Pakistan popularised the idea of human development. 2. He made the case that conventional measurements of economic growth, such the Gross Domestic Product (GDP), were insufficient to capture a society's whole spectrum of development and well-being. 3. The Human Progress Index (HDI), which Haq suggested as a new paradigm for assessing development, attempted to capture advancements in both education and health outcomes in addition to economic growth. 4. Today, several countries utilise the HDI as a gauge of human progress. Hence, the correct answer will be (B) ie. Mahbub-ul-Haq

q: Which one of the following canons of taxation was not advocated by Adam Smith?
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Adam Smith , the 18th century economist and philosopher, attempted to systematize the rules that should govern a rational system of taxation. Explanation: Four Canons or four principles of taxation of Adam smith are: Canon of Equality Canon of Certainty Canon of Convenience Canon of Economy Hence, the correct option is (d). Additional information: Other canons were added by other Economists in the list of Adam Smith’s Canon. Canon of productivity Canon of Elasticity Canon of Simplicity Canon of Variety Canon of Flexibility.

q: According to John Maynard Keynes, employment depends upon
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: According to J. M. Keynes, the amount of employment depends on the level of effective demand of individuals and their goods and services. Explanation: J.M Keynes’ theory of employment is based on the efficient demand. This means that Keynes approached employment by the demand side of the model. According to him, the volume of employment in a country is directly dependent on the level of effective demand of individuals. Hence, unemployment is seen as the deficiency of effective demand. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, effective demand

q: The working of the price mechanism in a free- market economy refers to which one of the following?
o: The interplay of the forces of demand and supply
Determination of the inflation rate in the economy
Determination of the propensity to consume economy's
Determination of the economy's full employment output
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: In the Price mechanism, the prices are determined by the free interaction of the forces of demand and supply. Explanation: A price mechanism, which is part of a market system, involves various ways of matching buyers with sellers. The price mechanism is an economic model in which price plays a key role in informing the activities of producers, consumers and resource providers. An example of a price mechanism is the prices of a product proposed and charged. Hence, the correct option is Option A i.e, The interplay of the forces of demand and supply.

q: Which one of the following is not a dimension of the Human Development Index?
o: Access to banking and other financial provisions
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Access to banking and other financial provisions is not considered as a factor of measuring the Human Development Index of any country. Explanation: A country's social and economic development is measured by calculating its Human Development Index by focusing on the following main four factors: mean years of schooling, expected years of schooling, life expectancy at birth, and gross national income (GNI) per capita. The United Nations Development Programme is the international institution which measures and publishes the Human Development Index. India ranks at 132nd position in 191 countries that were included in HDI report according to the latest report(HDR-2022). Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Access to banking and other financial provisions

q: When some goods or productive factors are completely fixed in amount, regardless of price, the supply curve is
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: When the quantity of products is fixed and the price varies, the slope will be vertical. Explanation: In a supply curve graph, the quantity of goods or products is shown on the x-axis and the price of those goods are depicted on the y axis. When the amount of products on the x-axis are fixed and the priceways the slope is vertical. Since the quantity of goods generally doesn't start with zero, there is no curve in the slope. Hence, the correct option is C Which is vertical

q: Sub-prime crisis' is a term associated with which one of the following events?
subject: economy
exp: Key point: Subprime generally refers to the credit score of a consumer which is lower than someone with good credit. Explanation: The subprime crisis that started in 2007 is an economic recession. It had badly affected the mortgage industry after the borrowers were not able to afford the repayment of loan. Many financial institutions and Hedge funds in the USA collapsed due to the economic crisis. It severely affected Global credit “Global turn down” is called an economic crisis. Hence, the correct option is (a).

q: Which one of the following countries is not a founding member of the New Development Bank?
subject: economy
exp: Key point: National Development Bank(NDB) was founded in 2014 formerly known as the BRICS Development Bank. It was established to fund sustainable development projects and infrastructure projects in the BRICS countries and other developing countries and emerging markets. Explanation: The BRICS countries, namely, Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa established the multilateral financial institution i.e. National Development Bank. Hence, the correct option is (c).

q: Which one of the following hypotheses postulates that individual's consumption in any time period depends upon resources available to the individual, rate of return on his capital and age of the individual?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Life Cycle Hypothesis (LCH) is an economic theory that explains how people maintain roughly the same level of consumption over time by saving when income is high and borrowing when income is low. Explanation: The consumption function theory was given by Endo and Modigliani and it is known as Life Cycle Hypothesis. The life-cycle hypothesis (LCH) is an economic theory that conveys the spending and saving patterns of individuals over the course of their life. It motivates people to work and save for retirement instead of spending all their income. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Life Cycle Hypothesis

q: Which of the following statements with regard to the 'Make in India' initiative is/are correct? 1. It was launched in the year 2018. 2.Its objective is to foster innovation. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
subject: economy
