exp: Question Demand: Make in India. Key Concept: Make in India. Explanations: 1. A key programme of the Indian government, Make in India, was introduced in 2014. 2. It was started with the intention of promoting India as a significant investment location as well as a centre for design, manufacturing, and innovation on a worldwide scale. 3. The program's promotion of innovation is one of its key goals. 4. The Ministry of Commerce and Industry's Department of Industrial Policy and Promotion is in charge of it. 5. By 2025, it is desired to increase manufacturing's contribution to GDP from its present 16% to 25%. Hence, the correct answer will be (B) ie.Only B

q: Who among the following is the Chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM)?
subject: economy
exp: Bibek Debroy is an Indian Economist appointed as the Chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) in September 2017. The Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM) is constituted to give economic advice to the Government of India, especially to the Prime Minister. Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister is a non-constitutional, non- permanent and independent body set up in September 2017. Hence, the answer is option (b) Bibek Debroy. Additional Information: Ratan P. Watal is a Finance Secretary at the Ministry of Finance. Ashima Goyal is a member of the Indian Technical Advisory Committee on monetary policy. Sajidid Chinoy serves on the Advisory Council of the 15th Finance Commission set up by the government of India.

q: In economics, if a diagram has a line passing through the origin and has angle with either axis and it is asserted that along the line , what is tacitly assumed?
o: Both variables are in the same unit.
Both variables are in different units.
At least one variable is a pure number.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: If a diagram has a line going through the origin and makes an angle of 45° with either axis and it is asserted that along the line X = Y, then both variables are in the same unit. Explanation: When a line in a diagram in economics passes through the origin and makes a 45° angle with either axis and it is said that along the line X=Y, both variables are in the same unit. Furthermore, it is impossible to compare the magnitudes of the estimated coefficients in different units. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, Both variables are in the same unit.

q: The Cash Reserve Ratio refers to
o: the share of Net Demand and Time Liabilities that banks have to hold as liquid assets
the share of Net Demand and Time Liabilities that banks have to hold as balances with the RBI
the share of Net Demand and Time Liabilities that banks have to hold as part of their cash reserves
the ratio of cash holding to reserves of banks
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The share of Net Demand and Time Liabilities that banks have to hold as balances with the RBI is known as the Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR). Explanation: It is mandatory for banks to hold a particular proportion of their deposits in the cash with the Reserve Bank of India. The minimum ratio, that is, the portion of the total deposits to be held in cash is stipulated by the RBI and is referred to as CRR. The Prime function of CRR is to maintain inflation in control. In times when inflation rises in the economy, the central bank increases the CRR to lower the bank's loanable funds. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, the share of Net Demand and Time Liabilities that banks have to hold as balances with the RBI Additional Information: Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) is the ratio of these liquid assets to the demand and time liabilities. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) can increase this ratio by up to 40%.

q: Suppose an agricultural laborer earns per day in her village. She gets a job to work as a babysitter in a nearby town @ ₹700 per day. She chose to work as an agricultural laborer. Which one of the following is the opportunity cost of the agricultural laborer?
subject: economy
exp: If a farm worker earns Rs. 400 per day and additionally secures a second babysitting position in a nearby town that pays Rs. 700 per day. Opportunity cost = Return on the Best Investment Option – Investment Returns Selected for Pursuit. The opportunity cost for her to work in agriculture would be (Return on the Best Investment Option) 700 – (Investment Returns Selected for Pursuit) 400 = 300. Hence, the Correct option is D‘ i.e. ₹ 300

q: A market, in which there are a large number of firms, homogeneous product, infinite elasticity of demand for an individual firm and no control over price by firms, is termed as
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Market Explanation: Perfect competition Market has a large number of buyers and sellers. All sellers are small sellers in competition with each other. There is no big seller hence, all the firms in such a market are price takers. Participants are high both buyers ans sellers. Products have many substitutes and no marketing or selling cost is incurred in this market. The seller is a price taker, not a price maker. Buyer is a price maker as he is willing to buy all at a certain price but none at a price higher. Hence, the correct option is D i.e.' Perfect Competition.'

q: Which one of the following is not an objective of the MGNREGA?
o: Providing up to 100 days of skilled labour in a financial year
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: MGNREGA stands from The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act which was passed in 2005. This act ensures the Right to work for Rural people. Prime minister P.V. Narshimha Rao proposed Rural Employment in 1991. Explanation: This act provides guaranteed at least 100 days of wage employment for unskilled labour work. provides the livelihood security for the poor people by creating productive assets like roads, water canal for irrigation and wells and ponds. It ensures livelihood security by merging people belonging to the poverty line. So it will reduce the migration of labour from Rural areas to urban areas. Providing helps and ensures work for the women belongs to the ST, SC and poverty line. Hence, the correct option is (a). Additional information: It was called the Employment Assurance scheme but later known as MGNREGA because of the inclusion of the food work programme in 2005

q: Since 2014-15, India has consistently run trade surplus with which one among the following countries?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Internaional Trade Explanation: USA was India's top trading partner for the second consecutive fiscal year in 2019-20. It shows increasing economic ties between the two. Bilateral trade between India and USA in 2019-20 was USD 88.75 billion. USA surpassed China to become a top trading partner of India. Bilateral trade between India and China decreased to 87.08 billion in 2018-19. Hence, the correct option is C i.e.'USA.'

q: Recently the Reserve Bank of India has imposed limitations, initially for a period of six months, on the withdrawal of amount by account holders of which one of the following banks?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Reserve Bank of India has imposed some restrictions on the withdrawal of money by the account holders of Punjab and Maharashtra Co-operative Bank initially for a period of 6 months. Explanation: The Punjab and Maharashtra Co-operative Bank is a Multi State scheduled Urban and Co-operative bank. It is currently working in the states of Maharashtra, Delhi, Karnataka, Goa, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh. This Bank was given the scheduled status by the Reserve Bank of India in 2000. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Punjab and Maharashtra Cooperative Bank Additional Information: A Cooperative Bank is a bank which is governed by both banking and co-operative legislation. These banks are registered under the Cooperative Society Act 1965.

q: Which of the following are considered to be the four pillars of human development?
subject: economy
exp: The process of enlarging individual’s/people’s freedom, opportunities and their well being is called Human development. People are central to all development under this concept. Human Development has six pillars: Equity, Sustainability, Productivity, Empowerment, Cooperation, and Security. Equity is based on the moral of equality (that people should be treated equally). Sustainability is human societies that live and meet their needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs. Productivity is a major determinant of inflation and also economic growth. Empowerment is people having the ability to make their own decisions for themselves and society. Cooperation is the process of working together for mutual benefit. Security is building people’s strength and aspirations. Hence, the answer is option (b) Equity, productivity, empowerment and sustainability. Additional Information: The Human development concept was developed by economist Mahbub ul Haq. The first Human Development report was published by the United Nations in 1990.

q: Normally, there will not be a shift in the demand curve when
o: Consumers want to buy more at any given price
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Market Explanation: The demand curve is used in microeconomics to determine the quantity of a particular commodity that people are willing to purchase if its price changes correspondingly. It depicts the relationship between the commodity's price (y-axis) and the quantity of the demanded commodity at that price(x-axis). It will shift to the right or left as per the change in a non-price determinant of demand. Factors such as Changes in expectations, Changes in taste and preference, Rising income and Population change affects market demand. Hence, the correct option is A, i.e.'Price of a commodity falls.'

q: Which one of the following statements about the Repo rate is not correct?
o: It is the interest rate charged by the Central Bank on overnight loans.
It is the interest rate paid by the commercial banks on overnight borrowing.
It is the rate on commercial banks' deposits with the central bank.
It is the interest rate applied to the borrowing of funds against collateral
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The repo rate is a liquidity adjustment facility (LAF) mechanism through which banks borrow money from the central bank. Explanation: It is the interest rate charged by the Central Bank on overnight loans. Correct: The repo rate is the interest rate at which the central bank lends money to commercial banks, typically for short periods such as overnight, against collateral securities. It is the interest rate paid by the commercial banks on overnight borrowing. Correct: The repo rate is the rate at which commercial banks borrow funds from the central bank, and the interest they pay is determined by this rate. It is the rate on commercial banks' deposits with the central bank. Incorrect: The rate on commercial banks' deposits with the central bank is the reverse repo rate. The repo rate pertains to borrowing by commercial banks from the central bank, not deposits. It is the interest rate applied to the borrowing of funds against collateral. Correct. It is the interest rate applied to the borrowing of funds against collateral. The collateral is used to secure the loan, but the cost associated with borrowing (the interest rate) defines the repo rate. Hence, the correct option is Option C.

q: Which one of the following statements in the context of social sector spending in India during 2014 - 19 (both States and the Union Government together) is true?
o: Expenditure on education was 5 % of GDP
Expenditure on health was 4 % of the social services expenditure
There was a stagnation in the spending on education as a percent of GDP
Health sector spending amounted to 10 % of the total expenditure
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: | | Defence The central government's education budget has been cut since 2014. Despite government promises to increase spending on education, the share of union budgets devoted to education fell from 4.14% in 2014-15 to 3.4% in 2019-20. In the 2019- 20 budget, the share of the union budget devoted to education remains at 3.4%. Explanation: According to the Economic Survey, the combined spending of the federal government and the state governments on social services increased to Rs 17.16 lakh crore during the fiscal year 2020–21. At Rs 17.16 lakh, the expenditure is 12% higher than Rs 15.31 lakh (revised estimate) for 2019-20. Overall, India's public health spending (sum of central and government spending) remained between 1.2% and 1.6% of GDP during 2008-09 and 2019-20. As the study shows, social services sector expenditure in FY 2019-20 was Rs 1.531 crore, of which Rs. 6.13 lakh crore was spent on education, Rs 3.12 lakh crore on health, and Rs 6.06 crore on others. This spending is relatively small compared to other countries such as China (3.2%), US (8.5%) and Germany (9.4%). Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, There was a stagnation in the spending on education as a percent of GDP Additional Information: For 2020-2021, the ministry received a quota of 67,112 kroner. This is an increase of 3.9% from the revised 2019-20 estimate of Rs 64,609 crore. Under the department, 97% of the department's allocation will go to the Department of Health and Family Welfare at Rs 6,501.2 crore. On the other hand, the health research department is allocated 2,100 kroner (3% of the allocation).

q: The mismatch in the regional or occupational pattern of job vacancies and the pattern of worker availability results in
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The type of unemployment where there is a mismatch in the regional pattern of job vacancies and the pattern of worker availability is known as structural unemployment. Explanation: The type of unemployment where the jobs are available but there's a mismatch between what companies need and what the workers available there offer is known as structural unemployment. This mismatch can occur because the jobseekers don't have the needed skills for the available jobs or the available jobs far away from the jobseekers. It is a long-lasting unemployment that is due to shifts in a nation's economy. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) i.e, Structural unemployment. Additional Information: Disguised employment or hidden unemployment is a type of fake scenario that is different from the underlying reality, where workers appear to be employed but in reality they are not. It is a type of unemployment that does not affect aggregate economic output.

q: The situation where the equilibrium level of real GDP falls short of potential GDP is known as
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Recessionary gap is a term used in macroeconomics when the Real Gross Domestic Product is lesser than its potential Gross Domestic Product. Explanation: A recessionary gap also known as contractionary gap generally occurs when a Nation's real gross domestic product is lesser than its Gross Domestic Product at full employment. Contractionary gaps close when real wages are back to equilibrium and the labor demand equals the quantity supplied. Sometimes, stabilization policy is implemented by the policy makers to close the recessionary gap and increase the Real Gross Domestic Product. Hence, the correct option is Option A i.e, Recessionary gap Additional Information: Inflationary gap- An inflationary gap measures the difference between the current real GDP and the GDP of an economy operating at full employment. It is called inflationary gap due to its outcome being inflation (continuous rise in prices).

q: Which one of the following is true of a pure voluntary exchange between two parties A and B ?
o: A can exploit B or vice versa
Both gain; it is a win-win situation
If A makes profit, it must be at the cost of B
subject: economy
exp: A voluntary exchange is a type of exchange where two parties freely trade goods or services. This happens in a market economy where both participants interact and gain a mutual benefit from it and they improve than when they started. For example, when an individual starts tutoring students in exchange of money then he is gaining money in exchange of their expertise and the student is gaining knowledge to pass their exam. The market economies are free and the exchange of goods and services is not undergarments restrictions. It means businesses or individuals can determine the prices of their products on their own. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, Both gain; it is a win-win situation Additional Information: Voluntary exchange is the term given by Neoclassical economists. It was used to describe their primary assumptions of trading. This is what today we call a free market. Voluntary exchange is beneficial because participants feel that they are | | Defence in profit from the transaction hence they are encouraged to complete it organically. When government restrictions are not there a voluntary exchange can create a fair competition in the market.

q: According to UNDP's Human Development Report - 2020, in which of the HDI components has India improved in recent years ? 1. Life expectancy at birth 2. Expected years of schooling 3. GNI per capita 4. Mean years of schooling Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: According to UNDP's Human Development Report 2020, India has improved in all the four mentioned components of HDI in recent years. Explanation: Overall HDI value of India in the year 2019 is 0.649, according to which India is considered as a medium Human Development category. Indians life expectancy at birth was 69.7 years in 2019 which increased by 11.8 years since 1990. Expected years of schooling in India were 12.2 years between 1990 and 2019 the expected years of schooling increase by 4.5 years The standard of living in terms of gross national income per capita is 6681 USD in India which also increased by 273.9% between 1990 and 2019. Hence, the correct option is Option D i.e, 1, 2, 3 and 4 Additional Information: India stands at 131st out of 189 countries and territories according to Human Development Report 2020 released by United Nation development program (UNDP).

q: Which one of the following is the opportunity cost of a chosen activity ?
o: Out of pocket cost plus cost incurred by the Government
Value of next best alternative that is given up
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Opportunity cost is defined as the cost of the next best alternative that was foregone. Explanation: It represents the sacrifices people have to make due to lack of resources. Resources are limited, but desires are limitless, so choices must be made. The opportunity cost of an activity is the value of the next best option that one must sacrifice in order to engage in that activity. Hence, the correct option is Option D i.e, Value of next best alternative that is given up Additional Information: Sunk costs cannot be ignored. The only costs that influence decisions about action are those that can be avoided if action is not taken. To properly assess opportunity cost, the costs and benefits of each available option must be considered and weighed against other options. Considering the value of the opportunity cost allows individuals and organizations to make more profitable decisions. For example, if you spend your time and money on movies, you can't spend that time at home reading a book, and you can't spend that money on something else.

q: The excess of total expenditure of Government over its total receipts, excluding borrowings, is known as
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Fiscal deficit is the term used to determine the difference between the government's total expenditure and its total receipt, including the borrowings. Explanation: The difference between the total income of the government including the total taxes and non-debt capital receipts and its total expenditure is known as fiscal deficit. A very high fiscal deficit shows that the government has been spending more than its income. Borrowing money is a method by which the government meets the fiscal deficit, hence the total borrowing requirements in a financial year are equal to the fiscal deficit in that year. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, Fiscal deficit Additional Information: Fiscal deficit = Total expenditure of the government - Total income of the government Fiscal Deficit = Primary Deficit + Interest Payment

q: Which one of the following is a typical example of monopolistic competition?
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Monopoly refers to business in the market without any competition and has exclusive control of trade and supply of goods in the market. Explanation: In Monopoly market prices are fixed according to the supplier or the provider due to lack of competition in the market. Monopoly market is the downward sloping demand curve. Monopolistic competition is a market in which many companies are present and they produce similar but differentiated products or not identical products, so the best example is the market of soaps. Hence, the correct option is (B). Additional information: Duopoly refers to the market in which only two companies or competitors have the exclusive control of supply and trade of goods in the market. Oligopoly refers to the market in which many companies or small businesses are present and all have the right to supply and trade goods in the market.

q: Which one of the following target groups is the beneficiary of 'Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan' scheme?
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan was launched by the Government of India on 20 June 2020, to minimize the impact of covid 19 pandemic on the all returning migrants rural worker. Explanation: Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan was launched by the P.M. Narendra Modi through video conference in the Telihar village, Kharagai district of Bihar. Main aim of this scheme is to provide employment for 67,0000 workers for 125 days who are directly affected by coronavirus. This scheme was launched in 116 District of 6 states in India Total 12 different Ministries put joint effort for this scheme. Hence, the correct option is (B). Additional information: Covid 19 is a severe acute respiratory syndrome. It is an infectious transmissible disease. The first case was reported in Wuhan, China.

q: The percentage by which the money the borrower pays back exceeds the money that was borrowed is called as
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The nominal interest rate is the percentage that is used by banks, money lenders and investment firms for borrowers to pay back. Explanation: The interest rates on consumer loans are normally called the annual percentage rate (APR). This is the percentage of repayment that lenders ask for as the ability to borrow their money. Annual percentage rate or apr is the annual cost of borrowing money. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, Nominal interest rate Additional Information: Bank Rate: It is the interest rate at which a country's central bank lends money to the banks generally in the form of short-term loans. The central bank directly affects economic activity by managing the bank rate.

q: The situation in an economy which is growing slowly along with rapid inflation (rising price level) is called
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The situation where and economy is growing along with rapid inflation is called Stagflation. Explanation: Stagflation is a time period when a slowly growing economy and unemployment go hand in hand with rapid inflation. Stagflation is mainly caused by cost-push inflation. When some kind of force or condition increases the costs of production, it leads to cost- push inflation. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, Stagflation. Additional Information: Deflation: It is a situation where the prices of goods continue to fall. It is totally opposite of inflation. Deflation generally occurs when there is a rise in productivity, decrease in overall demand or there is decrease in overall volume of credit in the economy.

q: The asset or assets that a borrower pledges in order to guarantee repayment of a loan is called as
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Collateral is an asset that a borrower pledges as a security of repayment of a debt or loan. Explanation: A collateral is property or an asset that an individual or borrower offers as a security for a debt. If the borrower, somehow, is not able to return the agreed repayments, the lender would have the right to seize the offered asset, known as collateral. Collateral reduces the risk for the lenders. Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, Collateral. Additional Information: There are two types of collateralized loans: Mortgages and car loans. Guarantee card: A card that is used, when we pay any cost by cheque, as proof that the bank will pay the certain money.

q: Which one of the following approaches of human development was initially proposed by the International Labour Organisation (ILO) and emphasised on health, education, food, water supply, sanitation and housing ?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Human Development Approach The enhancement of people's lives is the primary goal of development according to the human development (HD) approach. Major facets of the technique are summarised in this publication. Explanation: Basic needs were outlined in the ILO report for the 1976 World Employment Conference in terms of housing, food, clothes, education, and public transportation. October 1919 saw the founding of the ILO. The company's main office is in Geneva, Switzerland. Guy Ryder will be the ILO's director general beginning in March 2021. Employment served as both a means and an aim, and it also encompassed decision- making. This strategy went against the norm, which was to forecast an ideal annual rate of per capita economic growth for the future. Hence, the correct answer is option (b). Additional Information: 1. Welfare approach This method views people as the beneficiaries of development efforts. The strategy supports greater public spending on facilities, amenities, and amenities. 2. Income approach One of the earliest theories of human development is this one. | | Defence The progression of humanity is believed to be related to wealth. The level of human development rises in direct proportion to wealth levels. 3. Capability approach Prof. Amartya Sen is credited for developing this approach. Increasing human development requires strengthening human capacity in the areas of health, education, and resource access.

q: Exchange rates state the value of one currency in terms of other currencies. Which one of the following statements with respect to the exchange rate of currency is correct?
o: Floating exchange rates are rates in which the Governments interfere by buying or selling their currencies
Fixed exchange rates are rates set by Government decisions and maintained by Government actions
Under the Bretton Woods System, the exchange rates are floated in terms of rise or fall in price of gold
Under the classical gold standard, the exchange rates are fixed in terms of price of dollar
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Fixed Exchange Rates are set by Government decisions and maintained by Government actions and it is the value of one country's currency versus the currency of another country. Explanation: Mostly the exchange rates are free floating and they can increase or decrease based on the supply and demand in the market. And there are some currencies which are not free floating and have restrictions. There are a few types of exchange rates: Free floating Restricted currencies Currency peg Onshore versus Offshore Hence, the correct option is Option B i.e, Fixed exchange rates are rates set by Government decisions and maintained by Government actions Additional Information: Floating exchange rate- it is a rate based on the private market by supply and demand. This is opposite to a fixed exchange rate where the government entirely determines the rate.

q: Which of the following measures should be taken when an economy is going through inflationary pressures? 1. The direct taxes should be increased. 2. The interest rate should be reduced. 3. The public. speñding should be increased. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key concept- What is inflation and what are ways to control the same? Explanation Inflation refers to increase in price of goods and services or reduction in the value of currency. Inflation generally occurs when too much money chases few goods i.e the money supply in the market is high as well as demand for goods and services is high while availability of goods and services is low. So inorder to control inflation, we need to reduce money supply and public spending in the market. If government increases the direct tax, it will reduce income of individuals and therefore will reduce money supply in the market. This will therefore control the inflation. Hence, statement 1 is correct. If government lowers the interest rates, it becomes easier and cheaper to borrow money and therefore increases money supply and spending in the economy. This step will again boost the already existing inflation. Hence, statement 2 is incorrect. If government increases public spending, it will increase the money supply in the market, | | Defence which in turn will boost the inflation. So government reduces the public spending to control inflation. Hence, statement 3 is incorrect. Hence, the correct answer is option (a)

q: The increase in private investment spending induced by the increase in Government spending is known as
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Crowding is a situation when high government spending leads to a growth in private sector investment because there are more profitable investments opportunities. Explanation: When the government spends highly by borrowing it leads to two possible effects- crowding in or crowding out. Crowding in- this is related to a situation when higher government spending encourages private forms to invest more full stop. This occurs because of the income effect of higher government spending. If the economy of the country is going through recession, crowding in can be very helpful increasing the private sector Investments. Hence, the correct option is (a). Additional Information: Crowding out- higher government spending is when financed by the borrowing it leads to decrease in savings of the private sector.

q: Which one of the following is not a function of money ?
o: Acts as an intermediate in the exchange process
Acts as a store of value
Used as the unit of account
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Money is not used for regulating consumption. Money is used as a medium of exchange, as a store of value, and as a unit of account. Explanation: The most important function of money is as a medium of exchange to help in transactions. Money should hold value over time, hence must be a store of value. Money also works as a unit of account, by providing a common measure of the value of goods and services. Hence, the correct option is Option D i.e, Used for regulating consumption Additional Information: Barter system is an exchange of goods or services. A barter exchange is a method in which people contract with each other to exchange property or goods according to their needs.

q: Which one of the following may lead to movement along the demand curve of a commodity ?
o: Change in price of the other commodities
Change in income of the consumer
Change in tastes and preferences of consumers
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: A change in the price of a product leads to a movement along a specific demand curve and it generally causes some change in the demand of the product but it does not shift the demand curve. Explanation: A demand curve graphically represents the relationship between price of the product and the quantity of the product demanded. There is a shift in the demand curve whenever a determinant of demand other than the price of the product changes. Even when the price remains the same, there should at least one of the Other five determinants of the demand change. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) . Additional Information: The other five determinants of the demand are- Income of the buyer Consumer taste and preferences Expectations of future changes of goods, supply, needs etc Price of products Number of potential consumers

q: Consider the following statements about the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) : 1. The MSMEs comprise a dynamic sector of the Indian economy, providing large employment opportunities. 2. MSMEs require low capital costs and help the industrialization of backward regions. 3. MSMEs contribute enormously to socio- economic development. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: MSME stands for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. MSMEs are businesses that produce, process, and preserve goods and commodities. These are broadly classified based on their investment in plant and machinery for manufacturing or equipment for service enterprises, as well as their annual turnover. MSME Regulation in India: In 2007, the Ministry of Small-Scale Industries and the Ministry of Agro and Rural Industries merged to form the Ministry of Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises. This ministry develops policies, facilitates programs, and monitors implementation to support MSMEs' growth. The Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises Development Act of 2006 addresses various issues affecting MSMEs, establishes a National Board for MSMEs, defines the concept of "enterprise," and empowers the Central Government to enhance MSME competitiveness Explanation: Being a diverse sector in the Indian economy, it provided a large scale of enterprises. It requires low-cost capital. MSMEs not only play crucial role in providing large employment opportunities at comparatively lower capital cost but also help in industrialization of rural & backward areas, thereby, reducing regional | | Defence imbalances, assuring more equitable distribution of national income and wealth. Hence, the correct answer is option D.

q: During 2020-21, when India was passing through the adverse effects of COVID-19, which one of the following sectors witnessed positive growth ?
o: Electricity, gas, water supply, and other utility services
Financial, real estate and professional services
Public administration, defense, and other services
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- During 2020-21 India was hit by Covid - 19 A contagious illness called Covid - 19 (coronavirus disease) is brought on by the SARS-coV-2 virus. It is an epidemic by which the whole world shook. Because of the lockdown many sectors got affected but electricity, gas, water supply, and other utility services show positive growth. Hence, the correct option is ‘b’ i.e. Electricity, gas, water supply, and other utility services

q: Which one of the following is the first State in India to have 100 percent households with tap water connection?
subject: economy
exp: Key points- Under Jal Jeevan Mission, Goa is the first state to have 100% households with a tap water connection. Explanation- Goa is the first State in India to have100% households with tap water connection in the rural areas covering 2.30 lakh households. The main purpose of the Jal Jeevan Mission of the central government is to provide piped water to all rural households by 2024. Hence, the correct option is ‘B’ i.e. Goa.

q: Consider the following statements regarding e- RUPI prepaid digital vouchers : 1. The maximum amount of e-RUPI is rupees one lakh. 2. Beneficiaries can receive e-RUPI without having a bank account. 3. To receive e-RUPI on mobile phones, beneficiaries need a smart phone with Internet connection. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key points- e-RUPI prepaid digital vouchers:- The Reserve Bank of India on Thursday increased the maximum amount of e-RUPI prepaid digital vouchers from ₹10,000 presently to ₹1 lakh. RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das declared that the e-RUPI voucher can be used more than once till the amount is all redeemed. Explanations: It is mainly a digital voucher, which a beneficiary gets on his phone by an SMS or QR code. New instructions for e- RUPI as issued by RBI in February 2022. The maximum quantity of e- RUPI from Rs. 10,000 to Rs. 1 lakh. The voucher can be used more than once, until the amount is completely redeemed. Beneficiaries can get e-RUPI, even without having a bank account. Hence, the correct answer is option ‘A’. i.e Both 1 and 2.

q: Which one of the following statements about Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) is not correct?
o: NBFCs cannot issue cheques drawn on themselves.
NBFCs cannot offer deposit insurance facility of Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation.
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- NBFcomes under the Company Act of 1956. In comparison with banks NBFC cannot accept demand deposits. They cannot issue cheques drawn on themselves They can give loans such as home loans. They lend and made investments. They cannot offer the deposit insurance facilities of Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation. Hence, the correct option is (B).

q: The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has revised the base year index of Eight Core Industries having a combined weight of about 40 - 27 percent in the Index of Industrial Production. Which one of the following is not one of the Eight Core Industries ?
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- Eight core Industries contain 1. Coal 2. Crude oil 3. Natural gas 4. Refinery Products 5. Fertilizers 6. Steel 7. Cement 8. Electricity Eight core industries account for 40.27n% of the weight of of items included in the Index of Industrial Production(IIP). Hence, the correct option is ‘C’ i.e. Rubber products.

q: Which one of the following forms of money supply is considered as the most widely used in the Indian monetary system ?
subject: economy
exp: Key points- The total amount of money in an economy at any given time is called the Money supply. Explanation- Price level (inflation) and interest rates on deposits & loans are two factors where Money supply plays a crucial role M3 (Aggregate monetary resources) the most widely used in the Indian monetary system. M1= currency with public + demand deposits in Bank M2 = M1+ Saving deposits of post office savings banks M3= M1+ Time deposits with the banking system M4= M3+ All deposits with the post office savings banks. Hence, the correct option is ‘C’ i.e. M3.

q: Which one of the following does not influence quantity demanded for a good?
o: Prices of inputs into production of the good
subject: economy
exp: the demand of goods- it is an economic principle that refers to a consumer's desire to buy goods and services and his willingness to pay a certain amount of money for a particular good or service. The factors that directly affect the demand or the shift in demand are income of the consumer, buyers taste and preferences, expectation of future prices and supplies, the price of its related goods and the number of potential consumers. Hence, the correct option is Option (D) i.e, Prices of inputs into production of the good. Additional Information: The consumers buy more of a product which is low priced and less of a product which is at high price. This inverse relationship between the price of the product and the amount of buyers who are willing to buy that product is referred to as the Law of Demand. Demand curve is a graphically represented relationship between the price of the product and the quantity of the product demanded by the buyers.

q: Which one among the following reports defines: Sustainable Development as development that meets the needs of the present generation without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs?
o: Agenda-2 of the Rio Earth Summit
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- The Brundtland Report, officially known as "Our Common Future," was published by the World Commission on Environment and Development in 1987. The report is widely recognized as a landmark document in the history of sustainable development. The report defined sustainable development as "development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs." The Brundtland Report helped to raise awareness of the need for sustainable development and has been influential in shaping international agreements and policies, including the 1992 United Nations Conference on Environment and Development (UNCED) and the subsequent adoption of Agenda 21, a comprehensive plan of action to build a global partnership for sustainable development. Hence, the Correct option is A i.e. Brundtland report.

q: Which one of the following Labour Codes notified by the Government of India incorporated various provisions of the Central Labour Acts such as the Factories Act, 1948 and Plantations Labour Act, 1951?
o: The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020
subject: economy
exp: Key points- There are four Labour Codes In India namely, the Code on Wages 2019, the Industrial relation Code 2020, the Code on Social Security 2020 and the occupational, safety, Health, and working Conditions Code,2020. Explanation- The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code,2020 incorporated various provisions of the Central Labour Acts such as the Factories Act,1948 and Plantations Labour Act . Aim: To amend the laws governing the occupational safety, healthy and working conditions of the persons employed in an establishment. Hence, the correct option is ‘A’ i.e. The Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020.

q: Who among the following formulated the concept of poverty as a measurable development indicator in the Indian subcontinent?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The earliest estimation of poverty line was made by Dadabhai Naoroji in his book poverty and unbritish rule in India. Explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji in his book "Poverty and Un-British Rule in India" mentioned the estimation of poverty line. He estimated Rs.16 to Rs.35 per capita per year. The poverty line he proposed was entirely based on the cost of a subsistence or minimum basic diet. Poverty Estimation is a method commonly used to estimate poverty in India and it is based on income or consumption level. If the consumption or the income per capita Falls below a given minimum level then the household is said to be below the poverty line or BPL. In India now, Poverty Estimation is carried out by NITI Aayog's force by the calculation of the poverty line based on the data recorded by the National Sample Survey office. Hence, the correct option is (a). Additional Information: Professor Vijendra Kasturi Ranga Varadaraja Rao was a multi-talented personality, an economist, an educationist, an | | Defence administrator and an intuition builder. Mahadev Govind Ranade was born in 1842, in Nashik Maharashtra, he passed his Lord degree and then he was appointed as a judge at Bombay High Court he was one of the members who founded Prarthana Samaj. Romesh Chandra Dutt was seen as a national leader of the pre Gandhian era. He was a contemporary of Dadabhai naoroji and Justice Ranade. He, broadly known as RC Dutt, was a Civil Servant, political leader, economic thinker and a writer.

q: Among the following countries, where did the idea of 'Social Market Economy' emerge in 1950?
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Social market economy Explanation The idea of 'Social Market Economy' first emerge in Germany Hence, the correct answer is option(A) i.e, Germany. Additional Information After the nazi regime, there is a realization that if the gap between rich and poor will be wide then there are the chances of the rise of extreme policies( right wing or left wing) therefore every citizen should get the economic stability and growth and hence the concept of social market economy emerged

q: While forming the 'Indo-Pacific Economic Framework for Prosperity', the member countries launched collective discussions towards future negotiations on four pillars. Which one of the following is not one of those pillars?
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- Indo-Pacific Economic Framework for Prosperity(IPEF) has four pillars- 1. Clean energy, decarbonization, and infrastructure. 2. Supply chain. 3. Tax and anti-corruption. 4. Trade It is an initiative between the US and 13 other member countries e.g. India, Australia, etc strive toward a connected, resilient, clean, and fair economy. Hence, the correct option is ‘B’ i.e. Defense cooperation and intelligence sharing.

q: Which one of the following statements with regard to the National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, 2005 is correct?
o: It ensures 175 days of employment to needy persons in rural areas.
It gives higher wages to women workers.
People are generally given both skilled and unskilled jobs.
This is implemented only in rural areas of India.
subject: economy
exp: The Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act was earlier known as National Rural Employment Guarantee Act which was passed in 2005 to secure Employment in India. This scheme works under the Ministry Of Rural Development and it is a demand driven wage employment scheme. Any adult member of a household in a rural area in India with a job card is eligible for a job under this scheme. This scheme secures 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year to every adult member who is eligible for this scheme. Every district in India comes under this scheme accept those who have 100% Urban population Hence, the correct option is Option (D) i.e, This is implemented only in rural areas of India. Additional information: 56% of the MGNREGA workers are women. Unlike the minimum wages, under MGNREGA, men and women both are paid equal wages.

q: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) was established in the year
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: On October 1st, 2016, the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) was created. The IBBI amends and regulates the laws relating to Bankruptcy Code, 2016. Explanation: It was formed under Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016. Hence, the correct answer is Option (c).

q: Which one of the following assists a country through 'Extended Fund Facility', often talked about in the news
o: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept:- The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is a major financial agency of the United Nations and an international financial institution, with its headquarters in Washington, D.C. Explanation:- When a country faces serious medium- term balance of payments problems because of structural sins that bear time to address, the IMF can help through an Extended Fund Facility( EFF). Compared to backing handed under the Stand- by Arrangement, backing under an extended arrangement features longer program engagement — to help countries apply medium- term structural reforms and a longer prepayment period. Hence, the correct answer is Option ‘B’ i.e., International Monetary Fund.

q: Infant mortality ratio of which one of the following countries is comparable to that of India?
subject: economy
exp: India's infant mortality rate is 32 infant deaths per 1,000 live births. India is neither in the top countries with the highest infant mortality rate nor in those countries which have the lowest infant mortality rate. Infant mortality rate has decreased and it indicates an increase in medical care facilities and hygiene. It also shows decrease in female infanticide. Hence, the correct option is Option D i.e, None of the above. Additional Information: Globally the infant mortality rate is 29 dates per 1000 live births in 2018 it was calculated 65 deaths per thousand live births in 1990. The infant mortality rate in the United States is 5.8 and in China it is 5.5 per 1000 like birth calculated in 2020. In Sri Lanka this rate is 5.9 deaths per thousand live births.

q: Recently, the term 'two-pillar solution/two- pillar package' often seen in the news, refers to
o: Global energy security in near future
subject: economy
exp: The first pillar's objective is to ensure a better distribution of taxation of multinationals as per the countries in which they operate. The second Pillar aims to reduce tax competition on corporate profits. It will be done by starting a global minimum tax of 15% from 2023. After implementation it will have the potential to solve the complex problems of the very old-existing international tax system. It will also help in making it compatible with the digital revolution going on nowadays. Hence, the correct option is Option (C) i.e, Minimum global corporate tax. Additional Information: OECD stands for Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development. OECD is an international organization that aims to build better policies for better lives. OECD has 38 member countries which are inter- governmental, it was founded in 1961 to start the economic progress and world trade.

q: Which one of the following statements about a borrower from a Microfinance Company is not correct?
o: The borrower should not have annual income beyond a limit.
The borrower should not seek loan amount beyond a limit.
The borrower should not refuse to offer a collateral.
The borrower should not refuse to pay any rate of interest offered.
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- Like a bank, a microfinance company is a provider of credit. The borrower is generally from a low-income background. Loans under Microfinance Companies are small loans i.e. micro finance. Microfinance loans do not require collateral. These loans are generally repaid at higher interest. Hence, the correct option is ‘D’ i.e.the borrower should not refuse to pay any rate of interest offered.

q: Who among the following, first mooted the idea of deficit financing?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: John Maynard Keynes first mooted the idea of deficit financing. He used it as a compensatory spending measure to solve the problem of unemployment and depression. Explanation: Deficit Financing can be defined as a practice where the government's higher spending exceeds its received as a revenue, the difference being made up by borrowing. It generally occurs when the total income of the government falls below its total expenditure. This deficit is financed by borrowing money from the central bank or commercial banks by the government. The government generally withdraws money from its deposits in the Reserve Bank of India or asks the RBI to print new currency notes or sometimes it lends money from the public in the form of bonds or security. Hence, the correct option is Option C i.e, John Maynard Keynes Additional Information: Adam Smith was a Scottish economist and he is also known as the Father of economics or the Father of capitalism. Adam Smith has done impressive work in The theory of moral sentiments, 1759 An enquiry into nature and causes of the wealth of nations, 1776 Alfred Marshall - The general principles of pricing were given by Alfred Marshall and a textbook named, Principle of Economics by Alfred Marshall was published in 1890.

q: Which one of the following would be considered as Foreign Direct Investment?
o: A foreign company buying shares in stock exchanges in India
A foreign country pension fund investing in Indian stock markets
A foreign merchant banker buying shares from Indian stock markets
A foreign entity setting up an educational institution in India
subject: economy
exp: Foreign direct investment (FDI) is a type of international investment in which an investor of one economy establishes a interest in and influence over a company resided in another country. Foreign direct investment is different from the foreign portfolio investments where the foreign firm buys equity shares of a company. FDI includes following- Subsidiaries of foreign companies in India Majority of a liquidity holding in Indian companies Day to day operations of the company It is a major source of non debt financial resources Companies are exclusively financed by foreign companies Hence, the correct option is Option (D) i.e, A foreign entity setting up an educational institution in India. Additional Information: A few industries where the FDI is strictly prohibited under any circumstances.These industries are atomic energy generation, gambling business, lotteries, investment in chit funds, nidhi company, agriculture plantation activities, cigar cigarettes or any tobacco industry, etc.

q: Which of the following factors signify monopolistic competition ? 1. Differentiated products 2. Large number of buyers and sellers 3. Barriers to entry 4. Homogeneous products Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Monopolistic competition- it is a type of imperfect competition where many firms sell products and services that are similar to each other but are not substitutes of each other. In this type of competition more than one firm cell differentiate products that can arise from characteristics of goods or services, the location from which the farm is selling the products or intangible aspects or perceptions about the products. These many firms offer the products which are similar but not identical and there is easy entry and exit of the firms in the market. Hence, the correct option is Option (A) i.e, 1 and 2 only. Additional Information: Economic market can be divided into four types: perfect competition, monopolistic competition, oligopoly, and monopoly. These categories are different because of the difference in the number of producers which is many in perfect and monopolistic competition, few in oligopoly, and one in monopoly.

q: Which one of the following terms denotes the inputs in terms of tools, machines, buildings, raw materials and money in hand required at any stage of production ?
subject: economy
exp: Physical capital is generally defined as tools, machines and buildings. The tools and machines either very simple like farmers plough or very sophisticated machines such as the turbines, computers, generators etc all come under physical capital. Production of any good requires a variety of raw materials such as cloth for a textile company or yarn used by a weaver or the clay used by the potter. And some money is always needed in hand during the work of production to make necessary payments or to buy items. Hence, the raw materials and money in hand are known as working capital. These raw materials are used up in the production, not like the tools, machines and buildings. Hence, the correct option is Option (C) i.e, Physical capital Additional Information: Human capital means the group of people who are required to work at the production center full stop. These people should be equipped with required education and skills used during the production.

q: Which one of the following is not a permanent member of the United Nations Security Council?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The United Nations Security Council was established by the UN Charter in 1945. UNSC is one of 6 principal organs of the United Nations. The principal organs of the United Nations are the General Assembly and Trusteeship Council. Economic and Social Council, International Court of Justice and Secretariat. Explanation: The primary role of the UN is to maintain International Peace and Security. The head of UNSC is located in New York. UNSC has 15 members, i.e. 5 Permanent members and 10 non-permanent members. 5 permanent members are the United States of America, France, China, Russia and the United Kingdom. India is a non-permanent member of the UNSC. Hence, the correct option is (C).

q: Which of the following are the effects of devaluation or depreciation of currency? 1. It lends to an increase in imports and a decrease in exports 2. It leads to an increase in exports and a decrease in imports. 3. It leads to an increase in domestic inflation. 4. It lends to a decrease in domestic inflation Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: | | Defence Key Concept: A devaluation is an act of government declaring its currency as less valuable than other currencies. Explanation: Effects of Currency Devaluation Explanation Reduces export costs for a country, making its goods more competitive globally Currency devaluation lowers the relative price of a country's exports, making them more attractive to foreign buyers and potentially boosting export demand. Increases the cost of imports, making foreign goods relatively more expensive for domestic consumers Devaluation raises the price of imported goods, dampening domestic demand for foreign products, potentially reducing import volumes. Enhances competitiveness in international markets Cheaper exports due to devaluation can improve a country's position in global markets, increasing its share of international trade and potentially boosting export revenues. Helps narrow the trade deficit by increasing export revenues and decreasing import expenditures Devaluation can help reduce the trade deficit by stimulating exports and discouraging imports, improving the balance between export earnings and import spending. Effects of Currency Devaluation Explanation Contributes to domestic inflation by raising the prices of imported goods The increased cost of imports following a currency devaluation can contribute to inflation domestically as consumers and businesses face higher prices for foreign goods. Offers protection to domestic industries from foreign competition Higher import prices due to devaluation can make domestic goods more competitive than foreign alternatives, providing a shield against foreign competition. May reduce incentives for domestic industries to improve productivity and efficiency due to reduced competition from imports Protection from foreign competition resulting from devaluation may reduce the pressure on domestic industries to innovate and improve efficiency, potentially leading to stagnation. Can spur aggregate demand through increased exports, potentially fueling long-term inflation Devaluation-induced export growth can boost overall economic activity, potentially leading to higher inflation if not accompanied by measures to control demand or increase production | | Defence Hence, the correct option is C, i.e. '2 and 3.'

q: Which of the following concepts are considered pillars of the Human Development ? 1. Equity 2. Sustainability 8. Productivity 4. Security Select the correct answer using the code given below:
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Human Development is a process of extending opportunities, livelihood, and improvement of well-being. Mahbub ul Haq introduced the concept of human development. Explanation: The Main Pillars of the Human Development Index are: 1. Equity: To make access to opportunities available to everyone. 2. Productivity: Human labour productivity. 3. Sustainability: Continues availability of opportunities. 4. Empowerment: Having the power to make choices. Hence, the correct option is C, i.e. '1,2,3'

q: Which one of the following taxes is not subsumed under the Goods and Services Tax in India?
o: Taxes on Petroleum and Petroleum Products
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Goods and Services Tax (GST) was introduced on 1 July 2017. Many central and state indirect taxes are subsumed in GST. Explanation: GST is a destination-based tax and GST Council is responsible for increasing and decreasing tax slabs and transferring subjects from one slab to another. Goods that are beyond the scope of GST are High-speed diesel, Natural Gas, Aviation fuel, Petroleum and Petroleum Products, and Liquor. Some Central taxes subsumed under GST are Central Excise duty, Service Tax, Central Surcharge and Cess, Customs duties excise duties, etc. Some State taxes subsumed under GST are the Central Sales tax, State VAT, Advertisement tax, Purchase tax, Tax on gambling, betting, and lotteries, Entertainment Tax, Octroi tax, and Entry tax. Hence, the correct option is D i.e. 'Taxes on Petroleum and Petroleum Products.'

q: Which one of the following expenditure is subtracted from the Fiscal Deficit to arrive at the Primary Deficit?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Primary Deficit is the difference between the current year's fiscal deficit and interest payment on earlier borrowings. Explanation: Aspe ct Details Defi nitio n The difference between the current year's fiscal deficit and interest payments on earlier borrowings. Also, the difference between current spending on goods and services and total current revenue | | Defence Aspe ct Details from all types of taxes net of transfer payments. Calc ulati on Meth od Primary Deficit = Total Revenue - Total expenditure excluding interest payments on its debt. Com pone nts of Calc ulati on Total Revenue: All revenue collected by the government, including taxes and other income. Total Expenditure: Government spending on goods, services, and transfer payments. Interest Payments: Payments made by the government on borrowings from creditors. Signi fican ce Indicates a government's fiscal health by showing how much of its spending is covered by revenue, excluding interest payments on debt. The primary deficit is an important indicator of a government's fiscal position. It shows whether the government can meet its expenses from revenue sources, excluding interest payments on debt. Hence, the correct option is C, i.e. 'Interest Payments.'

q: The sustained decrease in the general price level is called as
subject: economy
exp: Deflation is the sustained decrease in the general price level of goods and services in an economy over a period of time. This means that the purchasing power of money increases as prices fall. Deflation can occur due to various factors, such as a decrease in demand, technological advancements leading to lower production costs, or tight monetary policy. It is the opposite of inflation, where prices generally rise over time. Hence the correct answer is option A

q: Which of the following is/are United Nations Sustainable Development Goal(s)? 1. Decent work and economic growth 2. Peace, justice, and strong institutions 3. Responsible consumption and production Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: "The Sustainable Development Goals agenda was accepted by all members of the United Nations in 2012 at the Rio De Janeiro Council Meet with an aim to promote a healthy and developed future of the planet and its people. It was in 2015 when the Sustainable Development Goals were implemented after a successful fifteen-year plan of development called the Millennium Development Goals.

q: The Wholesale Price Inflation has increased in India during 2021-2022 due to which of the following factors? 1. Sharp increase in international pricen of crude oil 2. Decrease in economic activity post-Covid 3. Disruption of global supply chain 4. High freight cost Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Wholesale Price Index is a measure of changes in the price of goods sold and traded in bulk by wholesale businesses to their businesses. The Consumer Price Index measures price changes from a retail buyer's perspective. Explanation: The Office of Economic Advisor releases WPI, and CPI is released by the Central Statistical Office. As per PIB data, Wholesale Price Inflation based on the Wholesale Price Index rose to 12.5% during 2021-22 (From April to December. Factors responsible for this increase are: 1. Low Base in the previous year. 2. The sharp increase in International crude oil prices and other inputs. 3. High freight costs 4. Pick up in economic activities. Hence, the correct option is D, i.e. '1 and 4 only.'

q: Which one of the following indicators is included in the National Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
subject: economy
exp: Each dimension of poverty in the provided list represents an aspect of well-being or living standards. The indicators associated with each dimension are used to assess whether a household is deprived in that particular aspect. Here's an explanation: 1. Nutrition: This dimension focuses on the nutritional status of individuals within the household. A household is considered deprived in this dimension if there is an undernourished adult under 70 years of age or a child. 2. Health: This dimension concerns the health status of individuals within the household. A household is considered deprived if any child under the age of 18 years has died in the five years preceding the survey, indicating high child mortality. 3. Years of schooling: This dimension reflects the educational attainment within the household. A household is considered deprived if no member aged 10 years or older has completed at least six years of schooling. 4. Education attendance: This dimension assesses school attendance among school-aged children. A household is considered deprived if any school-aged child is not attending school up to the age at which he/she would complete class 8. 5. Cooking Fuel: This indicator relates to the availability of clean energy sources for cooking. A household is considered deprived if it relies on inefficient or polluting cooking fuels like dung, wood, charcoal, or coal. 6. Sanitation: This dimension evaluates access to proper sanitation facilities. A household is considered deprived if its sanitation facility is not improved according to SDG guidelines or if it is shared with other households. 7. Standard of living: This dimension encompasses various aspects such as access to clean water, electricity, adequate housing, and asset ownership. A household is considered deprived if it | | Defence lacks improved drinking water access, electricity, adequate housing materials, or ownership of essential assets. These dimensions and associated indicators collectively provide a comprehensive understanding of multidimensional poverty, capturing various aspects beyond just income. Each indicator contributes to identifying different facets of poverty, allowing policymakers to target interventions effectively. Hence the correct answer is option A.

q: SDG: India Index, developed by NITI Aayog. Includes 17 SDGs for each state. Which one of the following is not included in that index?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: NITI Aayog releases the SDG India Index. It is a document of progress made by States and UTs of India towards achieving the 2030 SDG targets. Explanation: India spans 16 out of 17 SDGs in the 2018 index. The SDG India index is developed with the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation(MoSPI), the Global Green Growth Institute, and the UN. States are given scores as follows: Aspirant :0-49 Performer:50-64 Front Runner:65-99 Achiever:100 Hence, the correct option is B i.e.' Reduced Unemployment.'

q: Other things remaining constant, the marke supply for a good increases if: 1. its price increases 2. price of its factors of production decreases. 3. price of other goods decreases Select the correct answer using the code given below
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: According to the Law of Supply, when the price of a good increases, its supply also increases, and its demand decreases until an equilibrium is established. Explanation: When the price increases then sellers start to produce more goods which increases the supply. When the price of production factors like labour, land, and capital decreases, the cost of production also decreases, leading to more production and more goods in the market. When the price of other complementary goods (goods which are used jointly) decreases then there will be increased demand of its complement and hence, the market supply of other goods also increases in the market. Hence, the correct option is D i.e. '1,2,3.'

q: Which of the following policies help to raise interest rate unambiguously and thereby lead to appreciation of currency?
o: Contractionary fiscal policy and expansionary monetary policy
Contractionary monetary policy and expansionary fiscal policy
subject: economy
exp: Contractionary monetary policy involves measures taken by the central bank to reduce the money supply or increase interest rates. This typically involves actions like raising the central bank's policy interest rate, selling government securities, or increasing reserve requirements for banks. These actions aim to reduce the availability of credit and money in the economy, leading to higher interest rates. Higher interest rates attract foreign capital as investors seek better returns on their investments. Increased demand for the domestic currency to invest in higher-yielding assets leads to an appreciation of the currency. On the other hand, expansionary fiscal policy involves increasing government spending or reducing taxes to stimulate economic activity. While this policy might initially put downward pressure on interest rates due to increased government borrowing, the simultaneous contractionary monetary policy would outweigh this effect by raising interest rates. Thus, the combination of contractionary monetary policy (raising interest rates) and expansionary fiscal policy (increased government spending or tax cuts) helps to unambiguously raise interest rates and lead to the appreciation of the currency. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: What would be the impact on the economy if people start holding more currency in hand and less in deposits?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The money multiplier is a concept which measures the amount of money created by banks with the help of deposits after excluding the amount set for reserves from the deposits. Explanation: As people start holding more money as cash in their hands, there will be less money to deposit in the bank. The capacity of banks to lend money will also decrease in this situation. Liquidity and money supply in the economy will decrease. In this situation, the Money multiplier will decrease. Hence, the correct option is (B).

q: The unemployment that occurs due to changes in the technology or in the demand for particular products is called
subject: economy
exp: Structural unemployment occurs due to shifts in the structure of an economy, such as changes in technology, consumer preferences, or the composition of industries. These changes can lead to certain skills becoming obsolete or industries shrinking, causing workers to become unemployed. Unlike frictional unemployment, which occurs due to temporary transitions between jobs, structural unemployment arises from more fundamental changes in the economy's structure. Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Consider the following statements regarding poverty in India: 1. The poverty line in India is based on minimum requirements for food, clothing, fuel, education, and healthcare. 2. India's accepted average calorie requirement is 2400 calories per person per day in rural areas and 2100 calories per person per day in urban areas. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: An individual is considered to be poor if his/her income or consumption level falls below the minimum level that he/she is not able to fulfill their basic needs. Explanation: The poverty line in India is determined based on factors such as food requirements, clothing, footwear, fuel, light, medical, and educational requirements for subsistence. The present estimation of the poverty line is based on calorie requirements. The accepted average calorie requirement in rural areas is 2400 calories per person per day, and in urban areas, it is 2100 calories per person per day. The Alagh Committee, the Lakdawala Committee, the Tendulkar Committee, and the Rangrajan Committee are related to poverty estimation in India. Presently, poverty line calculation in India is carried out by NITI Aayog's task force based on the data of the National Sample Survey Office. Hence, the correct option is C i.e. 'Both 1 and 2.'

q: The contraction of private investment spending due to deficit spending by the Government is called
subject: economy
exp: | | Defence Crowding out refers to a situation in which increased government spending, often financed through borrowing, reduces private investment spending. When the government borrows funds to finance its deficit spending, it competes with the private sector for available funds in the financial markets. This increased demand for funds tends to raise interest rates. Higher interest rates make borrowing more expensive for businesses and individuals, reducing their willingness and ability to invest in new projects or make purchases, thus crowding out private investment. Therefore, the contraction of private investment spending due to government deficit spending is referred to as crowding out. Hence the correct answer is option A.

q: Which of the following action(s) by the Government would lead to contraction of money supply in the economy? 1. Purchase of Treasury Bills by the central bank from public. 2. Sale of Treasury Bills by the contral bank to public. 3. Sale of foreign exchange by the central bank. 4. Purchase of foreign exchange by the central bank. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Treasury bills are a short-term tool used by RBI to regulate the total money supply in the economy. These are zero coupon interest-free securities 4 types of treasury bills are 14 days,91 days,182 days, and 364 days. Sale of treasury bills by the central bank to the public: When the central bank sells treasury bills to the public, it takes money out of the economy, thus contracting the money supply. To maintain the fixed exchange rate, the central bank must intervene and sell foreign exchange to buy domestic currency. The foreign exchange market intervention will decrease the domestic money supply and shift the LM curve back to LM to restore the initial equilibrium at e. Hence, the correct option is (C).

q: Consider the following statements about the Public Distribution System : 1. The Public Distribution System is operated under the joint responsibility of Centre and State/Union Territories. 2. Food Corporation of India has assumed the responsibility for procurement, storage and transportation of food grains to the State Governments. 3. Both Centre and State have the joint responsibility to identify eligible beneficiaries. 4. Some States also distribute additional items of mass consumption through the PDS outlets such as pulses, edible oil, ete. Which of the statements given above are correct?
subject: economy
exp: The Public Distribution System is a food security system that is established under the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food, and Public Distribution. It is a system of management of scarcity of food through the food grains distribution at affordable prices. It ensures Food and Nutritional security in the country. It is operated under the combined responsibility of Central and State governments. Buffer stocks of food grains are maintained in the warehouses under this scheme. FCI(Food Corporation of India) is responsible for the procurement, storage, transportation, and allocation of food grains to state governments. State government is responsible for identifying beneficiaries, issuing ration cards, and supervising Fair Price Shops, etc. Presently, Kerosene oil, rice, sugar, wheat, pulses, edible oils, spices, and iodized oils are also distributed through PDS outlets. Hence, the correct option is B i.e. '1,2, and 4.'

q: Which one of the following is a measure that can be used by the Government for combatting inflation?
o: Increasing the non-planned expenditure on defence, police, etc.
Increasing the rate of interest on savings and fixed deposits
Reduction in the cash reserve ratio (CRR)
subject: economy
exp: Increasing the rate of interest on savings and fixed deposits is a contractionary monetary policy measure that can help combat inflation. When interest rates on savings and fixed deposits are increased, it encourages people to save more and reduces their propensity to spend. With reduced spending, there is lower demand for goods and services in the economy, which can help alleviate inflationary pressures. Additionally, higher interest rates can also discourage borrowing for consumption and investment purposes, further reducing aggregate demand and curbing inflationary tendencies. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: In India, the contribution of food inflation to overall inflation is around 1/3rd to 2/5th. Within food inflation, contribution of food articles is higher because price rise in food articles is
o: higher and their weight is also higher compared to food products
higher but their weight is lower compared to food products
lower but their weight is higher compared to food products
lower and their weight is also lower compared to food products
subject: economy
exp: Key concept When there is an increase in food prices and the demand remains constant but the supply decreases this lead to food price inflation and can cause social unrest Explanation Contribution of food inflation to the overall inflation is high in India around to And within the food inflation contribution of food articles is higher because price rise in food articles is higher and their weight is also higher compared to food products Hence, the correct answer is option(A) i.e, higher and their weight is also higher compared to food products

q: Which among the following is/are the objective/objectives of the NITI Aayog? 1. Imposing policies on the States/UTs 2. Allocation of funds at National and State levels 3. Design strategies and long-term policies and programme frameworks Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) is a policy think tank of the Government of India, established with the | | Defence aim of achieving sustainable development goals. Its objectives include: 1. Imposing policies on the States/UTs - This is not within the mandate of NITI Aayog. It does not possess the authority to impose policies on states/UTs. 2. Allocation of funds at National and State levels - While NITI Aayog does not have the power to allocate funds, it does play a role in recommending the allocation of funds to different sectors at both national and state levels. 3. Design strategies and long-term policies and programme frameworks - This is indeed one of the primary objectives of NITI Aayog. Hence, the correct answer is option d. The features of NITI Aayog are mentioned below: 1. Facilitate the development of a unified vision for national development priorities, sectors, and strategies through active collaboration with states. 2. Promote cooperative federalism by establishing structured support initiatives and mechanisms, recognizing the pivotal role of states in national progress. 3. Create mechanisms for crafting credible plans starting from the village level, progressively aggregating them at higher levels of governance. 4. Ensure that national security interests are integrated into economic strategies and policies as and when necessary. 5. Prioritize marginalized sections of society to ensure they adequately benefit from economic progress. 6. Formulate strategic and long-term policy frameworks and initiatives, actively monitoring their progress and effectiveness. Utilize feedback for innovative improvements and necessary corrections. 7.
