Provide advice and foster partnerships among stakeholders, including national and international think tanks, educational institutions, and policy research bodies. 8. Establish a knowledge and innovation support system by collaborating with national and international experts, practitioners, and partners. 9. Offer a platform for resolving inter- sectoral and inter-departmental issues to accelerate the implementation of the development agenda. 10. Maintain a cutting-edge resource centre, serving as a repository of research on governance best practices, sustainable development, and equitable growth. Facilitate dissemination to stakeholders. 11. Actively monitor and evaluate program implementation, identifying required resources to enhance the likelihood of success and broaden the scope of delivery. 12. Focus on technology upgradation and capacity building to effectively implement programs and initiatives. 13. Undertake additional activities as necessary to advance the national development agenda and achieve stated objectives.

q: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) relate to
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) relate to the International Monetary Fund. Explanation- The SDR is a reserve asset that was established by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) in and can be traded between nations in exchange for liquidity, or cash. SDRs are not a currency and do not represent IMF claims. However, they are an international reserve asset that can be converted into reserve currencies with the assistance of other countries. Hence, the correct option is ‘d’ i.e the International Monetary Fund.

q: Which one of the following situations can lead to inflation?
o: Rapid growth of aggregate demand outweighing supply
subject: economy
exp: A. Rapid growth of aggregate demand outweighing supply: Rapid growth of aggregate demand refers to a situation where there is a significant increase in the total demand for goods and services within an economy over a relatively short period. When demand grows faster than supply, it creates excess demand in the economy. This leads to increased competition for available goods and services, which in turn drives up prices. As prices rise due to increased demand and limited supply, consumers may experience higher costs for goods and services, resulting in inflation. B. Sluggish growth of aggregate demand: | | Defence Sluggish growth of aggregate demand refers to a situation where the overall demand for goods and services within an economy grows slowly or remains stagnant. In this scenario, prices have little upward pressure because demand is not outpacing supply. Instead, there may be a risk of deflation or disinflation, where prices either decrease or increase at a slower rate than expected. C. Reduction in the money supply: A reduction in the money supply occurs when the central bank or monetary authority implements policies to decrease the amount of money circulating in the economy. With less money available, consumers and businesses have reduced purchasing power. This can lead to a decrease in aggregate demand, potentially causing a decline in prices if demand falls below the level of supply. D. Higher levels of unemployment: Higher levels of unemployment refer to a situation where a larger proportion of the labour force is unemployed or underemployed. Unemployment can lead to decreased consumer spending and overall demand in the economy as unemployed individuals have less income to spend on goods and services. Hence the correct answer is A.

q: Which is the only country in the world to officially proclaim the Gross National Happiness (GNH) as the measure of the country's progress ?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: International Happiness Day is celebrated on March 20 every year. Explanation: The term 'Gross National Happines's (GNH) was coined by the 4th King of Bhutan King Jigme Singye Wangchuk in 1972. Bhutan first initiated the resolution of the International Day of Happiness by the UN. The concept of Gross National Happiness includes sustainable development, which considers equally important economic and non-economic well-being aspects. GNH is measured based on 9 parameters: Health, Psychological well- being, Education, Time use, Cultural Diversity and resilience, Community vitality, Good Governance,Living standards and Ecological Diversity and resilience. Hence, the correct option is B i.e. 'Bhutan.'

q: The computation of poverty in terms of Monthly Per Capita Consumption Expenditure (MPCE) based on the Mixed Reference Period was recommended by the
subject: economy
exp: The Tendulkar Committee, which submitted its report in December 2009, introduced significant changes in the methodology used to compute poverty lines and poverty ratios in India. One of the key changes was the adoption of the Monthly Per Capita Consumption Expenditure (MPCE) as the basis for defining the poverty line. 1. Monthly Per Capita Consumption Expenditure (MPCE): MPCE refers to the average monthly expenditure per person within a household. It is calculated by dividing the total household consumption expenditure by the total number of individuals in the household. MPCE serves as an important indicator of the economic well-being of individuals and households. 2. Mixed Reference Period: The Tendulkar Committee introduced the concept of a Mixed Reference Period for calculating MPCE. Instead of relying solely on a single reference period for collecting expenditure data (e.g., 30 days), the Mixed Reference Period approach allows for the collection of expenditure data over different time frames. This could include weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual reference periods, depending on the nature of expenditures and the availability of data. Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Which one of the following is not a fund managed by NIIFL (National Investment and Infrastructure Fund Limited)?
subject: economy
exp: NIIF is a collaborative investment platform set up between the Government of India, global investors, multilateral development banks (MDBs), and domestic financial institutions. Ministerial Statement: This information was confirmed by Union Minister of State for Finance, Dr. Bhagwat Kisanrao Karad, in a written reply in Lok Sabha. Funds Managed by NIIF: Master Fund Fund of Funds Strategic Opportunities Fund These funds have invested in 16 entities across various sectors, including: Ports and logistics Renewable energy Roads Digital infrastructure Healthcare Manufacturing Investment Facilitation: An MOU was signed in November 2022 to facilitate investments across multiple sectors in India through an India-Japan Fund. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: Which of the following statements with regard to the Report of Tendulkar Committee (2009) on poverty estimates is/are correct? 1. The Committee had used an all-India urban poverty line basket as a reference to derive both rural and urban poverty levels. 2. The Committee had anchored the poverty line to the official food calorie norms. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: 1. The Committee had used an all-India urban poverty line basket as a reference to derive both rural and urban poverty levels. If the committee indeed used an all- India urban poverty line basket to derive both rural and urban poverty levels, then this statement would be correct. 2. The Committee had anchored the poverty line to the official food calorie norms. However, if the committee did not solely anchor the poverty line to official food calorie norms but considered a broader range of factors,such as monthly spending on education, health, electricity, and transportation, to derive poverty lines for rural and urban areas. So it is incorrect. Hence the correct answer is option A.

q: Which one of the following statements for a firm's equilibrium in Perfect Competition is not correct?
o: The market price must be greater or equal to average variable cost in the short run.
The market price must be equal to marginal cost.
The market price must be equal to average cost in the long run.
The marginal cost decreases at the equilibrium output.
subject: economy
exp: In perfect competition, the following conditions hold true for a firm's equilibrium: A. The market price must be greater or equal to average variable cost in the short run: Correct. This ensures the firm covers its variable costs and can continue operating in the short run. B. The market price must be equal to marginal cost: Correct. In perfect competition, firms produce where price equals marginal cost (P = MC) to maximize profits or minimize losses. C. The market price must be equal to average cost in the long run: Correct. In the long run, firms in perfect competition operate where price equals both marginal cost and average cost (P = MC = AC), earning normal profits. D. The marginal cost decreases at the equilibrium output: Incorrect. At equilibrium output in perfect competition, marginal cost is typically rising, not decreasing. Firms produce where marginal cost equals the market price, and marginal cost slopes upward due to the law of diminishing returns. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: Which one of the following taxes is not included in the Central Pool to be shared with the States according to the recommendations of Finance Commission of India?
subject: economy
exp: The Finance Commission of India recommends the distribution of certain taxes between the central government and the states. However, not all taxes are part of the divisible pool. Here's an analysis of the options: A. Personal Income Tax: Included in the divisible pool and shared with states. B. Corporate Profit Tax: Also included in the divisible pool and shared with states. C. Surcharge and Cess: Not included in the divisible pool. Surcharge and cess are collected by the central government for specific purposes and are not shared with the states. For 2024-25, cess and surcharge collected by the Union government is estimated to be around 23% of its gross tax receipts, which does not form part of the divisible pool. D. Excise Duties: Part of the divisible pool and shared with states. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Level of per capita GDP depends upon which of the following? 1. Proportion of population in the working age 2. Work participation rate 3. Per worker productivity Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Proportion of population in the working age: A higher proportion of the population in the working age means more potential workers, which can lead to higher GDP. Work participation rate: The proportion of the working-age population that is actually employed or actively seeking work impacts the total output of the economy. Per worker productivity: The amount of output each worker produces directly affects the overall GDP. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: Under normal downward sloping demand curve and fully elastic supply curve of a commodity, an exogenous decrease in demand would lead to
o: Increase in equilibrium price and quantity
Decrease in equilibrium price and quantity
Decrease in equilibrium quantity and no change in price
Increase in equilibrium price and no change in quantity
subject: economy
exp: In this scenario, with a downward-sloping demand curve and a fully elastic supply curve, an exogenous decrease in demand would lead to a decrease in equilibrium price and quantity Decrease in Demand: The demand curve shifts to the left with decreased demand. Downward-sloping Demand Curve: A downward-sloping demand curve implies that as price decreases, quantity demanded increases, and vice versa. Fully Elastic Supply Curve: A fully elastic supply curve means that suppliers can provide any quantity of the good at the given price, without constraints. Equilibrium Adjustment: The decrease in demand would lead to a commodity surplus at the existing price. Suppliers would decrease the price to clear the market in response to this surplus, leading to a lower equilibrium price. Decrease in Equilibrium Quantity: Since both supply and demand are elastic, the price decrease would not fully offset the decrease in demand, leading to a decrease in equilibrium quantity. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

q: Consider the following statements regarding instruments of monetary policy : 1. Standing deposit facility (SDF) rate was introduced in April 2022. 2. SDF rate replaced fixed reverse repo rate as the floor of the LAF corridor. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: In April 2022, the RBI introduced the new Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) to absorb liquidity from commercial banks The SDF replaced the Fixed Rate Reverse Repo (FRRR) as the floor of the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) corridor. The Fixed Rate Reverse Repo (FRRR) was replaced by the SDF as the floor of the LAF corridor. This means that the SDF rate replaced the fixed reverse repo rate when determining the lower bound of the corridor within which short-term interest rates fluctuate. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: If India enters into Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) with other nations, then the growth of exports of India would depend upon which of the following? 1. Extent of tariff reduction vis-à-vis MFN tariffs 2. Extent of relaxation in terms of rules of origin 3. Extent of relaxation in sanitary and phytosanitary measures 4. Level of infrastructure in India 5. Income in nations with which India enters into FTAs Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The growth of exports of India, as influenced by Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) with other nations, can depend on various factors. Let's evaluate each option: Explanation: | | Defence 1. Extent of tariff reduction vis-à-vis MFN tariffs: This is a crucial factor. If FTAs lead to significant reductions in tariffs compared to the Most Favored Nation (MFN) tariffs, it can enhance India's export competitiveness by making Indian goods more attractive in the partner countries' markets. 2. Extent of relaxation in terms of rules of origin: Rules of origin determine the criteria used to determine the origin of goods traded under an FTA. Relaxation in these rules can facilitate the utilization of preferential tariffs and increase export opportunities for Indian products by allowing them to qualify under the FTA provisions. 3. Extent of relaxation in sanitary and phytosanitary measures: Sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) measures are regulations related to food safety and animal and plant health standards. Relaxation in these measures can facilitate smoother trade by reducing non- tariff barriers and ensuring compliance with partner countries' regulations, thereby potentially boosting exports. 4. Level of infrastructure in India: While infrastructure plays a significant role in facilitating trade, including exports, it may not directly determine the growth of exports resulting from FTAs. However, improved infrastructure can enhance the efficiency of export operations and reduce transaction costs, thereby indirectly supporting export growth. 5. Income in nations with which India enters into FTAs: The income levels in partner countries can influence their demand for goods and services, including those from India. Higher income levels may lead to increased purchasing power and demand for Indian exports, potentially boosting export growth under FTAs. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National Welfare Fund was established with the view to help which one of the following groups?
subject: economy
exp: The Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National Welfare Fund for Sportspersons (PDUNWFS) was established in March 1982 to support and assist former outstanding sportspersons who have fallen on hard times. This initiative recognizes the significant contributions these athletes have made to the country by achieving excellence in sports, often bringing national pride and glory. Key Objectives: 1. Financial Assistance: The fund aims to provide financial support to retired sportspersons who are facing economic hardships. This includes helping them with their basic needs and improving their quality of life. 2. Recognition of Achievements: The initiative acknowledges the dedication and achievements of sportspersons who have represented India at national and international levels, highlighting their roles in promoting sports and inspiring future generations. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Which of the following are included in M1 definition of money for the Indian economy? 1. Reserves 2. Currency 3. Time deposits 4. Demand deposits Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The M1 definition of money typically includes the most liquid forms of money, which are readily accessible for transactions. Explanation: 1. Reserves: These are not typically included in M1. Reserves represent funds that banks hold with the central bank to meet their reserve requirements and manage liquidity in the banking system. 2. Currency: It refers to physical currency notes and coins in circulation, which are included in M1. 3. Time deposits: Time deposits, such as fixed deposits, are not included in M1. They represent savings that are less liquid and usually have a fixed term. 4. Demand deposits: These are funds held in accounts that are accessible on demand by the depositor, such as current accounts and some types of savings accounts. They are typically included in M1. Hence, the correct answer is option C.

q: Which of the following indicators is/are used to observe the monetary transmission mechanism in the economy? 1. Weighted average lending rate 2. Weighted average domestic term deposit rate 3. 1-year median MCLR 4. SDF rate Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: To observe the monetary transmission mechanism in the economy, central banks and economists often use various indicators to gauge the effectiveness of monetary policy transmission. Explanation: 1. Weighted average lending rate: This is a key indicator used to assess how changes in monetary policy, such as adjustments to the central bank's policy rate, affect the interest rates banks charge on loans to consumers and businesses. It reflects the cost of borrowing for various economic agents and is thus relevant for understanding monetary transmission. 2. Weighted average domestic term deposit rate: Similar to the lending rate, changes in monetary policy can influence the interest rates offered by banks on deposits. Monitoring changes in deposit rates helps understand how monetary policy impacts savers and affects the supply of funds in the banking system, contributing to the assessment of monetary transmission. 3. 1-year median MCLR (Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate): MCLR is the benchmark lending rate used by banks in India to price their loans. It is determined based on the marginal cost of funds and is sensitive to changes in the central bank's policy rates. Monitoring changes in MCLR, especially over specific tenors like 1-year, provides insights into how monetary policy actions are transmitted through the banking sector. 4. SDF rate (Standing Deposit Facility rate): The Standing Deposit Facility is a facility provided by central banks where banks can park excess funds overnight. The rate associated with this facility can reflect the central bank's policy stance and liquidity conditions in the banking system. However, its relevance for observing monetary transmission may be more indirect compared to the other indicators listed. Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Which one among the following was the focus of the Twelfth Five-Year Plan?
subject: economy
exp: The focus of the Twelfth Five-Year Plan (2012–2017) was on Sustainable and Inclusive Growth. The plan aimed to achieve faster, more inclusive, and sustainable growth, addressing both economic development and environmental sustainability, along with social inclusion. Infrastructure Focus: Strengthening infrastructure projects and ensuring electricity supply in all villages. Educational Goals: Aimed at reducing the gender and social gap in school admissions and improving access to higher education. Environmental Target: Planned to increase the green cover by 1 million hectares annually. Non-farming Opportunities: Aspired to create new opportunities in the non- farming sector. | | Defence Growth Rate Target: Initially aimed for a 9% growth rate, but the National Development Council approved an 8% growth rate for the Twelfth Five-Year Plan in 2012. Hence the correct answer is option A.

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. GDP deflator captures the average price of an unchanging basket of commodities that constitutes the GDP of the country. 2. GDP deflator can be used to measure the real GDP of the economy but not the inflation rate. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: 1. GDP deflator captures the average price of an unchanging basket of commodities that constitutes the country's GDP. This statement is correct. The GDP deflator is a measure of the overall level of prices in the economy relative to the base year. It reflects the changes in the prices of all goods and services produced within an economy. 2. GDP deflator can be used to measure the real GDP of the economy but not the inflation rate. This statement is incorrect. The GDP deflator adjusts nominal GDP for inflation, thereby deriving real GDP. Real GDP is calculated by dividing nominal GDP by the GDP deflator. Additionally, changes in the GDP deflator over time can indicate inflation or deflation in the economy. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

q: Which one of the following statements is not correct for National Income Accounting for India?
o: Imports are subtracted in calculating Gross Domestic Product.
Net factor payments earned from abroad are included in Gross Domestic Product.
Purchase and sale of second-hand goods are not included in Gross Domestic Product.
Inventories are included in Gross Domestic Capital Formation.
subject: economy
exp: Net factor payments earned from abroad are not included in GDP but in the calculation of Gross National Product (GNP). GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced within the domestic territory. In contrast, GNP measures the total value of goods and services produced by the factors of production owned by a country's residents, regardless of where they are located. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

q: Consider the following statements : 1. A rupee Vostro account is an account that an Indian bank holds for a foreign bank in the domestic currency (rupee) to enable domestic banks to provide international banking services. 2. Insurance density, i.e., average insurance premium per capita does not fully capture the financing gap in the event of a premature death because most of the insurance products sold in India are savings-linked with a small component of protection. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: 1. Statement 1 is correct: A rupee Vostro account is indeed an account that an Indian bank holds for a foreign bank in rupees, facilitating international banking services. Vostro Account: An account that domestic banks hold for foreign banks in the domestic currency (rupee in India's case). Purpose: Domestic banks use Vostro accounts to offer international banking services to clients with global banking needs. Special Rupee Vostro Account (SRVA): An additional system that complements the existing one, which relies on freely convertible currencies like the US dollar and British pound. Existing System: Requires maintaining balances and positions in major convertible currencies (e.g., US dollar, pound) to facilitate international trade. SRVA Role: Provides an alternative mechanism for trade settlements, reducing reliance on foreign currency reserves. 1. Statement 2 is also correct: Insurance density does not fully capture the financing gap in the event of premature death because many insurance products in India are savings-linked, with a limited protection component. Thus, the correct answer is C: Both 1 and 2.

q: Consider the following statements regarding weightage of different articles in Wholesale Price Index (WPI) : 1. Fuel and power have higher weightage in WPI than that of primary articles. 2. Weightage of manufactured products in WPI is higher than that of fuel and power. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: 1. Fuel and power have higher weightage in WPI than that of primary articles. According to the WPI primary articles have a weightage of 22.62, while fuel and power have a weightage of 13.15. Therefore, primary articles indeed have a higher weightage than fuel and power in the WPI basket. 2. The weightage of manufactured products in WPI is higher than that of fuel and power. Manufactured products have the largest share in the WPI basket, with a weightage of 64.23. This confirms that the weightage of manufactured products is higher than that of fuel and power. Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Which one of the following statements regarding GST is not correct?
o: Amendment 115 to the Constitution of India kept alcohol for human use and five petroleum products outside the ambit of GST.
Amendment 122 to the Constitution of India kept only alcohol for human use outside the ambit of GST.
Precious metals are taxed at a rate of 1 % under GST.
Unworked diamond is taxed at a rate of 0.25%.
subject: economy
exp: A. Amendment 115 to the Constitution of India kept alcohol for human use and five petroleum products outside the ambit of GST. This statement is correct. Amendment 115 indeed excluded alcohol for human consumption and five petroleum products from the scope of GST. B. Amendment 122 to the Constitution of India kept only alcohol for human use outside the ambit of GST. This statement is incorrect. Amendment 122 primarily dealt with providing constitutional status to the Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council and its functions. It didn't specifically address the exclusion of alcohol for human use from GST. C. Precious metals are taxed at a rate of 1% under GST. This statement is incorrect. Precious metals are taxed at different rates under GST. For example, gold attracts a GST rate of 3%, not 1%. D. Unworked diamond is taxed at a rate of 0.25%. This statement is correct. Unworked diamonds are indeed taxed at a rate of 0.25% under GST. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Which one of the following dimensions is not included in Human Development Index ?
subject: economy
exp: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a composite index used to measure and compare the overall development of countries based on three key dimensions: 1. Health: Measured by Life Expectancy at Birth, which indicates the average number of years a newborn is expected to live if current mortality rates continue. 2. Education: This dimension includes two indicators: Mean Years of Schooling: The average number of years of education received by people aged 25 and older. Expected Years of Schooling: The total number of years of schooling that a child entering the education system can expect to receive, assuming current enrollment ratios remain the same throughout their life. 3. Standard of Living: Measured by Gross National Income (GNI) per capita, adjusted for purchasing power parity. Mortality rate: While related to health, mortality rates (such as infant mortality rates) are not directly included in the HDI. The HDI specifically uses life expectancy at birth as the key health indicator. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Most of India's reserves is held in the form of foreign currency. 2. There is no cost of holding foreign currency as reserves by a nation. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: 1. Most of India's reserves is held in the form of foreign currency. This statement is correct. A significant portion of India's foreign exchange reserves is indeed held in the form of foreign currencies. These reserves include assets like foreign currency assets (FCA), gold reserves, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), and the reserve position in the International Monetary Fund (IMF). Foreign currency assets constitute the largest component of these reserves. 2. There is no cost of holding foreign currency as reserves by a nation. This statement is incorrect. Holding foreign currency reserves does involve costs. These costs can include opportunity costs, such as the foregone investment returns that could have been earned if the reserves were invested in other assets with potentially higher returns. Additionally, managing and maintaining these reserves can incur administrative and operational costs. Hence the correct answer is option A.

q: Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. A price index captures the change in the average price of a constant basket of commodities. 2. If the price index takes values 100 , 110 and 121 in three consecutive years respectively, then the inflation rates in the 2 nd and 3rd years are 10% and 21 % respectively. Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: 1. A price index captures the change in the average price of a constant basket of commodities. A price index, such as the Consumer Price Index (CPI) or Wholesale Price Index (WPI), measures the average change over time in the prices paid by consumers (CPI) or producers (WPI) for a basket of goods and services. This basket typically contains a fixed set of goods and services to track price changes accurately. This statement is correct. 2. If the price index takes values 100, 110, and 121 in three consecutive years, respectively, then the inflation rates in the 2nd and 3rd years are 10% and 21%, respectively. If the price index takes values 100, 110, and 121 in three consecutive years respectively, then the inflation rates in the 2nd and 3rd years are calculated as follows: For the 2nd year: For the 3rd year: Similarly, if the price index increases from 110 to 121, the inflation rate would be [(121 - 110)/110] * 100 ≈ 10.91%, not 21%. Hence, the correct answer is option A.

q: Which one among the following items comprises the major portion of revenue expenditure of the Union Government of India?
subject: economy
exp: Interest payments on government debt typically constitute the largest portion of revenue expenditure for the Union Government of India. This is due to the country's substantial public debt and the associated interest obligations. Here's the expenditure breakdown presented in table format: Expenditure Category Perce ntag e (%) Interest Payments 19% Central Sector Scheme (excluding capital outlay on Defence and Subsidy) 16% Defence 8% Subsidies 6% Pensions 4% Centrally Sponsored Scheme 8% Finance Commission and Other Transfers 9% Other Expenditure 9% Hence the correct answer is option B.

q: Which of the following is not included in the Capital Account of the Balance of Payments of a country?
subject: economy
exp: The Capital Account of the Balance of Payments (BoP) records financial transactions that affect a country's assets and liabilities with the rest of the world. These include Foreign Direct Investment (FDI), Commercial Borrowing, and External Assistance. A. Foreign Direct Investment (FDI): Included in the Capital Account as it involves financial investments between countries. B. Commercial Borrowing: Included in the Capital Account since it involves loans and credit between countries. C. Invisibles: Not included in the Capital Account. Invisibles (such as services, remittances, and income from abroad) are recorded in the Current Account, not the Capital Account. D. External Assistance: Included in the Capital Account as it involves foreign aid or loans. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Which of the following components of Central Government taxes on petroleum products is/are not shareable with the States? 1. Basic Excise Duty 2. Additional Excise Duty 3. Special Additional Excise Duty Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: The component of Central Government taxes on petroleum products that is not shareable with the States is: Special Additional Excise Duty Basic Excise Duty: This is a shareable component of the central taxes with the states under the revenue-sharing arrangements. Additional Excise Duty: This can also be shared with the states under specific conditions. Special Additional Excise Duty: This is typically not shareable with the states as it is often levied for specific purposes and retained entirely by the central government. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: The practice of reducing the size of a product while maintaining its sticker price is called :
subject: economy
exp: Shrinkflation is the practice where companies reduce the quantity or size of a product while keeping its price the same. It is a form of hidden inflation because consumers pay the same amount but receive less. Companies often adopt shrinkflation during periods of rising input costs (like raw materials, packaging, or transportation) to avoid directly increasing prices. Hence, the correct answer is option A.

q: Which of the following statements about Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) is/are correct? 1. It was launched in 2021 by the Government of India. 2. Its aim is to democratize e-commerce in India. 3. India has surpassed China in 2024 in e-retail penetration. Select the answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: 1. Statement 1: It was launched in 2021 by the Government of India. Correct. The Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) was incorporated on December 31, 2021, as a Section 8 company by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Government of India. 2. Statement 2: Its aim is to democratize e-commerce in India. Correct. ONDC aims to democratize digital commerce by creating an open network that enables small and medium enterprises (SMEs) and local businesses to participate in e-commerce, thereby reducing the dominance of large platforms. 3. Statement 3: India has surpassed China in 2024 in e-retail penetration. Incorrect. While India's e-retail market has grown significantly, becoming the world's second-largest in terms of online shoppers in 2024, it has not surpassed China in e-retail penetration. China remains the leader in e-retail penetration. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

q: Consider the following statements about Union Government’s Expenditure on revenue account and effective capital expenditure : 1. Effective capital expenditure as percentage of GDP has increased from 2020-21 to 2023-24. 2. Expenditure on revenue account as percentage of GDP has increased from 2020-21 to 2023-24. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
subject: economy
exp: 1. Effective Capital Expenditure (Capex) Definition: Effective capital expenditure = Capital expenditure + Grants-in-aid for creation of capital assets. It is a more comprehensive measure of government’s contribution to long-term asset creation in the economy. Trend (2020-21 to 2023-24): 2020–21 → 3.2% of GDP 2023–24 (RE) → 4.2% of GDP Reason for increase: Government’s focus on infrastructure push under National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP). Priority to transport, railways, roads, and defence modernization. Multiplier effect of Capex in boosting private investment. 2. Revenue Expenditure Definition: Expenditure incurred for day- to-day running of the government, such as salaries, pensions, subsidies, interest payments, defence revenue expenses, etc. Trend (2020-21 to 2023-24): 2020–21 → 14.4% of GDP 2023–24 (RE) → 10.8% of GDP Reason for decline: Gradual withdrawal of COVID- related support measures. Fiscal consolidation efforts. Rationalization of subsidies (especially food and fertilizer).

q: The United Nations Carbon Offset Platform is an e-commerce platform to compensate greenhouse gas emissions by purchasing carbon credits. Who among the following can buy these carbon credits? 1. A company 2. An organization 3. А citizen Select the answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: The United Nations Carbon Offset Platform is an e-commerce portal run under the UNFCCC where users can purchase carbon units (CERs) to compensate greenhouse-gas emissions or to support climate projects. Who can buy: The UNFCCC explicitly states that a company, an organization or a regular citizen can purchase units on the platform — i.e. purchases are open to businesses, NGOs/organisations and individual members of the public. How it works (practical steps): Buyers browse listed UN-certified projects (examples: wind, hydro, biomass, methane-capture etc.), select the project/volume they want, and purchase the corresponding Certified Emission Reductions (CERs). The platform also | | Defence enables voluntary cancellation of CERs so the purchased units are retired and cannot be resold. Type and purpose of credits: The credits offered are CDM-issued CERs (Certified Emission Reductions). Purchases are voluntary and are used either to offset unavoidable emissions (by cancelling the credit) or to directly support project finance in the host countries. Evidence of public participation: The UN reported milestones showing uptake by the general public, individuals, businesses and organisations (for example, the platform had reached a multi-million CER cancellation milestone), which underlines that all three categories of buyers participate.

q: Which one among the following pairs of type of firm and feature is not correctly matched?
o: Monopolistic : Firm is a price setter firm
Monopoly firm : Produces an efficient level of output
Perfectly : Produces socially competitive firm optimum output
subject: economy
exp: Oligopoly firm → Characterized by interdependence in decision-making, because a few firms dominate the market and each firm’s actions affect others. Correct. Monopolistic competition → Firm is a price setter to some extent due to product differentiation. Correct. Monopoly firm → Does not produce an efficient level of output. A monopoly restricts output to raise prices, leading to allocative inefficiency (price > marginal cost). Incorrect match. Perfectly competitive firm → Produces at the socially optimal output where Price = Marginal Cost, achieving allocative efficiency. Correct.

q: The Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) launched in 2015-16 by the Central Government is aimed to :
o: make agriculture more modern and globally competitive.
provide improved access to irrigation and enhanced water efficiency.
support promotion of organic farming in the country.
reduce dependence of agriculture on monsoon.
subject: economy
exp: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) was launched in 2015-16 under the National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA). The main objective of the scheme is to promote organic farming. PKVY is a key component under the Soil Health Management (SHM) scheme. It is part of the larger National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA). Promotes organic farming through: Cluster approach by adopting organic villages. Participatory Guarantee System (PGS) certification. Expected Outcomes: Promotion of commercial organic production through certified farming. Ensures pesticide residue-free produce, improving consumer health. Helps in increasing farmers’ income and creating market opportunities for traders. Motivates farmers to use natural resources for producing organic inputs. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Which one among the following sectors is included in the list of the Sunrise Sectors in India?
subject: economy
exp: In the context of the Indian economy, a Sunrise Sector is an industry that is in its infancy but shows promise of a rapid boom. These sectors are typically characterized by high growth rates, high levels of innovation, and significant potential for job creation and investment. Why Food Processing is a Sunrise Sector The Government of India has officially classified the Food Processing Sector as a "Sunrise Sector" due to several factors: Vast Raw Material Base: India is a global leader in the production of milk, fruits, vegetables, and cereals. Value Addition: The sector acts as a bridge between agriculture and manufacturing, reducing post-harvest losses and increasing farmers' income. | | Defence Investment: It allows 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) under the automatic route for most segments. Government Support: Initiatives like the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Sampada Yojana (PMKSY) and the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme specifically target this sector to make India a "Global Food Hub." Manufacturing, Services, and Textile Sectors: These are considered Traditional or Established sectors. While they are vital to the economy, they have already reached a level of maturity and do not fit the definition of an "emerging" or "sunrise" industry in the current economic landscape.

q: Which of the following statements with respect to the Gender Inequality Index (GII) is/are correct? 1. GII reflects gender-based disadvantage in work participation, property and income dimensions. 2. GII reflects gender-based disadvantage in reproductive health, empowerment and the labour market dimensions. 3. The value of GII ranges from 0 to 1, with a GII of 0 indicating women and men faring equally in all dimensions. Select the answer using the codes given below:
subject: economy
exp: The Gender Inequality Index (GII) is a composite metric introduced by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) in 2010. It measures the opportunity cost of gender inequality. Statement 1 is Incorrect: The GII does not specifically measure "property and income." Statement 2 is Correct: The GII focuses on three specific dimensions: 1. Reproductive Health: Measured by Maternal Mortality Ratio (MMR) and Adolescent Birth Rate (ABR). 2. Empowerment: Measured by the share of parliamentary seats held by women and attainment in secondary and higher education. 3. Labour Market: Measured by women's participation in the work force. Statement 3 is Correct: The GII ranges from 0 to 1. 0 indicates perfect equality (men and women fare equally). 1 indicates total inequality (one gender fares as poorly as possible in all dimensions).

q: The financial instruments that generate proceeds for investment in environmentally sustainable and climate-suitable projects are called as:
subject: economy
exp: Green Bonds are fixed-income instruments specifically earmarked to raise money for climate and environmental projects. They are a key part of "Green Finance," aimed at mitigating the effects of climate change. How Green Bonds Work When an entity (like a government or a corporation) issues a green bond, they are legally bound to use the proceeds for projects that have a positive environmental impact. Comparison of the Options Green Bonds (Option A): Used for renewable energy, energy efficiency, clean transportation, and sustainable water management. In India, the government issues Sovereign Green Bonds to fund its green infrastructure. Carbon Bonds (Option B): These are generally related to carbon credits or the right to emit a certain amount of carbon dioxide, rather than being a standard investment vehicle for infrastructure projects. Climate Credit Bonds (Option C): This is not a standard or widely recognized financial term in the same way Green Bonds are; it is often a misnomer for climate-linked securities. Social Impact Bonds (Option D): These focus on social outcomes (like reducing recidivism, improving healthcare, or education) rather than environmental projects. They follow a "pay-for-success" model.

q: Which one among the following statements correctly defines the Infant Mortality Rate (IMR)?
o: IMR is the average number of infants born to a woman over her lifetime.
IMR is the average number of infants born in a year per 1000 deaths before attaining one year of age.
IMR is the number of deaths of infants under one year old per 1000 live births in a year.
IMR is the number of deaths of infants under one year of age per year.
subject: economy
exp: The Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) is a vital demographic indicator used to assess the quality of healthcare, nutrition, and overall living standards in a country or region. Breaking Down the Definition To be technically accurate, IMR must include three specific components: 1. Age Limit: It only counts deaths of children under one year of age. 2. Denominator: It is calculated per 1,000 live births (not per 1,000 deaths or the total population). 3. Timeframe: It is usually measured over a one-year period.

q: The Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) is measured by:
o: Measuring each person's overlapping deprivations across the indicators in the health, education and standard of living dimensions
Measuring each person's separate efficiencies across the indicators in the wealth, income and standard of living dimensions
Measuring each person's poverty on the indicators of health, happiness and ownership of vehicles
Measuring inequalities and deprivations in respect of wealth, income and standard of living dimensions
subject: economy
exp: The Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) was launched in 2010 by the Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative (OPHI) and the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP). It goes beyond income to understand how people experience poverty in their daily lives. How MPI is Measured Unlike traditional poverty measures that look only at income (how much money you have), the MPI looks at deprivations (what you are missing). It identifies overlapping disadvantages across three dimensions and ten indicators: Dimensi on Indicators (Weighted equally within the dimension) Health Nutrition, Child Mortality Educatio n Years of Schooling, School Attendance Standard of Living Cooking Fuel, Sanitation, Drinking Water, Electricity, Housing, Assets

q: Which one of the following items comprises the major portion of Revenue Expenditure in Budget 2025-26 of the Union Government of India?
subject: economy
exp: The correct option is B: Interest Payments. In the Union Budget 2025-26, Interest Payments continue to be the single largest component of the Union Government's Revenue Expenditure. Key Breakdown of Expenditure (Budget 2025-26) According to the budget estimates, interest payments account for approximately 25% of the total expenditure and a significant 37% of the total revenue receipts. Item Estimated Intere st Paym ents ~25% of total expenditure; the largest single item under Revenue Expenditure. Defen ce Servic es While it receives a massive allocation, a large portion is split between Revenue (salaries/operations) and Capital (modernization). Pensi ons A significant "committed expenditure," but smaller in scale compared to the interest on historical debt. Road Trans port Primarily falls under Capital Expenditure as it focuses on asset creation (building highways). Why Interest Payments? Interest payments are classified as Revenue Expenditure because they do not result in the creation of new assets or the reduction of liabilities (only the principal repayment reduces the liability). Due to the accumulation of fiscal deficits over the decades, the cost of servicing this national debt remains the heaviest burden on the government's annual revenue. Contextual Summary | | Defence Committed Expenditure: This includes interest payments, salaries, and pensions. In the 2025-26 budget, these three items together consume more than 60% of the government's total revenue receipts, leaving limited room for discretionary spending on development. Revenue vs. Capital: The government has been trying to curb Revenue Expenditure (like subsidies) to increase Capital Expenditure (Capex) for infrastructure, which reached a record ₹11.21 lakh crore in this budget cycle.

q: In which of the following years was the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) absorbed into the World Trade Organization (WTO)?
subject: economy
exp: Key point: The World Trade Organisation created in 1995 with 164 members is an international institution that oversees the rules among nations for global trade. Explanation: The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade was signed in October 1947 and became law in 1948 aimed at making international trade easier. The GATT was absorbed into the world trade organization ( WTO )in 1995. Hence the correct option is (b).

q: The lower growth of coal production in India during 2011-2012 was primarily due to 1. environmental restrictions 2. non-availability of forestry clearance 3. poor law and order situation in coal- producing States 4. excessive rainfall in coal-mining areas Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key concept The lower growth rate of coal production in India. In India during 2011-2012 coal production was low Explanation The low growth was primarily due to the excessive rainfall in coal mining areas and environmental restrictions. 1. Environmental Restrictions: Environmental regulations and laws aim to protect the environment by enforcing stricter rules on mining practices and controlling emissions. These restrictions can limit coal production, making it harder to expand operations and meet demand. 2. Non-Availability of Forestry Clearance: Forestry clearance is required to access forest land for activities such as mining. The lack of necessary approvals from forest authorities can delay or block coal production by restricting access to essential land for mining operations. 3. Poor Law and Order Situation in Coal- Producing States: Instability and disruptions caused by poor law and order conditions in coal- | | Defence producing states can severely impact coal production. Operational challenges, workforce safety, and transportation delays are common issues that arise, all of which hinder consistent production. 4. Excessive Rainfall in Coal-Mining Areas: Heavy rainfall can result in landslides, flooding, and other weather-related disruptions in coal-mining regions. These adverse conditions can damage infrastructure and equipment, hampering mining operations and leading to lower coal production. Hence, the correct answer is option A. Additional Information Recently, in the last eight years the coal production has increased by 37.3%

q: The Government of India and the Reserve Bank of India have decided to introduce 1 billion pieces of Rs. 10 notes in polymer/ plastic on a field trial basis. Which of the following is/are the objectives behind this move? 1. Increase the lifetime of the notes 2. Combating counterfeiting 3. Reducing the cost of minting of currency Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: The Indian government and RBI trials for polymer/plastic Rs 10 notes: The government of India has informed Parliament that RBI will conduct field trials of 1 billion pieces of new polymer/ plastic based Rs. 10 notes Explanation: The Reserve Bank of India will conduct city trials of a new polymer/plastic based 10 Rs. banknote The field trials will be conducted on five cities Mysore, Kochi, Shimla, Jaipur and Bhubaneswar These notes are stronger and smaller than cotton based notes thus Increases the lifetime of the notes, makes harder to counterfeit and also reducing the cost of minting of currency Therefore, all the three statements are correct Hence, the correct answer is option D.

q: Indian monetary policy includes the "Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF)". Which of the following tools is utilized under LAF? 1. Repo Rate 2. Reverse Repo Rate 3. Bank Rate 4. Cash Reserve Ratio Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key concept: Monetary Policy Explanation: One of the most important elements of India's monetary policy is the "Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF)". The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) uses it as a tool to control liquidity in the banking system. LAF's principal goal is to maintain the market's short-term interest rates within the policy corridor, which is indicated by the repo and reverse repo rates. Repo rate: Repo is short term for "Repurchase Agreement." The RBI loans money to banks in exchange for government assets when it conducts a repo auction. Banks receive money from the central bank and guarantee their securities to the RBI in exchange. The interest rate at which banks borrow money from the RBI by guaranteeing certain securities is known as the repo rate. Reverse Repo Rate: The opposite of a repo rate is reverse repo rate. In a reverse repo auction, the RBI borrows money from banks by pledging government assets as security. Banks deposit their extra cash with the RBI and receive interest on it. The interest rate at which banks deposit their excess cash with the RBI is known as the reverse repo rate. Hence, the correct answer is option D.

q: How does partìcipatory budgeting seek to make the functioning of local governance institutions more transparent and accountable? 1. By allowing citizens to deliberate and negotiate over the distribution of public resources 2. By allowing citizens to play a direct role in deciding how and where resources should be spent 3. By allowing historically excluded citizens with access to important decision-making venues Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Benefits of partìcipatory budgeting Explanation: Participatory budgeting (PB) is a citizen or public participation process in which community members decide how a portion of the public budget should be allocated. Participatory budgeting is important because it gives citizens real control over what they spend their budgets on. Participatory budgeting enables citizens to identify, discuss and prioritize public spending projects and empowers them to make real decisions about how their money is spent. It enables citizens and civil society, and governmental and legislative bodies, to advise and negotiate the distribution of public funds. Hence the correct answer is option (a) i.e., 1 and 2 only

q: Share of food in total consumption expenditure has been coming down as per capita income grew over time in last sixty years because
o: people have been purchasing less food
people have been preferring non-cereal items in their food basket
growth in food expenditure has been lower than growth in per capita income
percentage of the poor in population has increased over time
subject: economy
exp: Key concept In the last sixty years share of food in total consumption expenditure has been declined as the per capita income grew Explanation Since the growth in the food expenditure has been lower than the growth in per capita income therefore,the share of food in total consumption expenditure declined Hence, the correct answer is option(C) i.e, growth in food expenditure has been lower than growth in per capita income

q: Consider the following statements: Statement I: Deficit financing does not lead to inflation if adopted in small doses. Statement II: Deficit financing is an often-used tool for financing budgetary deficits. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
o: Both the statements are individually true and statement II is the correct explanation of statement I
Both the statements are individually true but statement II is not the correct explanation of statement I
Statement I is true but statement II is false
Statement I is false but statement II is true
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Deficit financing is a practice in which the government spends more than it receives as revenues, the difference being made up by borrowing or minting new funds. Explanation- Deficit financing is the borrowing that a government uses to cover up its budget deficit. Deficit financing is covered up by either printing up a new currency, issuing bonds, or burrowing. So, deficit financing is used as a tool for financing deficit budgets. And also if it is taken in small doses it does not lead to inflation. So, both statements are correct but statement II is not the correct explanation of statement I. Hence, the correct option is ‘b’ i.e.Both statements are individually true but statement II is not the correct explanation of statement I.

q: The government can influence private sector expenditure by 1. taxation 2. subsidies 3. macroeconomic policies 4. grants Select the correct answer using the code given below.
subject: economy
exp: Key concept Private sector refers to that part of the economy which is not under the direct control of state but however government can influence the private sector Explanation The government can influence private sector expenditure through various mechanisms: 1. Taxation: By increasing or decreasing taxes, the government can influence how much private entities spend or save. 2. Subsidies: Subsidies reduce the cost of production for businesses, thereby encouraging more spending or investment. 3. Macroeconomic Policies: These include monetary and fiscal policies that can alter interest rates, inflation, and overall economic conditions, affecting private sector spending decisions. 4. Grants: Direct financial assistance from the government can stimulate private sector spending by reducing financial constraints. Hence, the correct answer is option A.

q: Which of the following is a measuring indicator of Human Development Index?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: In 1990, Prof. Mehbub Al Haque of Pakistan and Prof. Amartya Sen of India introduced the concept of Human Development Index (HDI). Since 1993, it has been used by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) each year to calculate the Human Development Index (HDI). Explanation: The Human Development Index is calculated on the basis of three indicators- 1. Life Expectancy Index (LEI): Calculated from Life expectancy at birth. 2. Education Index (EI): Calculated from Mean years of schooling and Expected years of schooling 3. Income Index (II): Calculated from GNI per capita Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: A recent survey (by Bloomberg) shows that the USA has fallen behind emerging markets in Brazil, China, and India as the preferred place to invest. Why is this so? . The unstable economic situation of the USA which the global investors feel not likely to improve in the near future. . Global investors are finding Brazil, China and India to be actually more amenable to foreign investment. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- According to a report of Bloomsburg due to the unstable economic situation of the USA, global investors are facing market instability there. This gives rise to emerging markets of the world. Explanation- The USA has fallen behind emerging markets in Brazil, China, and India as the preferred place to invest, as a Bloomberg survey shows , though the world’s largest economy still ranks highest of all major countries. Brazil, China, and India are part of five emerging markets known as BRICS- the other being Russia and South Africa. Also, these markets are more amenable to foreign investment. So, both statements are correct. Hence, the correct option is ‘c’ .

q: Which one of the following is not a component of the scheme Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research (INSPIRE)' launched by the Union of India?
o: Scheme for early attraction of talents for science
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Innovation in science pursuit of inspired research is a cutting-edge programme that the Department of Science and Technology sponsors and oversees. Explanation: Vocation training after higher education is not the component of the scheme innovation in science pursuit for inspired research. Hence,the Correct option is D i.e. Vocational training after higher education. Additional Information: Career and technical education are known as vocational training and education. Vocational education is a type of institution of higher learning created primarily to offer vocational education.

q: Which one of the following is not correct in the context of balance of payments of India during 2013-2014?
o: India's exports were less than its imports
subject: economy
exp: Balance of Payments (BOP): A Balance of Payment concept that includes the sum of the Capital and Current accounts. Covers all international transactions between a domestic country and the rest of the world. Encompasses items such as the goods account, services account, unilateral transfers, and capital accounts. India's BOP Position in 2013-14: Current Account Deficit (CAD) decreased sharply from a record high of US 32.4 billion (1.7% of GDP) in 2013-14. The trade deficit in March 2014 was $10.5 billion, which was slightly higher than the previous year. The net exports figure or trade balance is negative when exports are less than imports. There was a rise in net Invisibles receipts during 2013-14. The capital account balance declined but remained positive in 2013-14; therefore, the statement regarding its negativity is incorrect. Hence the correct answer is option D.

q: Which one of the following continents accounts for the maximum share in exports from India?
subject: economy
exp: Key concepts: India ranks among the top exporters of grains and is the world's second-largest rice producer. Explanation: The largest portion of Indian exports for the fiscal year 2021—more than 26 percent —was engineering items. Nearly half (47.8%) of India's export value went to other Asian countries, while 19.3% went to European buyers, according to the continent share. India is anticipated to achieve $46 billion in exports to the ASEAN region (2021). Hence, the Correct option is ‘A i.e. Asia

q: The Second Five Year Plan that called for the establishment of Socialist pattern of society was commonly referred to as the
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept- Five year plan of India . Explanation- 1. The second five year plan called for the pattern of socialist pattern of society was commonly referred to as Mahalanobis plan. 2. The Mahalanobis plan was proposed by the renowned professor Prasantha Chandra Mahalanobis,who had set up the national statistical institute. 3. The basic objective of the plan was to adopt the idea of rapid industrialisation and rapid development in the PSU’s. 4. The plan was totally focussed on economic growth and was often criticized for neglecting the education sector. Hence, the answer is option B.

q: During the Eleventh Five Year Plan (FYP), the agriculture sector in India witnessed a growth rate of per cent per annum which is higher than 2.4 per cent per annum in the previous FYP. This is largely due to better performance of:
subject: economy
exp: Key concepts: The eleventh five-year plan is India's blueprint for economic growth from 2007 to 2012. The agriculture sector includes businesses whose main activities are the production of crops, the care and feeding of animals, and the harvesting of fish and other animals from farms, ranches, or their natural habitats. Explanation: Agriculture and rural development have been prioritized in the Eleventh Five Year Plan (2007–2012). Agrarian distress issues have been addressed through a variety of policy initiatives. In India, the agriculture sector grew at a pace of 3.3 percent annually during the Eleventh Five Year Plan, compared to 2.4 percent annually during the previous plan. This is largely because crops and animals are performing better. The growth rate was improved by special initiatives including Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana (RKVY) and the National Food Security Mission. Hence, the Correct option is A‘ i.e. Crops and Livestock

q: Which of the following statements about the Livestock sector in India is/are correct? 1. Livestock contributed about 25 per cent of gross value added in agriculture 2. It provides self employment to a large segment of population 3. Rapid growth of livestock sector can be egalitarian and inclusive Select the correct answer using the code given below:
subject: economy
exp: Key concepts: The formula for calculating gross value added (GVA) is production (at basic prices) minus intermediary consumption. In the Indian economy, the livestock sector is a significant subsector of agriculture. Explanation: In India's agriculture sector, the gross value added from livestock in fiscal year 2020 was over seven trillion Indian rupees. Livestock now accounts for 29.35percent (2019-20) of all agriculture and allied sector GVA (at constant prices), up from 24.32 percent in 2014– 15. Dairy, fisheries, and animal husbandry are all included in the livestock sector. India has a large cattle resource base, hence 8.8% of the population works in the livestock industry. About 4% of the GDP and 25% of the gross value added in agriculture were contributed by it. India owns the most livestock worldwide. Rapid expansion of the cattle industry can be inclusive and egalitarian. It gives a significant portion of the population in India self- employment opportunities. Hence, the Correct option is D‘ i.e. 1,2 and 3

q: As per the RBI guidelines, which one of the following is the minimum tenure of Masala Bonds that an Indian company can issue offshore?
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- 1. As per the RBI guidelines, the minimum tenure of Masala Bonds that an Indian company can issue offshore is 3 years. 2. Masala Bonds are offshore rupee-denominated bonds issued by Indian companies in overseas markets to raise funds. The name "Masala Bond" reflects the Indian culture and cuisine, and is used to distinguish these bonds from other types of bonds. 3. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) issued guidelines for Masala Bonds in 2015. According to these guidelines, Indian companies can issue Masala Bonds overseas for a minimum tenure of 3 years and a maximum tenure of 10 years. The guidelines also specify that the funds raised through Masala Bonds can be used for any permissible end use, except for real estate activities, investment in capital markets, and equity investment in India. 4. Masala Bonds are popular among Indian companies as they provide an alternative source of funding, which is not subject to exchange rate fluctuations as the bonds are denominated in rupees. They also allow Indian companies to diversify their investor base and access a wider pool of global investors. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Which of the following is/are not FDI policy change(s) after 2010 ? 1. Permission of 100 percent FDI in automotive sector 2. Permitting foreign airlines to make FDI up to 49 percent 3. Permission of up to 51 percent FDI under the government approval route in multi-brand retailing, subject to specified conditions 4. Amendment of policy on FDI in single-brand product retail trading for aligning with global practices Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Changes in policy of FDI (Foreign Direct Investment) after 2010 Explanation: Foreign direct investment is a form of investment in which a company based in another country owns a majority of production assets, such as businesses, real estate or factories, in one country. FDI consists of construction of new infrastructure, reinvestment of profits from overseas operations and intra-company investment . There are two ways by which India obtains FDI i.e.the automatic route and the government route Only, 49% of FDI in the automotive sector is admitted to power exchanges registered with the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission. Up to 49% of FDI is permitted to the foreign airlines. Up to 51% of FDI is allowed under government approval process in multi-brand retail, subject to specific conditions FDI Policy for Single Brand Retailers Revised to Align with Global Practices Hence the correct answer is option (A) i.e., 1 only

q: Which one of the following is not a target of the 12th Five-Year Plan?
o: Real GDP Growth Rate of 8 percent
Increase in green cover by 1 million hectares every year during the Plan period
subject: economy
exp: Key points- One of the targets of the Five-Year Plan is to increase agriculture growth rate of per cent. Explanation- The government of India's twelfth five-year plan was the country's final five-year plan.The Planning Commision was abolished and NITI Ayog was established instead of that. The target of the Five-Year Plan are:- Real GDP Growth Rate by per cent. agriculture growth rate of per cent. During the Plan period, green cover will increase by 1 million hectares per year. Manufacturing Growth Rate of per cent. In terms of economic growth, each state must outperform the 11th plan. Hence, the correct option is ‘b’ i.e. Agriculture Growth Rate of 5 percent.

q: Which one of the following was set as a target of average growth of GDP of India over the plan period 2012-2017 by the Approach Paper to the Twelfth Five Year Plan?
subject: economy
exp: Explanation- The government of India's twelfth five-year plan was the country's final five-year plan. The Planning Commission was abolished and in 2014 NITI Ayog was established instead of that. The target of the 12th Five-Year Plan are:- Real GDP Growth Rate of 8% Agriculture growth rate of 4% During the Plan period, the green cover will increase by 1 million hectares per year. Manufacturing Growth Rate of 10% In terms of economic growth, each state must outperform the 11th five year plan Hence, the correct option is ‘b’, i.e. 8%

q: In its emphasis on enhancing human capabilities, which one among the following does NOT figure in the Twelfth Five-Year Plan ?
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Life and longevity do not figure in the Twelfth Five-Year Plan. The twelfth five-year plan of the government of India was India’s last five-year plan. Explanation- The Twelfth Five-Year Plan emphasizes enhancing human capabilities are education delivery of public service skill development poverty health environment and long-term viability infrastructure. Other than this it aims to reduce poverty , to pace up GDP, etc. Hence, the correct option is ‘a’ i.e. long and longevity.

q: Surge pricing takes place when a service provider
o: raises the price of its product or service as demand outstrips supply
follows preset prices immune to demand and supply dynamics
fixes a minimum price for its services
fixes an average price on the basis of transactions carried over a day
subject: economy
exp: Key Points- Surge pricing takes place when a service provider raises the price of its product or service as demand outstrips supply. Explanation- When a company raises the price of its product or service if there is an increase in demand but supply is not enough to fulfill it then its prices surge; this is known as surge prices. Surge price is also known as dynamic pricing or demand pricing or time-based pricing. Hence, the correct option is ‘a’ i.e. raises the price of its product or service as demand outstrips supply.

q: The Nehru-Mahalanobis Strategy of Development was implemented for the first time by which one of the following Five-Year Plans?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Nehru-Mahalanobis Strategy of Development The Nehru-Mahalanobis model was developed on the foundation of a long-term development strategy that gave higher precedence to long-term development goals rather than giving in to immediate and short-term ambitions. Explanation: 1. The Nehru-Mahalanobis Strategy of Development was implemented for the first time by the 2nd Five-Year Plan. 2. Its primary emphasis was on the nation's industrial development. 3. The plan's time frame was from 1956 to 1961. 4. First Five-Year Plan: Harrod- Domar model with an emphasis on the nation's agricultural development. 5. The third five-year plan, known as the Gadgil plan, was centred on establishing economic independence so that it could take off on its own. 6. Fourth Five-Year Plan: Stable development and increasing self-sufficiency. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

q: 'Seva Bhoj Yojana', a scheme of the Government of India that seeks to reimburse the Central share of CGST and IGST on Food/Prasad/Langar/Bhandara offered by religious institutions, is introduced recently by which one of the following Ministries?
o: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Seva Bhoj Yojana A central sector programme of the Indian government's ministry of culture is called "Seva Bhoj Yojna". It plans to reimburse the Central Government's portion of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) and the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) in order to lessen the financial burden on charitable religious institutions that offer food, prasad, community kitchen space, and brahmana to the general public and devotees without charge. Explanation: 1. 'Seva Bhoj Yojana', a scheme of the Government of India was introduced by the Ministry of Culture. 2. It is a programme to offer financial aid, free "prasad," free food, or free "langar" or "bhandara" (community kitchen), which are provided by charitable/religious organisations such Gurudwara, Temples, Dharmik Ashram, Mosques, Dargah, Church, Math, Monasteries, etc. 3. Because it is a Central Sector Scheme, the Central Government provides all financial support. 4. The programme would reimburse religious organisations for the CGST and IGST they paid while buying particular foods. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

q: The principal component(s) of Nehru- Mahalanobis strategy of economic development was/were 1. Restructuring economic dependency on metropolitan capitalism into independent economic development. 2. Transition from semi-feudal agriculture to capitalist farming. Select the correct answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Pt. Jawahar Lal Nehru's close advisor, Mahalanobis, founded the Indian Statistical Institute. Explanation: 1956–1961's Mahalanobis model. 1956 replaced the 1948 Industrial Policy Resolution. To construct an industrial basis, the policy stressed fast heavy industry development. One goal was to swiftly make India independent on producer goods growth so that capital accumulation would not be limited by challenges in getting essential producer goods from other countries. Statement 1 is correct. Emphasis on transitioning from semi-feudal agricultural to capitalist farming. Statement 2 is correct. Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: The call for Garibi Hatao was incorporated in
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Five-Year Plans Explanation: The slogan "Garibi Hatao" was famously given by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi during the 1971 general elections. It became the central theme of the Fifth Five-Year Plan, which focused on: Poverty alleviation Self-reliance in agriculture and defence Improving employment and income levels Hence, the correct answer is Option (b).

q: The idea of Planning in Independent India was drawn from
o: the demand made by workers' unions
the Gandhian vision of India's future
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: Planning Explanation: A section of the big industrialists got together in 1944 and drafted a joint proposal for setting up a planned economy in the country. This proposal was called the Bombay Plan. The Bombay Plan wanted the state to take major initiatives in industrial and other economic investments. So,the idea of planning in independent India was drawn from the Bombay Plan. Hence, the correct answer is Option (a).

q: Which Five Year Plan of India focused on rapid industrialization based growth process?
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The second five-year plan focused on heavy industries and Rapid industrialization. Explanation: The Harrod-Domar Growth Model was used for overall projections. The strategy of resource allocation was based on the two-sector (agriculture & industry) and four-sector model developed by Prof. P. C. Mahalanobis. The Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61) is also known as the Mahalanobis Plan. It was conceived in an atmosphere of economic stability. Agriculture was given lower priority as the focus was on rapid industrialization. Emphasis was placed on heavy and basic industries. The Plan advocated huge imports financed through foreign loans. The Industrial Policy of 1956 aimed at establishing a socialist pattern of society. Acute foreign exchange shortage led to the pruning of development targets. A 30% price rise was observed, in contrast to the decline in prices during the First Plan. The Second Five-Year Plan was only moderately successful. Hence, the correct answer is option B.

q: Plan allocation in agriculture and irrigation as percentage of total plan outlay was highest in :
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The concept of Five-Year Plans in India was inspired by the Soviet model of economic planning and was first introduced in 1951 as part of the country's strategy for economic development. Explanation: Plan allocation in agriculture and irrigation as a percentage of total plan outlay was highest in the First Five- Year Plan ( 1951-56). The year 1951-56 was the first year of the Plan allocation in agriculture and irrigation. The plan allocation of agriculture including irrigation and power. It was recorded and accorded the highest priority and therefore, the First Plan assigned the maximum priority to the agricultural sector. Moreover, the plan cleared that from the fact that 31 p.c. of the total plan outlay was strongly devoted to agriculture. However, the plan was known and was based on the Harrod-Domar model. It gave priority to the agricultural development of the country and the Plan was presented before the parliament by Jawaharlal Nehru. Lastly, it was the most successful plan of agricultural production as the growth rate of agriculture has been recorded more in this decade Hence the correct answer is option C i.e., First Five-Year Plan

q: Who among the following is known as the architect of Indian planning?
subject: economy
exp: P.C. Mahalanobis: He is considered the father of modern statistics in India. He founded the Indian Statistical Institute. He is also known as the Architect of Indian Planning. Mahalanobis's model of the second five-year plan focused on Rapid industrialization and strengthening of the public sector. Hence, the correct answer is option C.

q: Which one of the following statements regarding the objectives of the Second Five- Year Plan is not correct?
o: Development of the basic and heavy industry sector
Increase in national income to raise living standards
Expansion of the consumer goods sector
subject: economy
exp: Key Concept: The Second Five-Year Plan centered on fostering the public sector and accelerating industrialization. Explanation: The Second Five-Year Plan, which took place in India from 1956 to 1961, focused more on the development of basic and heavy industries to support industrial growth and infrastructure rather than expanding the consumer goods sector. A substantial allocation of nearly 50 billion rupees was designated across various sectors to meet set objectives. Emphasis was placed on scaling up production and refining production methods. The statement that is not correct regarding the objectives of the Second Five-Year Plan is "Expansion of the consumer goods sector." Hence the correct answer is option C.

q: Which among the following is/are the objective(s) of setting up of Planning Commission in the 1950s in India? 1. To increase production and offering opportunities to all for employment 2. To make assessment of all resources of the country Select the answer using the code given below :
subject: economy
